films.title: French Girls
films.synopsis: An inspired act of micro-budget filmmaking, this playful gig economy parable set in the Sydney fashion scene introduces an impressive array of new Australian talent.
films.description: Mia is floating through life: dating a small-time scammer; killing time in front of screens; working a menial job in construction. When the unexpected opportunity arises for her to exchange hi-vis for high fashion, she reluctantly falls into the world of modelling, where there’s ample money, prestige, gossip … and just as much boredom. Lost between old friends who judge her new job and new friends she can’t quite trust, Mia keeps floating along. Via a series of questionable choices and increasing misadventures, however, she will ultimately become infamous. Made for just $28,000 and drawn from her own experiences as a fashion photographer, Korean-Australian writer/director Hyun Lee’s ( Asian Girls , MIFF 2018) debut feature film is a DIY portrait of the drudgery of labour that punches well above its weight. Shot in Sydney locations by cinematographer Dimitri Zaunders ( The Golden Spurtle , MIFF 2025) with a host of non-professional actors – including impressive newcomer Mia Kidis in the central role – it’s at once a comedy about the absurdities of the fashion industry, a commentary on the construction of imagery and a film in open dialogue with the Nouvelle Vague.
films.awards: Shot in Sydney locations by cinematographer Dimitri Zaunders ( The Golden Spurtle , MIFF 2025) with a host of non-professional actors – including impressive newcomer Mia Kidis in the central role – it’s...
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.36/5；纪录片、剧情、喜剧 / 澳洲电影 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：有电影节展映/奖项线索

films.title: Silenced
films.synopsis: Jennifer Robinson, Amber Heard and Brittany Higgins have all experienced the full force of weaponised defamation laws – but these indomitable activists and survivors of gendered violence won’t keep quiet
films.description: Jennifer Robinson, Amber Heard and Brittany Higgins have all experienced the full force of weaponised defamation laws – but these indomitable activists and survivors of gendered violence won’t keep quiet. In 2017, #MeToo replaced the social stigma of discussing gendered violence with public solidarity and support for victim-survivors. The re-silencing was swift, vicious … and legalistic. Alleged perpetrators now used defamation lawsuits as cudgels to punish survivors for speaking out, and to deter journalists from exposing prominent people to public scrutiny. As a barrister who famously stood up for Amber Heard in a long-running, all-too-public defamation case in the UK, Jennifer Robinson has dedicated her career to fighting against these attacks, witnessing firsthand how such actions drag survivors through emotionally, professionally and financially ruinous legal proceedings.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 4.16/5；纪录片 / 纪录片单元 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: The Diary of a Chambermaid
films.synopsis: Romania’s premier provocateur returns, as razor-sharp as ever, with a finely wrought parable skewering the ‘good intentions’ of the bourgeoisie.
films.description: Gianina is a live-in nanny for a wealthy family in Bordeaux. One of the many Romanians who go to France seeking better economic conditions, she keeps the extravagant home shipshape, cooks the family their favourite Thai meals and cares for their occasionally bratty young son. In the evenings, Gianina divides her time between rehearsals for a play – specifically, a bawdy, experimental adaptation of the titular 1900 Octave Mirbeau novel previously brought to screen by Jean Renoir and Luis Buñuel – and video-calling her daughter, who jealously pines for her back in their home village. Feted at Cannes, Radu Jude’s latest class satire operates in a realist, somewhat melancholic register closer to that of Kontinental ’25 (MIFF 2025) than to his anarchic Dracula . Anchored by excellent performances from Ana Dumitraşcu as Gianina and Mélanie Thierry and Vincent Macaigne ( Suspended Time , MIFF 2024) as her employers – as well as Ilinca Manolache, the irrepressible star of Jude’s 2023 opus Do Not Expect Too Much From the End of the World – this left-of-field literary riff is both deeply compassionate and merciless in dramatising the sometimes absurd, often humiliating, plight of migrant workers.
films.awards: Feted at Cannes, Radu Jude’s latest class satire operates in a realist, somewhat melancholic register closer to that of Kontinental ’25 (MIFF 2025) than to his anarchic Dracula .
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.66/5；剧情 / 欧洲与英国 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、悬疑 / 犯罪张力。奖项/展映线索：戛纳

films.title: The Loneliest Man in Town
films.synopsis: An ageing Austrian blues musician faces up to the fact that the times they are a-changin’ in this poignant tale rooted in real life.
films.description: Resembling a pompadoured, older Robert Mitchum, Al Cook (born Alois Koch in Vienna, 1945) has lived in the same apartment all his life. In the basement, he maintains a humble recording studio – also the site of a shrine to his musical idols, Elvis chief among them. But Al’s building is under threat by developers; he is, in fact, the last remaining resident. Contemplating the upheaval to come, Al is drawn to the idea of finally visiting Memphis, the faraway, foreign city that gave birth to the music to which he’s dedicated his life. As in their previous films Vera , Mister Universo and La Pivellina (MIFF 2010), directors Tizza Covi and Rainer Frimmel centre a 16mm character study on a non-professional actor whom they’ve invited to enact a version of themselves for their camera – in this case, a minor celebrity in his home country who is possessed of a mellowed, graceful swagger. Pricked with the dry humour of Aki Kaurismäki, this Berlinale Competition selection is a laconic, bittersweet portrait of a man who is beautifully – and, in some ways, tragically – out of sync with his time.
films.awards: Pricked with the dry humour of Aki Kaurismäki, this Berlinale Competition selection is a laconic, bittersweet portrait of a man who is beautifully – and, in some ways, tragically – out of sync with his time.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.65/5；剧情、喜剧 / 欧洲与英国 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：音乐与表演、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：柏林电影节

films.title: Flesh and Fuel
films.synopsis: Awarded the inaugural Queer Palm Revelation award at Cannes, this swooning feature debut trails the deepening romantic spark that forms between two long-haul truckers.
films.description: Due to the nature of their work, Étienne and Bartosz are ships – or semitrailers – in the night. And while the Frenchman and the Pole both drive trucks carrying all manner of goods across Europe, they couldn’t be more different: whereas reticent Étienne has been hardened by his experiences, gregarious Bartosz retains his zest for life despite its hardships. What was meant to be a one-off hook-up by the side of the road transforms into a profound, continent-spanning romance. There’s no dearth of works tackling the difficult pursuit of queer love, but Pierre Le Gall’s indelible answer to God’s Own Country (MIFF 2017) and Brief Encounter enriches its tale of unexpected connection by implicating clashes of culture and economy and exploring questions of self-acceptance, ambition and blue-collar alienation. Bolstered by Antoine Cormier’s sensitive lensing and Paul Sabin’s affecting score, actors Alexis Manenti and Julian Świeżewski enthral as the film’s central couple, magnetically portraying their chemistry and their distinct responses to this tricky relationship. Flesh and Fuel shows how love can bloom in the most unlikely of places, so long as we’re willing to take a different route.
films.awards: Awarded the inaugural Queer Palm Revelation award at Cannes, this swooning feature debut trails the deepening romantic spark that forms between two long-haul truckers.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.63/5；剧情、爱情 / 欧洲与英国 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：亲密关系、政治与社会现实。奖项/展映线索：获奖、戛纳

films.title: Ticket of No Return
films.synopsis: An enigmatic woman with no name books herself a one-way trip in order to drink her way through West Berlin in Ulrike Ottinger’s most revered film.
films.description: Dressed to the neuns, Tabea Blumenschein (known only here as “she”) arrives at Tegel Airport with one thing on her mind: alcohol. Setting off on a curious odyssey through Berlin’s bourgeois and bohemian drinking establishments, she invites a woman of very different means (Christine Lutze) to join her for a cognac or 10, and together they rub shoulders – at an aloof distance – with the city’s outré inhabitants (played by an eclectic cast including Nina Hagen, Eddie Constantine and Martin Kippenberger). Intent on her own oblivion, she pays no attention to the Greek chorus following her all the while: three sociologists in houndstooth suits whose conversation revolves around moral judgements and facts about gendered alcoholism. Director Joanna Hogg ( The Eternal Daughter , MIFF 2023; The Souvenir , MIFF 2019) has said that this film had a “decisive influence” on her desire to become a filmmaker, having seen it at age 19 and likening it to her love of the work of both Derek Jarman and Powell and Pressburger. Frequent Ottinger collaborator Blumenschein (star of Madame X: An Absolute Ruler and co-director of The Enchantment of the Blue Sailors ) is dazzling in this “portrait of a drinker” (as the original German title translates to), bringing a doomed charisma to a mostly wordless performance. She also designed the glamorously clamorous costumes, and appears briefly in drag.
films.awards: An enigmatic woman with no name books herself a one-way trip in order to drink her way through West Berlin in Ulrike Ottinger’s most revered film. | Setting off on a curious odyssey through Berlin’s bourgeois and bohemian drinking establishments, she invites a woman of very different means (Christine Lutze) to join her for a...
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.73/5；剧情、实验 / Ulrike Ottinger: Radical Travels。推荐理由：家庭关系、亲密关系、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：柏林电影节

films.title: Parallel Tales
films.synopsis: In the latest Cannes-bowing psychological drama from Asghar Farhadi, Isabelle Huppert leads an all-star ensemble cast, playing a creatively blocked author whose voyeurism causes art and life to bleed into one another.
films.description: Sylvie, a reclusive and irascible novelist living in squalor in Paris, struggles to get the creative juices flowing. To address this, she decides to train her telescope on the apartment across the street, where she spies two men and a woman fumbling around with sound recording equipment. Fascinated, she conjures up a melodramatic backstory for the threesome and starts typing. But when a stranger enters her orbit, the lines between fantasy, fabrication and fixation begin to blur. Asghar Farhadi’s follow-up to the Cannes Grand Prix–awarded A Hero (MIFF 2021) and Oscar winners The Salesman (MIFF 2016) and A Separation (MIFF 2011) is a suspenseful meta-thriller loosely based on the episode of Krzysztof Kieślowski’s Decalogue (MIFF 1990) that became A Short Film About Love . Co-written by Farhadi and his brother Saeed, the film evokes Hitchcock and Coppola but deploys its own psychologically complex provocation on truth, intention and interpersonal obsession. Elevated by Guillaume Deffontaines’s sumptuous cinematography and Zbigniew Preisner’s haunting score, the performances from Vincent Cassel ( The Shrouds , MIFF 2024), Virginie Efira ( A Private Life , MIFF 2025), Pierre Niney ( The Count of Monte Cristo ) and Adam Bessa ( Ghost Trail , MIFF 2024) – along with screen legend Catherine Deneuve in cameo – are superb, while lead Isabelle Huppert shines as an ill-tempered artist desperate to cling on to fading relevance.
films.awards: In the latest Cannes-bowing psychological drama from Asghar Farhadi, Isabelle Huppert leads an all-star ensemble cast, playing a creatively blocked author whose voyeurism causes art and life to bleed into one another. | Asghar Farhadi’s follow-up to the Cannes Grand Prix–awarded A Hero (MIFF 2021) and Oscar winners The Salesman (MIFF 2016) and A Separation (MIFF 2011) is a suspenseful me...
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 2.82/5；剧情 / 主打佳片 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、亲密关系、政治与社会现实、悬疑 / 犯罪张力、恐怖 / 超自然。奖项/展映线索：大奖、获奖、戛纳

films.title: Vertigo
films.synopsis: Kim Novak’s iconic dual performance in Alfred Hitchcock’s psychological thriller masterpiece, presented in glorious 70mm, has long been regarded as her best.
films.description: Former San Francisco detective John “Scottie” Ferguson (James Stewart) is hired by an old college acquaintance to trail his wife, Madeleine Elster (Kim Novak), claiming she might be possessed and suicidal. It’s not long before Scottie is deeply infatuated with his enigmatic mark, then shattered at her untimely death. In the grip of obsession, he meets a woman on the street, Judy Barton, whom he proceeds to make over in order to resurrect Madeleine as he remembers her – leading once again to tragedy. Initially, Alfred Hitchcock wanted Vera Miles for the dual role of Madeleine and Judy in Vertigo . When she became unavailable, he instead hired Novak, at that time a darling of the American moviegoing public. Though she was only 24 at the time of the film’s production, her performance is that of a much wiser and more experienced actor: while the genius of Hitchcock is undeniably central to the film’s construction and Novak’s presentation within the frame, it’s the latter’s own complexity that brings nuance to the two distinct women she plays. The palpable chemistry between her and Stewart (again on display in Bell, Book and Candle , released the same year) makes the tragedy that unfolds in Vertigo all the more keenly felt.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 4.22/5；剧情、惊悚 / Kim Novak: Hollywood Heretic。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实、失去与哀悼、悬疑 / 犯罪张力。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Gabin
films.synopsis: Gracefully condensing a decade’s worth of footage, this tender nonfiction gem out of Cannes chronicles the adolescence of a boy in northern France.
films.description: Young Gabin Jourdel lives in Artois, an agricultural region in economic decline. His mother, Patricia, is a cattle farmer; his father, Dominique, a butcher. Dominique intends for Gabin to one day take over his business, but even at the age of eight, the boy has reservations; demonstrably less at ease with his peers than with the Brown Swiss cows tended to by his mother, he would prefer to work with “living animals”. Over 10 years, as he grows up in front of director Maxence Voiseux’s camera, Gabin negotiates not only the burdens of familial expectation but also the limitations of his rural environment. Voiseux achieves a seamless, effortlessly intimate vérité style that suggests immense trust on the part of the Jourdel family, who first appeared in the director’s 2016 mid-length film The Heirs (this earlier documentary focusing on Dominique, his two brothers and their own cattle dealer father). Recalling both Richard Linklater’s Boyhood (MIFF 2014) and the beloved Up series (MIFF 1986), Gabin , with its quietly grand scope, is a poignant, truly impressive feature-length debut.
films.awards: Gracefully condensing a decade’s worth of footage, this tender nonfiction gem out of Cannes chronicles the adolescence of a boy in northern France.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.60/5；纪录片 / 纪录片单元 / 国际首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：戛纳

films.title: American Doctor
films.synopsis: An unapologetic cry for humanity that reveals the on-the-ground reality of the genocide in Gaza and the experiences of the frontline medical volunteers who put their principles into practice
films.description: An unapologetic cry for humanity that reveals the on-the-ground reality of the genocide in Gaza and the experiences of the frontline medical volunteers who put their principles into practice. Across late 2024 and early 2025, three doctors from the United States – Jewish orthopaedic surgeon Mark Perlmutter, Zoroastrian trauma surgeon Feroze Sidhwa and Palestinian emergency doctor Thaer Ahmad – worked in hospitals in the Gaza Strip trying to save some of the many lives being destroyed by the US-backed Israeli Defence Forces. Back home in North America, they attempt to reconcile these experiences with their day-to-day lives, as they also strive to raise awareness in the face of a world that would much prefer to look away.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 4.08/5；纪录片 / 纪录片单元 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：政治与社会现实。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Look Both Ways
films.synopsis: Winner of four AFI Awards, Sarah Watt’s prismatic and improbably funny tale of looming tragedy is independent Australian cinema at its off-kilter best.
films.description: An Adelaide artist who earns her crust as a painter of sympathy card seascapes, Meryl – played by Justine Clarke, stepping out from Play School ’s square window – is prone to morbidly nightmarish fantasies, lovingly rendered in hand-drawn animations. On her way home from her father’s funeral, Meryl has a genuine run-in with the reaper when she witnesses the death of a pedestrian in a train accident. This tragedy becomes the unlikely basis for a fraught whirlwind romance with a photojournalist (William McInnes) dealing with demons of his own, as well as the fulcrum of the film’s multiple interlocking portraits of grief. This auspicious live-action debut by Sydney-born, Melbourne-schooled animator and filmmaker Sarah Watt premiered in 2005, when it screened at MIFF alongside a retrospective of her animated shorts. In addition to sweeping that year’s AFI Awards, Watt also picked up the Toronto International Film Festival’s Discovery Award. Two decades on, Look Both Ways stands as a prime and, against all odds, uplifting example of the breezily black humour that has characterised a number of the nation’s most iconic films.
films.awards: Winner of four AFI Awards, Sarah Watt’s prismatic and improbably funny tale of looming tragedy is independent Australian cinema at its off-kilter best. | This auspicious live-action debut by Sydney-born, Melbourne-schooled animator and filmmaker Sarah Watt premiered in 2005, when it screened at MIFF alongside a retrospective of her animated shorts.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.38/5；剧情、喜剧 / Director in Focus: Sarah Watt。推荐理由：家庭关系、成长与青春、亲密关系、政治与社会现实、失去与哀悼、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：获奖、首映

films.title: The Currents
films.synopsis: Why is a successful, stylish young mother floundering? This sensuous portrait of dissociation refuses easy answers.
films.description: Argentinian designer Catalina has just won a prestigious award in Geneva, but something within her has clearly come unmoored. She drifts aimlessly through city streets in her turquoise coat, then casually throws herself off a bridge into the river below. Though she survives the attempt, back home in Buenos Aires she can’t shake a new, debilitating fear of water, which she’s too ashamed to share with her husband Pedro and small daughter Sofia. Her hair becomes increasingly bedraggled – one of the few visible signs of an inner turbulence that buffets Catalina between mental agony, dissociation and an almost sublime sensitivity. Bowing in TIFF’s Platform strand, The Currents went on to win San Sebastián’s RTVE – Another Look Award for the work at the festival best capturing “subjects related to the female world”. Immersing us in Catalina’s experience, director Milagros Mumenthaler’s deeply observational storytelling invites comparisons to her compatriot Lucrecia Martel ( Zama , MIFF 2018), submerging Catalina’s traumas in metaphor through a flow of light and textures.
films.awards: Argentinian designer Catalina has just won a prestigious award in Geneva, but something within her has clearly come unmoored.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.28/5；剧情 / 拉丁美洲 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实、音乐与表演、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：获奖

films.title: La libertad
films.synopsis: Made on a shoestring when the director was just 25, Lisandro Alonso’s groundbreaking first feature gatecrashed Cannes with a hypnotic depiction of rural labour
films.description: Made on a shoestring when the director was just 25, Lisandro Alonso’s groundbreaking first feature gatecrashed Cannes with a hypnotic depiction of rural labour. In the remote La Pampa province of Argentina, Misael ekes out an ascetic existence. Living alone in an isolated corner of a rural property, he rarely sees other people, and barely says a word when he does. Working as a woodcutter, he chops trees, shaves them down and loads them for transport. In quieter moments, he smokes, takes a shit or lies in the sun. And at the end of the day, he kills, prepares and barbecues his dinner: a classic woodcutter’s meal of roasted armadillo.
films.awards: Made on a shoestring when the director was just 25, Lisandro Alonso’s groundbreaking first feature gatecrashed Cannes with a hypnotic depiction of rural labour | Made on a shoestring when the director was just 25, Lisandro Alonso’s groundbreaking first feature gatecrashed Cannes with a hypnotic depiction of rural labour.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.67/5；剧情 / Lisandro Alonso。推荐理由：导演表达与人物处境。奖项/展映线索：戛纳

films.title: Joybubbles
films.synopsis: Inspiring Apple’s Steve Wozniak and Steve Jobs, the telephone hacker who was born blind made headlines as both a tech prodigy and a big-hearted iconoclast.
films.description: Before internet hackers popularised the concept of scrambling technology, the analog pioneers known as ‘phone phreaks’ discovered that they could manipulate landlines simply by whistling at certain frequencies. Born Josef Engressia in 1949 before adopting his cheerful mononym in 1991, Joybubbles was one such phreaker. From the tender age of four he was obsessed with the telephone, and he first discovered he could perform the magical 2600-hertz whistle aged just seven. As a whiz-kid, he was drawn to experiment with technology, but it also represented a kind of lifeline: born blind, Joybubbles used the phone to forge connections with people – and to help others connect, too. Though he died in 2007, Joybubbles can narrate his own story thanks to filmmaker Rachael J. Morrison, who uncovered a treasure trove of his audio recordings. In chronicling its singular subject’s life, underpinned by trauma but still overflowng with kindness, this loving and suitably uplifting Sundance-premiering documentary – produced by Sarah Winshall, who also produced By Design (MIFF 2025) and served as a consulting producer on Maddie’s Secret (MIFF 2026) – takes viewers back to a pre-internet era via its wealth of archival material, spotlighting a sidelined but fascinating chapter of hacking history.
films.awards: ...erpinned by trauma but still overflowng with kindness, this loving and suitably uplifting Sundance-premiering documentary – produced by Sarah Winshall, who also produced By Design (MIFF 2025) and served as a consulting producer...
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.57/5；纪录片 / 纪录片单元 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、记忆与历史、音乐与表演、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：圣丹斯、首映

films.title: Tristan Forever
films.synopsis: A burnt-out Parisian doctor travels to an ultra-remote, penguin-populated South Atlantic isle in search of isolation, salvation and perhaps even love.
films.description: Tristan da Cunha is the most remote place of human habitation in the world. A volcanic speck in the South Atlantic, midway between Argentina and South Africa, it’s visited by only six to eight boats per year, its 230-odd inhabitants vastly outnumbered by penguins. Loran Bonnardot, a jaded Parisian physician with a lifetime serving in Médecins Sans Frontières, has spent 30 years visiting Tristan; now, he’s hoping to permanently relocate there to escape the hectic nature of 21st-century living. As Bonnardot explores the island’s wilderness and tries to integrate into its community, he awaits approval for permanent residency. Swiss filmmaker Tobias Nölle has found acclaim for his experimental, eerie narrative features, from 2016 Berlinale stand-out Aloys to 2024 sci-fi parable Preparations for a Miracle . Here, directing in collaboration with Bonnardot, Nölle mounts a dreamlike, meditative film that blurs lines between ethnography, documentary and fantasy. With cinematography that evocatively brings Tristan da Cunha’s landscape to life, this is a quiet, contemplative study in both the pleasures and the perils of isolation – the perfect film for anyone with a love of exploration or a desire to escape.
films.awards: Swiss filmmaker Tobias Nölle has found acclaim for his experimental, eerie narrative features, from 2016 Berlinale stand-out Aloys to 2024 sci-fi parable Preparations for a Miracle .
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.56/5；纪录片、剧情 / 纪录片单元 / 维州首映。推荐理由：亲密关系、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：柏林电影节

films.title: Crocodile
films.synopsis: Spanning 13 years, this lively documentary chronicles the incredible rise of a group of young Nigerian filmmakers working outside the system.
films.description: When The Critics, as they call themselves, first formed, its members were not even teenagers. Though the Nigerian cinema emerging from the Nollywood hub of Lagos is famously prolific, the collective’s members live hundreds of kilometres away in the northern city of Kaduna (also known as Croc City). The films that truly inspire these young adults – predominantly sci-fi blockbusters – were made not in Nollywood but in Hollywood. Hand-sewn green screen, makeshift tripods, borrowed iPhones: schooling themselves via Wikipedia and YouTube tutorials, The Critics use whatever’s on hand to furnish their playful but grand moviemaking visions. In the process, they attract thousands of fans online, including Star Trek director J.J. Abrams. New Zealand director Pietra Brettkelly ( Yellow Is Forbidden , MIFF 2018) worked with behind-the-scenes footage – shot over more than a decade by The Critics themselves – to co-author an account of their story to date. Tracing the group’s burgeoning political consciousness alongside their creative development and launching its international festival run at this year’s Berlinale, Crocodile reveals the tensions that arise between members as well as the harsh realities that their love of cinema has enabled them to transcend.
films.awards: ...de their creative development and launching its international festival run at this year’s Berlinale, Crocodile reveals the tensions that arise between members as well as the harsh realities that their love of cinema has enabled t...
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.55/5；纪录片 / 电影之梦 / 维州首映。推荐理由：成长与青春、亲密关系、政治与社会现实、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：柏林电影节

films.title: I Want Your Sex
films.synopsis: In a rollicking return for queer provocateur Gregg Araki, an adorable Gen Z art assistant finds wild erotic joy in submitting to his insatiable boss.
films.description: Elliot starts working for debauched artist Erika Tracy, who foxed the fine-art world into intellectualising her giant papier-mâché phalluses and labial canvases. She immediately grooms her wide-eyed young assistant as her submissive muse, awakening his nascent appetites. Dazzled by the pleasures of pegging, handcuffs, ball gags and pig masks, Elliot shares all the steamy tea with his horny gay co-worker and his repressed housemate. But nine-and-a-half weeks of candy-coloured erotic mayhem later, Elliot’s in pink lingerie and in trouble with the law … Queer punk provocateur Gregg Araki ( Mysterious Skin , MIFF 2005) titillated Sundance 2026 with his first big-screen outing since 2014: a delirious dom-com that’s a raunchy blast for everyone, teasing Gen Z with the fun they’re missing while also satirising the Teen Apocalypse Trilogy auteur’s own permissive cultural sphere. Delightful as the down-for-anything Elliot, Cooper Hoffman ( Licorice Pizza ) sparks with Olivia Wilde ( Don’t Worry Darling ; Drinking Buddies , MIFF 2013), whose Erika is a hilariously unhinged mix of Norma Desmond, Miranda Priestly and Maude Lebowski. Joining this game cast are Daveed Diggs, Margaret Cho, Johnny Knoxville, Chase Sui Wonders and Charli xcx ( Erupcja , MIFF 2026), playing against type as Elliot’s uptight med student girlfriend.

films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.55/5；喜剧 / 主打佳片 / 维州首映。推荐理由：成长与青春、音乐与表演。奖项/展映线索：圣丹斯

films.title: Tangles
films.synopsis: Sarah Leavitt’s acclaimed graphic novel about Alzheimer’s is brought to the screen with an exceptional voice cast including Abbi Jacobson, Julia Louis-Dreyfus and Bryan Cranston.
films.description: Sarah is in her twenties and embracing life in San Francisco, where she has a job she loves at a queer magazine and a new girlfriend she adores even more. It’s 1999, and the future looks bright. But on a trip back home to the conservative Maine town she only recently escaped, she notices something’s not quite right with her mum, Midge. When the diagnosis comes – early-onset Alzheimer’s disease – Sarah finds herself caught between two worlds, juggling the life she was just beginning with the one she thought she’d left behind. Working closely with Sarah Leavitt, the author of the graphic novel Tangles: A Story about Alzheimer’s, My Mother and Me, debut director Leah Nelson has created an exceptionally moving tribute – not only to the source material, but also to everyone touched by the ruthless tangles of Alzheimer’s. The gorgeous, mostly black-and-white, hand-drawn animation pays homage to the book while not replicating it, and weaves seamlessly from authenticity to surreal fantasy as the story dictates. Featuring a voice cast that includes Abbi Jacobson, Julia-Louis Dreyfus, Bryan Cranston, Seth Rogen, Samira Wiley, Beanie Feldstein, Pamela Adlon, Sarah Silverman, Bowen Yang and Wanda Sykes, Tangles is a playful, sweet and achingly honest film about family, love and the brutal truths of a cruel disease. Bring tissues.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 4.05/5；动画 / 动画单元 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、亲密关系。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Liverpool
films.synopsis: A sailor travels through a harsh but beautiful landscape in search of his old hometown and what remains of his family.
films.description: Farrel (Juan Fernández) is a merchant seaman, toiling away below deck on long-haul shipping routes. When his ship is forced to dock at Ushuaia, southern Argentina’s gateway to Antarctica, he requests permission to go ashore to find out if his ailing mother is still alive. Downing drinks as he goes, the permanently soused sailor eventually manages to hitch a ride on a logging truck headed for his old hometown, to the place and the people he left behind. There, he discovers that his mother is still alive, but barely – and that she’s living with Farrel’s own daughter, Analía, whom he abandoned prior to her birth. Lisandro Alonso’s fourth feature – which, like its predecessors, bowed at Cannes – marshals a larger budget to follow another of his lonely men across a vivid landscape. Shot amid the icy surrounds of Tierra del Fuego, it’s a sensorial work aswirl with wind and snow, aching with isolation both environmental and emotional. Though armed with more traditional narrative elements than Alonso’s first three movies, Liverpool is another work in which so much story – and backstory – is conveyed silently, perceptible only to engaged viewers.
films.awards: Lisandro Alonso’s fourth feature – which, like its predecessors, bowed at Cannes – marshals a larger budget to follow another of his lonely men across a vivid landscape.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.61/5；剧情 / Lisandro Alonso。推荐理由：家庭关系。奖项/展映线索：戛纳

films.title: Roya
films.synopsis: The Iranian state has imprisoned her body; now it wants her innermost self. Under torture, can a rebel teacher defend her values, emotions and memories?
films.description: Iranian teacher Roya (Melisa Sözen, Winter Sleep ) is a political prisoner, detained and humiliatingly tortured after her students burned their hijabs. The regime offers her two options: make a televised confession falsely denouncing her beliefs; or face indefinite solitary confinement in her tiny prison cell. But for Roya, choice and coercion blur as she drifts between memories, interrogators’ lies and isolation-driven hallucinations. Is her family really at risk if she doesn’t confess? Is her silence resilience, or will it only confirm her guilt? Will confessing end the terror, or bring it inside forever? Director Mahnaz Mohammadi has been repeatedly arrested for defending Iranian women’s rights; like her protagonist, she spent months in Tehran’s notorious Evin prison. Premiering in Berlinale’s Panorama section, her empathetic drama refuses to reinterrogate Roya through the camera’s objectifying gaze. Instead, visceral point-of-view sequences and dreamlike voyages into Roya’s defiant past and bruised present honour the dissident mind’s struggle against authoritarian power.
films.awards: Premiering in Berlinale’s Panorama section, her empathetic drama refuses to reinterrogate Roya through the camera’s objectifying gaze.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.51/5；剧情、惊悚 / 非洲与中东 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实、记忆与历史。奖项/展映线索：柏林电影节、首映

films.title: Goodbye, Cruel World
films.synopsis: In Cannes Critics’ Week’s tender, strikingly shot closing-night film, two lonely teenagers find an unlikely connection
films.description: In Cannes Critics’ Week’s tender, strikingly shot closing-night film, two lonely teenagers find an unlikely connection. In a village tucked in the mountains of Normandy, 14-year-old Otto (Milo Machado-Graner, the astonishing young star of Anatomy of a Fall , MIFF 2023) just wants to disappear. Relentlessly persecuted by his classmates, he writes a farewell letter, sends it out to all and sundry, and heads to a bridge to end it all – but botches it. As everyone thinks he’s dead anyway, he decides to hide out on the outskirts of town. There, he’s discovered by fellow student Léna, who offers him shelter and an affection that fills his void.
films.awards: In Cannes Critics’ Week’s tender, strikingly shot closing-night film, two lonely teenagers find an unlikely connection | In Cannes Critics’ Week’s tender, strikingly shot closing-night film, two lonely teenagers find an unlikely connection.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.50/5；剧情 / 新锐视野 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：成长与青春。奖项/展映线索：戛纳、影评人周

films.title: Human Resource
films.synopsis: A Bangkok HR manager keeps her pregnancy secret from both her employers and her husband in this sly study of corporate culture.
films.description: HR manager Fren is so busy navigating the complexities of her corporate role that she’s not sure if she should tell her own partner – let alone her employers – that she’s pregnant. As she screens job applicants, manages competing workplace personalities and tries to be a supportive spouse to a frustrating husband, Fren begins to wonder whether this unfeeling metropolis populated with people trapped in soul-crushing, screen-centric jobs is a world that she even wants to bring a child into. Premiering in Venice’s Orizzonti section, Human Resource received a special award for its exploration of work and environmental issues. Though set in the specific environs of Bangkok, there’s a globally relevant quality to its depiction of overly demanding workplace drudgery. Capturing the callousness with which large corporations treat their workers and the harsh realities awaiting anyone setting out on a new career, Nawapol Thamrongrattanarit’s film is a steely, thoughtful meditation on workplaces – and the place of work – in the age of late-stage capitalism.
films.awards: Premiering in Venice’s Orizzonti section, Human Resource received a special award for its exploration of work and environmental issues.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.50/5；剧情 / 亚太单元 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：政治与社会现实、音乐与表演。奖项/展映线索：威尼斯地平线单元、获奖、威尼斯、首映

films.title: Freak Orlando
films.synopsis: In what she describes as a “small theatre of the world”, Ulrike Ottinger explores the span of human history in this deeply surreal, outré romp.
films.description: Across five sweeping, nonlinear adventures, the multi-named, gender-fluid and barely ageing Orlando (played by avant-garde New German Cinema star Magdalena Montezuma) explores the history of civilisation. From a modern department store in Freak City to medieval times, and from the Spanish Inquisition to a carnival sideshow, Orlando ultimately lands in Northern Italy for a competition crowning “The Ugliest Person of the Year”. A punk shapeshifter, our hero jostles with a wild cast of acrobats and performers with a diverse array of bodies and identities – including self-flagellating leather boys, the “Freak Orlando bunnies”, and conjoined twins memorably played by legendary French New Wave figure Jackie Raynal and frequent Ulrike Ottinger collaborator Delphine Seyrig (who appears throughout in multiple roles). A loose adaptation of Virginia Woolf’s epic 1928 novel Orlando: A Biography by way of Tod Browning’s infamous 1932 horror Freaks , this is the second in Ottinger’s ‘Berlin Trilogy’, wedged between Ticket of No Return and Dorian Gray in the Mirror of the Yellow Press . Described by Ottinger as a “ histoire du monde ”, or “small theatre of the world”, Freak Orlando is perhaps her most unclassifiable film. A cinematic fever dream, it challenges idealised norms of beauty, gender and sexuality through pageantry – an assemblage of irreverent tableaux vivants in lush colour and extravagant costuming.
films.awards: ...by way of Tod Browning’s infamous 1932 horror Freaks , this is the second in Ottinger’s ‘Berlin Trilogy’, wedged between Ticket of No Return and Dorian Gray in the Mirror of the Yellow Press ....
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.57/5；剧情、实验、奇幻 / Ulrike Ottinger: Radical Travels。推荐理由：家庭关系、记忆与历史、恐怖 / 超自然。奖项/展映线索：柏林电影节

films.title: Light Pillar
films.genre: Animation, Drama, Experimental, Comedy, Adventure, Fantasy, Sci-Fi
films.synopsis: This amusingly deadpan portrait of loneliness, the death of cinema and technology’s seductive appeal combines animation and live action in visionary ways.
films.description: In a near future in which affordable space travel has sent the film industry into freefall, Zha is a humble caretaker at an unused studio lot in snowbound northern China, tending to the decaying ruins of past productions. When the studio can’t afford to pay his salary, he’s gifted a VR headset instead. Zha soon grows obsessed with the virtual realm, and the borders between reality and illusion begin to blur. Moving between hand-drawn animation and digital live action in its VR sequences, Light Pillar offers a remarkably refreshing blend of romance, sci-fi and drama. Blessed with both a sense of Ghibli-esque whimsy and a cultural critique of modernisation-mad China that’s reminiscent of the works of Jia Zhang-ke, the film is at once an achingly sad portrait of loneliness, an epitaph for the glory days of cinema and a smart critique of people turning to technology to solve their problems. Enthusiastically received on its Berlin premiere, this low-key crowdpleaser is sure to charm MIFF audiences.
films.awards: Enthusiastically received on its Berlin premiere, this low-key crowdpleaser is sure to charm MIFF audiences.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.46/5；动画、剧情、实验、喜剧、冒险、奇幻、科幻 / 动画单元 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：亲密关系、记忆与历史、失去与哀悼、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：柏林电影节、首映

films.title: The Road to Zennor
films.synopsis: A poetic valentine to Mark Jenkin’s beloved Kodak Ektachrome, this hypnotic short glimpses Cornwall in bittersweet late autumn.
films.description: The mercurial moods of Cornwall in late autumn find their perfect match in Kodak’s famous Ektachrome emulsion, which evokes the encroaching shadows thrown by the last rays of the sun. Winner of the Best Experimental Film at the 2017 London Short Film Festival.
films.awards: Winner of the Best Experimental Film at the 2017 London Short Film Festival.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.30/5；纪录片、实验 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：导演表达与人物处境。奖项/展映线索：获奖

films.title: 9 Temples to Heaven
films.synopsis: An extended family go on a religious pilgrimage and navigate their interpersonal dramas along the way in this deeply personal reflection on modern Thailand.
films.description: When Sakol hears an unsettling, weirdly specific prophecy about his ageing mother’s imminent death, he springs into action. Knowing that nine is a fortuitous number in Thai culture, he bundles the same amount of family members into a mini-van to visit nine temples in one day. It’s an ambitious attempt to cultivate enough karma for “Grandma” to find a peaceful afterlife, but things don’t go as planned. As three generations of family members squabble over religion and personal responsibility, and they encounter a mix of genuinely kind and commercially opportunistic monks, Sakol’s grand plan starts to feel like a futile undertaking. As the day comes to an end, will his faith finally be rewarded? Sompot Chidgasornpongse’s debut, the documentary Railway Sleepers (MIFF 2017), bunked down with passengers taking long-distance train rides across Thailand. His second feature – and first venture into fiction – is a beautifully observed portrait of the relationship between religious tradition and capitalist society, drawing from personal experiences of his own family pilgrimages to Buddhist temples. Produced by Apichatpong Weerasethakul, on whose own films Sompot regularly worked as an assistant, and premiering at Cannes Directors’ Fortnight, 9 Temples to Heaven is a sensitive, involving saga.
films.awards: Produced by Apichatpong Weerasethakul, on whose own films Sompot regularly worked as an assistant, and premiering at Cannes Directors’ Fortnight, 9 Temples to Heaven is a sensitive, involving saga.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.39/5；剧情 / 亚太单元 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、亲密关系、政治与社会现实、失去与哀悼、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：戛纳、导演双周、首映

films.title: The River Train
films.synopsis: In this spellbinding urban fantasy, a young malambo dancer breaks free of his rigid rural life by catching a train to the Argentinian capital.
films.description: In the countryside, nine-year-old Milo seems stuck in an endless loop of responsibilities: washing dishes, helping to cook family dinners and, most significantly, training with his father to perfect malambo: a traditional Argentinian gaucho folk dance. One day, he catches a glimpse of a boy on TV raving about finding fame in Buenos Aires. This sparks a flame in Milo, who decides to escape his provincial existence by way of a city-bound train. But Milo quickly discovers that the journey and the destination are so much more than he could have ever imagined. Lorenzo Ferro and Lucas A. Vignale’s Berlinale-premiering feature debut is a sublime, magic-realist coming-of-age tale that blurs the factual and fantastical elements of Milo’s journey to striking effect. As the film unpacks its depictions of duality – between childhood and growing up, creative rigour and rebellion, the safety and stagnation of home – breakout actor Milo Barría skilfully embodies the protagonist’s wide-eyed hunger for adventure. Intimate and inventive, The River Train captures the ever-difficult question of whether to continue to dream or face the reality of waking life.
films.awards: Vignale’s Berlinale-premiering feature debut is a sublime, magic-realist coming-of-age tale that blurs the factual and fantastical elements of Milo’s journey to striking effect.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.38/5；剧情 / 拉丁美洲 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、成长与青春、音乐与表演。奖项/展映线索：柏林电影节、首映

films.title: Mouse
films.synopsis: The summer of 2002 changes everything for shy Arkansas teenager Minnie in this critically lauded queer coming-of-age marvel co-starring Sophie Okonedo.
films.description: Minnie and Callie are chalk and cheese. Callie is the star of every school play and has her sights set on Juilliard after graduation. Minnie hasn’t quite figured out what she wants to be yet, and has always been content cheering her best friend on from the sidelines. When a tragic accident tears their friendship apart just before the start of senior year, Minnie embarks on a close friendship with Callie’s elegant mother, Helen – a complicated dynamic that causes friction between Minnie and her own perpetually frazzled working mother, Barbara. The third feature collaboration from husband-and-wife duo Kelly O’Sullivan and Alex Thompson ( Saint Frances ; Ghostlight ), Mouse is a heartfelt and deeply humanistic portrayal of the complexities of navigating loss at any age. The film deftly shifts between wincing humour, sincerity and heartbreak thanks to committed performances from Katherine Mallen Kupferer as Minnie, Sophie Okonedo ( Clarissa , MIFF 2026) as Helen and Tara Mallen (Kupferer’s real-life mother, whom she also appeared alongside in Ghostlight ) as Barbara, while David Hyde Pierce contributes a poignant turn as Minnie’s emotionally intuitive drama teacher.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.87/5；剧情 / 北美单元 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、成长与青春、失去与哀悼、音乐与表演、恐怖 / 超自然。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Double Freedom
films.synopsis: In his newest work, fresh from its Cannes 2026 premiere, visionary director Lisandro Alonso revisits his acclaimed debut with a beguilingly rhythmic rural family tale.
films.description: Misael is content with his spartan life in a remote corner of Argentina’s picturesque La Pampa province. Dwelling in a shack, he spends his days cutting down trees, treating the wood and dealing with local traders. Soon, however, an unexpected responsibility upends his life, and the logic of his days fades away in a place where human reason holds no meaning. Lisandro Alonso returns 25 years later to the protagonist of his debut feature, 2001’s simple, soft-spoken landmark of slow cinema La libertad . This latest feature, whose title – in Spanish, La libertad doble – directly calls back to its predecessor, reunites Alonso with the same nonprofessional lead, Misael Saavedra, along with cinematographer Cobi Migliora, once again shooting on 35mm. Through this mirroring, the director reflects on how life in Argentina has grown more precarious over the past quarter-century, for lumberjacks and filmmakers alike.
films.awards: In his newest work, fresh from its Cannes 2026 premiere, visionary director Lisandro Alonso revisits his acclaimed debut with a beguilingly rhythmic rural family tale.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.36/5；剧情 / Lisandro Alonso / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、记忆与历史。奖项/展映线索：戛纳、首映

films.title: Marc by Sofia
films.synopsis: Made in her signature intimate style, Sofia Coppola’s debut documentary invites long-time friend Marc Jacobs to share his creative sensibility.
films.description: Filmmaker Sofia Coppola met fashion designer Marc Jacobs in 1993, when her downtown New York minimalism intrigued the upstart who’d just brought grunge to the Perry Ellis runway. They shared a taste for poised perfectionism, as well as an unusually self-effacing temperament among the big egos in their respective professions. Thirty years on, as Marc’s spring 2024 show approaches, his one-time muse hangs out in his studio, watching him plan the staging as meticulously as the clothes. “In another life I really want to be a theatre director,” sighs the 60-year-old as he enthuses about a creative practice they both know intimately. Jacobs and Coppola both intuitively gravitate towards the playful and attainable rather than confronting or luxuriating in wealth. Despite its familiar ‘prepping for the big show’ template, Coppola’s disinterest in both industry drama and didactic biography separates this Venice-premiering A24 documentary from more conventional couturier profiles; her fascination with the evanescent is instead perfectly suited to tracing the wandering pathways of creative inspiration and influence that guide her subject’s creative sensibility. Fans of Coppola’s fiction work will appreciate these generous conversations and associative flurries of archival montage alike.
films.awards: ...plate, Coppola’s disinterest in both industry drama and didactic biography separates this Venice-premiering A24 documentary from more conventional couturier profiles; her fascination with the evanescent is instead perfectly su...
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.35/5；纪录片 / 纪录片单元 / 维州首映。推荐理由：政治与社会现实、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：威尼斯、首映

films.title: Strawberries
films.synopsis: In this Cannes Un Certain Regard–premiering political drama, two Moroccan women doing seasonal work in Spain struggle against dire labour conditions.
films.description: Hoping to make ends meet, tetchy Hasna and timid Meriem set sail for the Spanish province of Huelva, where they will earn 35 euros per day as strawberry pickers. The work is onerous, and their difficult circumstances – the language barrier, the oppressive conditions, the distance from loved ones in Morocco – are ameliorated only by their camaraderie with their female colleagues. When acts of extortion and sexual assault throw a wrench into those relationships, however, Hasna and Meriem must decide whether to protect their livelihoods or their dignity. Co-written by Delphine Agut ( The Story of Souleymane , MIFF 2024; Inshallah a Boy , MIFF 2023) and based on real-world cases, Laïla Marrakchi’s bracing third feature depicts the insidious systems that enable migrant and gender-based exploitation across the Mediterranean. Timely and eye-opening, Strawberries sheds light not just on the toxic feedback loop between desperation and oppression, but also on the oblivious privilege of the First World societies that rely on this workforce. Nisrin Erradi ( Adam , MIFF 2019) is formidable as Hasna: a compelling yet flawed heroine whose combative spirit jars with her instinct to prioritise self-preservation.
films.awards: In this Cannes Un Certain Regard–premiering political drama, two Moroccan women doing seasonal work in Spain struggle against dire labour conditions.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.35/5；剧情 / 欧洲与英国 / 维州首映。推荐理由：亲密关系、政治与社会现实。奖项/展映线索：戛纳、一种关注、首映

films.title: The Best Summer
films.synopsis: Beastie Boys, Sonic Youth, Foo Fighters, Bikini Kill, Pavement and more, all at one fateful Aussie music festival. Get in line – we’re heading to 1995.
films.description: December 1995: riding high on her hit comedy Billy Madison and numerous celebrated music videos, Tamra Davis ( Jean-Michel Basquiat: The Radiant Child , MIFF 2010) brought her Sony Hi8 camcorder to Australia on tour with her new husband, Mike D of the Beastie Boys. At Summersault, an upstart festival hoping to outshine the Big Day Out, Davis recorded electric live sets from the Beasties and pals Pavement, Sonic Youth, Dave Grohl’s new band Foo Fighters and Kim Deal’s Breeders-hiatus project The Amps. And as Bikini Kill’s Kathleen Hanna playfully interviewed other musos backstage, Davis unknowingly documented the riot grrrl icon falling for future husband Adam ‘Ad-Rock’ Horovitz. Thirty years later, Davis found a shoebox of old 8mm videotapes while evacuating her home during the Los Angeles Palisades wildfire. This precious time capsule – a last hurrah of scrappy underground camaraderie before landmark albums and arena superstardom – formed the basis for this wistful feature-length window into another era. Summersault didn’t last, but its young promoter Steve Pavlovic went on to found cult label Modular Recordings, decisively shaping Australian indie music. “It was such a different time,” Davis told the crowd at the film’s 2026 Sundance premiere – and this shaggy hangout travelogue will take you right back there.
films.awards: “It was such a different time,” Davis told the crowd at the film’s 2026 Sundance premiere – and this shaggy hangout travelogue will take you right back there.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.34/5；纪录片、音乐 / 音乐电影 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、成长与青春、音乐与表演。奖项/展映线索：圣丹斯、首映

films.title: We Are All Strangers
films.synopsis: The first Singaporean film to screen in competition in the Berlinale’s 70-plus-year history, Anthony Chen’s latest feature is an epic, tender tale of biological and found family.
films.description: Having finished military service, Junyang is unsure what comes next – though this doesn’t stop him from disparaging his father Boon Kiat and the latter’s humble noodle stand. Junyang’s girlfriend Lydia, a piano prodigy with university in her sights, faces her mother’s distaste for their relationship. Meanwhile, Boon Kiat grows fond of ‘beer auntie’ Bee Hwa, who peddles alcohol near his stall. As life throws one curveball after another, it’s not long before these four individuals become deeply intertwined. Reuniting with Koh Jia Ler and Yeo Yann Yann – who both starred in Ilo Ilo (MIFF 2013) and Wet Season (MIFF 2020), the earlier entries in the director’s ‘Growing Up’ trilogy – Anthony Chen again dissects the intricacies of human relationships, this time also critiquing a Singapore where consumerism and class foment divisions. Recalling Edward Yang’s explorations of family, Chen’s masterful portrait dramatises the yearning masked by tough exteriors and devotion, however imperfectly expressed, for loved ones. Touching and quietly thought-provoking, We Are All Strangers challenges its own title, revealing how shared burdens can break down the walls that keep us apart.
films.awards: The first Singaporean film to screen in competition in the Berlinale’s 70-plus-year history, Anthony Chen’s latest feature is an epic, tender tale of biological and found family.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.34/5；剧情、爱情 / 亚太单元 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、亲密关系、政治与社会现实、记忆与历史、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：柏林电影节

films.title: The Blood Countess
films.synopsis: Screen icon Isabelle Huppert mesmerises as a glamorous vampire in iconoclastic queer filmmaker Ulrike Ottinger’s high-camp comedy.
films.description: Countess Elizabeth Báthory awakens from a four-century-long slumber in modern-day Vienna, costumed in extravagant crimson gowns and a perfectly coiffed copper updo. Reunited with her faithful handmaid Hermine and her self-hating vegetarian vampire nephew Rudi Bubi Baron von Strudl (with his sceptical psychotherapist in tow), the countess sets out to find and destroy an ancient magical book that has the power to end vampire immortality. With police inspectors and a duo of dithering vampirologists in hot pursuit, the eccentric entourage scours the city’s baroque libraries and cathedrals – but the countess soon realises that her nephew’s plans for the book are very different from her own. A glorious addition to an astonishing oeuvre, The Blood Countess is brought to eternal life by one-of-a-kind New German Cinema filmmaker Ulrike Ottinger ( Paris Calligrammes , MIFF 2020), who is the subject of an entire strand devoted to her work and influences this year, Ulrike Ottinger: Radical Travels. Co-written by Nobel laureate and author of The Piano Teacher Elfriede Jelinek, this playful reimagining of one of history’s most notorious serial killers is a sumptuous visual feast. In a role she was born to play, Isabelle Huppert embodies the coolly fabulous countess, leading a committed cast including Birgit Minichmayr ( Everyone Else , MIFF 2016), Lars Eidinger ( Dying , MIFF 2024), Thomas Schubert ( Afire , MIFF 2023) and iconic former Eurovision winner Conchita Wurst.
films.awards: ...r ( Dying , MIFF 2024), Thomas Schubert ( Afire , MIFF 2023) and iconic former Eurovision winner Conchita Wurst....
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.02/5；剧情、喜剧、惊悚、恐怖 / Ulrike Ottinger: Radical Travels / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、记忆与历史。奖项/展映线索：获奖

films.title: Queen at Sea
films.synopsis: Juliette Binoche and Tom Courtenay star in this unflinchingly knotty drama about a woman and her stepfather at odds over her dementia-stricken mother
films.description: Juliette Binoche and Tom Courtenay star in this unflinchingly knotty drama about a woman and her stepfather at odds over her dementia-stricken mother. Recently divorced academic Amanda (played by fearless screen icon Juliette Binoche) has relocated to London to be closer to her mother, Leslie, who has advanced dementia. While checking on Leslie one day, Amanda walks in on her and her stepfather, Martin, having sex, despite recent medical advice stating that Leslie is no longer able to give meaningful consent. Furious, Amanda calls the police, setting in motion a chain of processes that completely upends their lives – and especially Martin’s role as Leslie’s carer – leaving Amanda to question her decision.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.82/5；剧情 / 主打佳片 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Nino
films.synopsis: A modern riff on Cléo from 5 to 7 , this tender debut feature follows the weekend odyssey of a young Parisian man after receiving a surprising diagnosis
films.description: A modern riff on Cléo from 5 to 7 , this tender debut feature follows the weekend odyssey of a young Parisian man after receiving a surprising diagnosis. Nino’s 29th birthday weekend kicks off with a gut punch. His doctor informs him that not only does he have throat cancer, but he must start chemotherapy on Monday and also produce a sperm sample to freeze in case the treatment renders him infertile. Over the course of the weekend, Nino moves through the world in a fog while life goes on as normal around him. He visits his mother, runs into an old schoolmate and attends the surprise party thrown for him by his best friend. At every turn, Nino struggles to break the news to his loved ones – because to say it out loud would make it real.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.81/5；剧情 / 欧洲与英国 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、亲密关系。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: The Awake
films.synopsis: Be immersed in a dance-driven VR journey into the inner world of ALS patients, whose frozen bodies conceal minds and souls burning with resilience.
films.description: Vision becomes the primary anchor to reality for patients in the later stages of motor neurone disease – also known as amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, or ALS. Twin interdisciplinary choreographers Hsieh Hsiao-Ting and Hsieh Hsiao-Wei invite you, too, to watch without moving. Poetic, abstract dance evokes ALS patients’ rich, heightened consciousness as everyday objects dissolve under the weight of illness: a motionless wheelchair; drifting curtains; a dining table for two; a fish in its bowl. Winner of the Humanity Award in the 2026 XR Competition at NewImages Festival in Paris, this meditative work entwines the sensory and virtual to express something hidden but intensely human.
films.awards: Winner of the Humanity Award in the 2026 XR Competition at NewImages Festival in Paris, this meditative work entwines the sensory and virtual to express something hidden but intensely human.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.21/5；实验 / 沉浸式 XR / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：政治与社会现实、音乐与表演。奖项/展映线索：获奖

films.title: Back Home
films.synopsis: Let loose in Laos without a crew, master auteur Tsai Ming-liang delivers a quiet, contemplative meditation on place and belonging.
films.description: Slow-cinema legend Tsai Ming-liang accompanies actor Anong Houngheuangsy ( Days , MIFF 2021) back to his hometown, a rural Lao village situated amid rice paddies. Armed with only a Leica camera and a Canon camcorder, Tsai undertakes a minimalist meditation on the power of place. More drawn to the surrounding environment than the reunion between Anong and his family, Tsai’s ever-patient camera gazes on dogs, cats, cows, goats and house plants. For the Buddhist filmmaker, these lives are just as interesting as those of family members, farmers selling their wares at markets and stonemasons carving statues of Buddha. Tsai calls this film, shot over two weeks, a work of “handcrafted cinema”. Premiering at Venice, Back Home offers another still, quiet study for those attuned to Tsai’s filmmaking approach, and particularly the entries in his ongoing Walking series ( Abiding Nowhere , MIFF 2021; Journey to the West , MIFF 2014). Tsai first graced Melbourne screens with his sumptuous Vive l’amour in 1995; each new outing for cinema’s Zen master is a humble treasure to behold.
films.awards: Premiering at Venice, Back Home offers another still, quiet study for those attuned to Tsai’s filmmaking approach, and particularly the entr...
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.28/5；纪录片 / 纪录新视野 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实。奖项/展映线索：威尼斯、首映

films.title: Gail Daughtry and the Celebrity Sex Pass
films.synopsis: Laugh your Oz off in this delightfully ludicrous farce as genre parody king David Wain sends a perky Kansas gal off on a whirlwind quest to seduce Jon Hamm.
films.description: Wholesome young Kansas hairdresser Gail (Zoey Deutch, Nouvelle Vague , MIFF 2025) is about to marry her perfect guy … until they attend a Jennifer Aniston book signing and she catches him and Jen being way more than Friends . Fair’s fair: now Gail gets a pass to chase her own celebrity crush, Mad Men ’s studly Don Draper. Gail and her sassy hair salon pal Otto head for Hollywood, gathering a ragtag band of LA helpers including budding talent agent Caleb, jaded paparazzo Vincent and washed-up ex– Mad Men actor John Slattery. Will TV’s wonderful wizard of ohhhs grant Gail’s sexy wish? After all, there’s no place like Hamm … David Wain has been wrangling retro comedy tropes since his 2001 debut, the cult-hit summer camp parody Wet Hot American Summer . He’s spoofed rom-com clichés ( They Came Together ) and lampooned the founder of National Lampoon magazine ( A Futile and Stupid Gesture ). For over a decade, Wain worked mainly in TV, until he popped up at Sundance 2026 to ruin the festival’s gravitas with this absolutely gag-stuffed, star-studded Tinseltown romp shot entirely on location. Behind the curtain of its determined silliness is an affectionate, surprisingly wholesome ode to a city that runs on desire.
films.awards: For over a decade, Wain worked mainly in TV, until he popped up at Sundance 2026 to ruin the festival’s gravitas with this absolutely gag-stuffed, star-studded Tinseltown romp shot entirely on location.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.28/5；喜剧 / 北美单元 / 维州首映。推荐理由：亲密关系、音乐与表演。奖项/展映线索：圣丹斯

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Harlem
films.synopsis: Gather alongside Harlem Renaissance luminaries in this incredible time capsule, finally completed more than 50 years after the footage was shot.
films.description: In 1972, groundbreaking Black filmmaker William Greaves – maker of the reality-bending 1968 vérité masterpiece Symbiopsychotaxiplasm: Take One (MIFF 2023) – issued an invitation to the surviving figureheads of the cultural boom that emanated from Harlem in the 1920s and 30s: to come together once again. The likes of Broadway pioneers Noble Sissle and Eubie Blake, artist Aaron Douglas and activist Louise Thompson Patterson convened one summer evening at the stately home of Duke Ellington, where the drinks and reminiscences flowed, crystallised by time. Behind the camera at this historic gathering was William’s son, David Greaves. With the elder Greaves having passed in 2014, it fell to David to ensure that this remarkable footage could at last be seen and celebrated. In Once Upon a Time in Harlem , he skilfully blends roving scenes from the party with sit-down interviews, also filmed onsite, to create an inviting, immersive atmosphere that positively crackles with the revolutionary verve and commentary of its subjects.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.77/5；纪录片、Historical / 纪录片单元 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Time and Tide
films.synopsis: A Melbourne filmmaker returns to the small Chinese town of his childhood, reconnecting with his family under difficult, emotionally profound circumstances.
films.description: When his father suffers a stroke, Melbourne-based filmmaker Vee Shi returns to his hometown, Longtian, Fuqing, in south-eastern China, a small rural community that he left behind when he was 13. As the family’s proud, stubborn patriarch struggles with his compromised health and need for care, his estranged wife and daughter – who, due to China’s one-child policy, was given up for adoption prior to the director’s birth – are suddenly entrusted with the duty, and burden, of looking after a man who has often treated them badly. These strained family dynamics evoke a bigger-picture portrait of changing Chinese society and social values, where the once-total dedication to family is falling away. This unique hybrid documentary, initially conceived of as a scripted film wherein Shi’s family would play themselves, is an at times shockingly intimate portrait of familial love, duty and trauma. The MIFF Accelerator alum’s previous short Jia (MIFF 2023) was a drama inspired by his relationship with his mother, as well as the familial and cultural estrangement of living in a different country in another hemisphere. The Visions du Réel–selected Time and Tide – the first film ever to be shot in Longtian, and only the second to feature the disappearing Fuqing dialect – profoundly and often painfully extends that inquiry to the contested space of a fractured family home.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.76/5；纪录片、剧情 / 纪录新视野 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、成长与青春、亲密关系、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: The Man I Love
films.synopsis: Rami Malek captivates as an HIV-positive man who combats mortality with moxie in Ira Sachs’s Cannes-competing ode to queerness and libertinism.
films.description: New York, late 1980s. Having just left hospital, ebullient theatre artist Jimmy wants to take on life itself: partying with other figures from the West Village demimonde; preparing a spectacular oeuvre for the stage; being seen and loved in every way possible. He is loved, of course, despite his mercurial ego: by Dennis, his devoted partner, and by Brenda, his sister. But Jimmy’s HIV meds don’t guarantee survival; so, when a handsome young Brit moves into the building, Jimmy proceeds with abandon. Following Peter Hujar’s Day (MIFF 2025), Ira Sachs crafts another stirring time capsule of queer New York; The Man I Love also recalls his immediately previous work, Passages (MIFF 2023), in featuring a love triangle, but here the focus is less on erotic foibles than on the impetuousness of living on borrowed time. Working from a script from Sachs and his regular co-writer Mauricio Zacharias, and acting alongside a formidable Tom Sturridge, Rami Malek brilliantly incarnates Jimmy’s unapologetic audacity and the yearning fragility beneath his bravado. More than an AIDS drama, The Man I Love is a story of perseverance and performativity – about the shows we put on despite the obstacles of health and the heart.
films.awards: Rami Malek captivates as an HIV-positive man who combats mortality with moxie in Ira Sachs’s Cannes-competing ode to queerness and libertinism.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.25/5；剧情 / 主打佳片 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、亲密关系。奖项/展映线索：戛纳

films.title: La Perra
films.synopsis: From Cannes Directors’ Fortnight comes a haunting tale of a woman with a troubled past and the free-spirited dog that gives her life renewed meaning
films.description: From Cannes Directors’ Fortnight comes a haunting tale of a woman with a troubled past and the free-spirited dog that gives her life renewed meaning. In a wind-lashed, sparsely inhabited island off the coast of Chile, Silvia (Manuela Oyarzún, No , MIFF 2012) supplements her fishmonger husband’s income by harvesting seaweed. One day, she crosses paths with a puppy, whom she decides to adopt and name Yuri. Over time, she and this wayward creature – both seemingly forsaken by life – become inseparable. But when Yuri suddenly disappears, unhealed wounds from Silvia’s childhood are reopened.
films.awards: From Cannes Directors’ Fortnight comes a haunting tale of a woman with a troubled past and the free-spirited dog that gives her life renewed meaning | From Cannes Directors’ Fortnight comes a haunting tale of a woman with a troubled past and the free-spirited dog that gives her life renewed meaning.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.23/5；剧情 / 拉丁美洲 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：成长与青春、政治与社会现实、记忆与历史、音乐与表演。奖项/展映线索：戛纳、导演双周

films.title: Portrait of Jason
films.synopsis: In Shirley Clarke’s magnetic work of queer cinema, New York hustler, would-be cabaret star and raconteur supreme Jason Holliday delivers a monologue for the ages.
films.description: Holding court over a long, boozy December night at New York’s Hotel Chelsea, Jason Holliday serves up wild anecdotes of a life lived on the city’s margins, including working the streets as a hustler, doing time on Riker’s Island and even befriending Miles Davis – fraught, flamboyant tales that become profound reflections on race, class and sexuality. Meanwhile, the offscreen prompts from director Shirley Clarke ( Ornette: Made in America , MIFF 1987), a pioneering figure in documentary’s avant-garde, add to the film’s air of electric, off-the-cuff vérité. Just how much of Jason’s studied fabulousness, in which the self is nested within endless layers of performance, is an elaborate shield against a hostile world, including the provocations of his director? And to what extent might Clarke, whose antagonism stokes the film’s irresistible frisson, be said to be exploiting him? Both an exquisite study in character and a fascinating look at the ethics behind capturing performance in documentary, Portrait of Jason – like its subject – is a slippery, shapeshifting work, as exhilarating as it is unsettling.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.83/5；纪录片 / 锋利现实。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Memory
films.synopsis: Visually imaginative and richly symbolic, this filmic collage recalls the writer/director’s own childhood in Chechnya, where she witnessed the atrocities of war firsthand.
films.description: In the late 1980s, six-year-old Vladlena Sandu was sent from Crimea to Grozny, Chechnya, to live with her grandparents. Little did she, or her family, know that the horrors of war would be right on their doorstep over the following decades through the fall of the Soviet Union and the ensuing Russo-Chechen Wars. In this poetic, autobiographical hybrid documentary, Sandu revisits her traumatic childhood memories, confronting the ways in which geopolitical conflict impacts children, and asking what can be done to break these generational cycles of violence. The influence of Andrei Tarkovsky, Pier Paolo Pasolini and Sergei Parajanov looms large in the evocative and lyrical imagery that makes up Memory , which incorporates live-action re-enactment, puppetry, animation, archival material and surreal vignettes shot on 16mm film. Nevertheless, this staggering, unique cinematic tapestry is thoroughly Sandu’s own. Depicting her tumultuous past with breathtaking intimacy and artistry, she also bears singular witness to the children whose lives are being threatened today by wars and conflict the world over.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.72/5；纪录片 / 线上放映 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、成长与青春、政治与社会现实、记忆与历史、悬疑 / 犯罪张力、纪录片视角、恐怖 / 超自然。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Whistle
films.synopsis: Welcome to the Masters of Musical Whistling, where the competitors require monumental skill, massive determination and a mighty wind.
films.description: The Masters of Musical Whistling is an international competition that celebrates, well, whistling – at an elite level. Every two years, dedicated and supremely talented whistlers converge on Hollywood to showcase their art and, hopefully, whistle up a coveted trophy. The 2023 competitors include global whistling superstar Molly Lewis, who features on the Barbie soundtrack; Spaniard Ayna, who’s inherited her circus-performing parent’s charismatic stage presence; and Yuki, a Japanese whistling prodigy sick of being the runner-up. They’re all here because Carole Anne Kaufman, founder of the Masters and two-time world champion, wants to change how the world thinks about whistling. Thanks to Australian director Christopher Nelius, she just might. With genuine affection and care, Nelius ( Girls Can’t Surf ; Buoy , MIFF 2017) documents high and low notes for Kaufman and her competitors, on and offstage. Nelius clearly understands the visual language of the mockumentary; but while many of his subjects wouldn’t be out of place in a Christopher Guest film, there’s no mockery here. Instead, he respectfully centres this community’s incredible artistry through all the tears, bruised egos, heartaches and soaring joys. Funny, poignant and wonderfully weird, Whistle is a treat from start to finish – and, perhaps, just what the world needs right now.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.71/5；纪录片、喜剧 / 澳洲电影 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、音乐与表演。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Revolving Rounds
films.synopsis: A camera floats across farmland and into an ancient stereoscopic device, plunging the viewer into the splintered heart of the image itself
films.description: A camera floats across farmland and into an ancient stereoscopic device, plunging the viewer into the splintered heart of the image itself. A hit with audiences at MIFF 2025, Johann Lurf’s unforgettable collaboration with artist Christina Jauernik returns for a special 3D encore of experiences. Harnessing synchronised cameras and a vintage cyclostereoscope, the Austrian filmmakers’ visionary work moves from a hypnotic, pastoral landscape into the inner workings of mechanised wonder, before seeming – via some cinematic wizardry – to dive into the film grain itself. A micro universe of a million particles, rendered in three spectacular dimensions. Awarded a Special Mention at Locarno 2024.
films.awards: Awarded a Special Mention at Locarno 2024.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.51/5；实验。推荐理由：政治与社会现实。奖项/展映线索：获奖、洛迦诺

films.title: Sheep in the Box
films.synopsis: In Hirokazu Kore-eda’s sci-fi-inflected parable, a bereaved couple adopt an AI-powered android to deal with a tragic loss.
films.description: In a near-future Japan of delivery drones and diet-monitoring fridges, REbirth has devised the ultimate remedy for grieving parents: a ‘replacement’ in the form of an anthropomorphic robot. Two years prior, an accident took architect Otone and carpenter Kensuke’s son Kakeru; when REbirth offers them a free trial, Otone doesn’t hesitate. While she becomes enamoured of the revived Kakeru, Kensuke is sceptical: “It’s a Tamagotchi. It’s a Roomba.” Gradually, they learn that making do is not the same as moving on. Despite its sci-fi trappings, Sheep in the Box is, more accurately, a study of complicated families, building on director Hirokazu Kore-eda’s earlier works Broker (MIFF 2022), Like Father, Like Son (MIFF 2013) and the Palme d’Or–winning Shoplifters (MIFF 2018). Inspired by an illustration from The Little Prince and evoking Pinocchio with its (not so) ‘real boy’, the film raises questions about humanity, loss and the ways technology can both help and harm. Actors Haruka Ayase ( Our Little Sister , MIFF 2015) and Daigo movingly portray Otone’s technophilia and Kensuke’s neo-Luddism as synecdoche for coping, while Rimu Kuwaki precociously imbues the robotic Kakeru with an affecting emotional depth.
films.awards: ...zu Kore-eda’s earlier works Broker (MIFF 2022), Like Father, Like Son (MIFF 2013) and the Palme d’Or–winning Shoplifters (MIFF 2018)....
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 2.89/5；剧情、科幻 / 主打佳片 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实、失去与哀悼。奖项/展映线索：金棕榈相关、获奖

films.title: Mile End Kicks
films.synopsis: Over one cool indie-sleaze summer in the city of poutine and Arcade Fire, a self-sabotaging rising rock critic played by Barbie Ferreira falls for two guys in the same band.
films.description: In 2011, ambitious young music critic Grace plans a summer sabbatical in Montreal, sick of getting exploited by her Toronto alt-weekly editor and snubbed by chin-stroking bro colleagues. Moving in with chic DJ Madeleine and drummer Hugo, Grace starts promoting Hugo’s band Bone Patrol to impress its vain frontman Chevy, “the worst guy in Montreal” – though something slower and sweeter simmers with floppy-haired guitarist Archie. Things go off the frets as Grace’s planned 33 ⅓ book on Alanis Morissette’s Jagged Little Pill falls by the wayside, her funds run perilously low and past traumas resurface as bad choices. Writer/director Chandler Levack (Netflix’s Roommates ) based her sophomore feature on her own youth in Montreal’s alt trenches. Her affectionate but bittersweet romantic dramedy – more Reality Bites than Almost Famous – premiered at TIFF and toured to Vancouver, Stockholm and SXSW. As Grace, Barbie Ferreira ( Bob Trevino Likes It , MIFF 2024) brings fierce empathy to an impulsive, sometimes exasperating but easily recognisable protagonist, while Bone Patrol’s personnel include members of real-life Montreal band TOPS.
films.awards: In 2011, ambitious young music critic Grace plans a summer sabbatical in Montreal, sick of getting exploited by her Toronto alt-weekly editor and snubbed by chin-stroking bro colleagues.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.19/5；剧情、喜剧、音乐、爱情 / 北美单元 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：成长与青春、记忆与历史、音乐与表演。奖项/展映线索：多伦多

films.title: Strangers When We Meet
films.synopsis: Kim Novak excels alongside Kirk Douglas in this heartbreaking story of an extramarital affair among the sleepy suburban hills of 1950s Los Angeles.
films.description: Margaret “Maggie” Gault (Kim Novak) is listless, persisting in a stable but passionless marriage to a conservative prude. Kirk Douglas is Larry Coe, an architect in a happy but comfortable marriage who has just taken on a new project to design a house for a successful writer played by Ernie Kovacs ( Bell, Book and Candle , MIFF 2026). After a meet-cute at school pick-up and a follow-up at the local grocery store, Maggie and Larry embark on a tender, deeply felt affair, sharing a connection they haven’t previously enjoyed with anyone else – one in which they can be their authentic selves. But when nosy neighbour Felix (Walter Matthau, The Odd Couple ) discovers the dalliance, he makes a sinister play for Larry’s wife. Adapted from a novel by Evan Hunter, Strangers When We Meet is a gorgeously rendered melodrama that fields a mature viewpoint on adult relationships, layered with shades of grey. The film boasts an exceptional supporting cast: Matthau revels in his sleazy role as a smug spanner in the works; while Barbara Rush is also wonderful, exuding quiet wisdom and grace as Larry’s pragmatic and perceptive wife – her rebuff of Felix’s unwanted advances is a scene-stealer. The house that Larry constructs, meanwhile, was purpose-built for the film, designed by legendary Hollywood art directors Carl Anderson and Ross Bellah.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.77/5；剧情、爱情 / Kim Novak: Hollywood Heretic。推荐理由：家庭关系、成长与青春、亲密关系。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: The Dollhouse
films.synopsis: When you invite someone into your home, how do you treat them? Play along with little Juniper and help her learn the kindness her family lacks.
films.description: In the handmade paper diorama of her dollhouse, nine-year-old Juniper untangles her feelings of guilt about how her family treated Magnolia, a refugee who worked as a cleaner in their house. Re-enacting troubled memories with her dolls, an ashamed Juniper finds the courage to disagree with her parents and follow her heart. Using childlike textures inspired by puppetry, this animated interactive XR work explores how power dynamics arise intimately in our homes, passing between generations. Audiences at Cannes, Annecy, BFI London Film Festival and SXSW have already discovered it’s never too late to be kind – can you help Juniper figure out how? “Inside a Tim Burton–style dollhouse … the film seeks to expose the conditions of mistreatment that too many domestic workers around the world endure every day.” – Wired Italia
films.awards: Audiences at Cannes, Annecy, BFI London Film Festival and SXSW have already discovered it’s never too late to be kind – can you help Juniper figure out how?
