films.title: Los muertos
films.synopsis: Lisandro Alonso’s sophomore feature follows a newly freed ex-con on an upriver trip into the jungle, seeking either redemption or retribution
films.description: Lisandro Alonso’s sophomore feature follows a newly freed ex-con on an upriver trip into the jungle, seeking either redemption or retribution. Vargas is a convicted murderer coming to the end of decades of incarceration in the ramshackle, sun-dappled rural prison that has long been his home. On his release, seemingly ill at ease with his newfound freedom, he sets out on a journey into the Argentine jungle armed with his trusty machete. He has two goals in mind: one is to deliver a letter to the wife of another prisoner; the second is to see his estranged daughter, for whom he’s splurged on a new green blouse. As he journeys north along the Paraná River into increasingly wild surrounds, we’re left to wonder if Vargas is a man rehabilitated, or if violence is bound to follow wherever he goes.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.65/5；剧情 / Lisandro Alonso / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、悬疑 / 犯罪张力。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Statues Also Die?
films.synopsis: Communist history decays quietly under children’s curious gaze, leaving statues to commune with one another.
films.description: Budapest’s Memento Park is an ideological graveyard where uprooted communist-era monuments are reinstalled in conversation. What might they say now? Children lead this imaginative inquiry, interviewing passing tourists and older Hungarian visitors who lived through communism. Riffing on the title of a 1953 Chris Marker and Alain Resnais short, this playful elegy to fallen political aspirations probes crumbling stone for lingering sparks of life.

films.title: Despite the Night
films.synopsis: Rising stars Joe Bird and Ed Oxenbould play brothers agonising over whether to stay with their grandmother in a looming environmental collapse.
films.description: As a volcanic ash cloud plunges northern Australia into an eternal night, Cole (Joe Bird, Leviticus ) begs his emphysemic brother Moss (Ed Oxenbould, Head South , MIFF 2024) to flee the sealed apartment where they’ve been caring for their ailing grandmother. A quiet struggle unfolds between them as an oppressive inertia swallows their city.

films.title: Phenomena
films.synopsis: A filmmaker’s approach to chemistry experiments produces dazzlingly psychedelic results in this spectacular, scientifically illuminating Australian debut
films.description: A filmmaker’s approach to chemistry experiments produces dazzlingly psychedelic results in this spectacular, scientifically illuminating Australian debut. When thinking about the world’s natural wonders, it’s likely the big stuff – the mountains, the canyons, the oceans – that first spring to mind. But what about those things you can’t see, such as light, gravity and quantum energy? Using a high-powered camera and absolutely no AI, Melbourne-based filmmaker Josef Gatti makes brilliantly manifest those intangible building blocks of our universe. Across 10 chapters, each dedicated to a single scientific concept, kaleidoscopic Petri dish displays are augmented by tracks from Nils Frahm and British electronic musician Rival Consoles. It’s nature, but like you’ve never seen it before.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.63/5；纪录片、实验、音乐 / 学校单元 / 维州首映。推荐理由：音乐与表演。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: The Fox
films.synopsis: Jai Courtney stars – and Olivia Colman and Sam Neill voice a fox and a magpie respectively – in this absurdist black comedy about small-town duplicity and discontent.
films.description: Affable oaf Nick (Jai Courtney, Felony , MIFF Closing Night 2014) cruises through life courtesy of his rich daddy’s money, and his fiancée Kori (Emily Browning, One More Shot , MIFF 2025) has just accepted his marriage proposal. But when he discovers she’s been cheating on him – with the husband of her best friend! – everything unravels. While out hunting one day, he catches a talking fox, who tells him there’s a magical pit in the forest that can fix all of his problems. Nick hatches a plan … but they don’t call foxes tricksters for nothing. Marking the feature directorial debut of Danger 5 co-creator Dario Russo – who also writes, edits and composes here – this tale of dissatisfied couples and the percipient animals that gawk and gossip about their silly human foibles deftly showcases his singular brand of dark humour. Alongside the animatronic critters, the star-studded cast – which also includes Australian actors Damon Herriman ( Once Upon a Time in Hollywood , MIFF 2019) and Miranda Otto ( Love Serenade , MIFF 1996) – goes ham with the film’s audacious concept, building a ‘be careful what you wish for’ fable like no other.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.62/5；喜剧、惊悚 / 澳洲电影 / 维州首映。推荐理由：亲密关系、政治与社会现实、音乐与表演。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Ghost in the Cell
films.synopsis: When a vengeful ghost starts mutilating the inmates of one of Indonesia’s toughest prisons, the men must work together to stay alive in this socially conscious action-horror-comedy.
films.description: The newest inmate to join cell block C of Labuan Angsana prison is Dimas, a weedy journalist doing time for the brutal murder of his editor. Suspiciously soon after Dimas’s arrival, prisoners start getting picked off one by one, their bodies found gruesomely maimed and arranged into macabre sculptures that would make Hannibal Lecter proud. The prisoners panic, pointing the finger at Dimas, but one clairvoyant inmate is adamant that there’s a sinister supernatural entity behind the killings. He warns the others that they’d better keep their auras clean through art, prayer and dance, lest they become the ghost’s next victim. The 12th feature by Indonesian genre master Joko Anwar ( Satan’s Slaves ), this action-packed horror-comedy has a lot on its mind. Smuggled in between the film’s visually imaginative gore and twisted sense of humour is an astute commentary on systemic inequality and corruption, using the prison setting and its ensemble of colourful characters as a microcosm of society at large – with a few dance battles thrown in for good measure.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.60/5；恐怖 / 午夜 / 类型片 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实、悬疑 / 犯罪张力、音乐与表演、恐怖 / 超自然。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Fatherland
films.synopsis: In Paweł Pawlikowski’s third post-WWII meditation, exiled author Thomas Mann tours a devastated Germany whose divisions echo his own fractured family.
films.description: In 1949, ageing German Nobel laureate Thomas Mann breaks his long exile from his homeland to honour Johann Wolfgang von Goethe’s 200th anniversary in the latter’s birthplace of Frankfurt, West Germany. Next, he plans to visit the city where the literary giant died: Weimar, now in the communist East. Chauffeured by his resentful actor/writer daughter Erika, Thomas crosses a country he barely recognises, even as he waxes poetic about its heritage. Meanwhile, Erika notices in person – as her beloved, tormented brother Klaus does in his fiction – how two divergent but equally compromised regimes are being installed atop the still-festering wounds of Nazism. The country they grew up in is a fading memory; but can this intellectual family even feel at home with one another? Paweł Pawlikowski’s drama is the third in a thematic triptych of European postwar reverberations – filmed, like its predecessors Ida and Cold War (MIFF 2018), in luminous black-and-white. Pawlikowski’s immaculate road trip through Germany’s cultural crossroads follows a crisp yet elliptical map of details: disgusted looks at parties; silences in half-empty restaurants; dryly ironic new uses for old infrastructure. Meanwhile, the Manns’ repressed tensions emerge gradually in masterful lead performances by Hanns Zischler ( Kings of the Road , MIFF 1976) and Sandra Hüller ( Rose , MIFF 2026; Anatomy of a Fall , MIFF 2023), whose brilliantly understated turn as Erika captures a breadth of emotions lurking just below the surface.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.59/5；剧情、Historical / 主打佳片 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实、记忆与历史。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Species
films.synopsis: A stressed-out young doctor suspects hostile jobs are doing something gnarly to her generation. Turns out work doesn’t just suck – it mutates.
films.description: Margot (Mara Taquin, Zero Fucks Given ) has worked hard to land her first job as a doctor in France’s most prestigious emergency department. But even in this life-and-death medical field, Charles Boyau Hospital is a brutal pressure cooker: the relentless competition among colleagues and merciless production-line pace set by her boss Hélène (Karin Viard, Polisse , MIFF 2011) have Margot so stressed that she’s literally sweating blood. She recalls treating similar unexplained symptoms in patients her own age: young professionals in French workplaces are brutally lashing out, seeming somehow to be … changing. Is this extreme burnout, a novel virus or a transformative environmental adaptation? Premiering in the Cannes Midnight Screenings section, Marion Le Corroller’s brash debut feature shares a blood type with her body- horreur compatriots Coralie Fargeat ( The Substance , MIFF 2024) and Julia Ducournau ( Titane , MIFF 2021). But Species delivers its critique of ‘rise-and-grind’ productivity culture with an axe, not a scalpel: every frame is a gonzo frenzy, captured expressionistically by cinematographer Guillaume Schiffman at tilted angles and through fish-eye lenses, all set to a pulsating electronic score by ROB. Imagine Philip Brophy’s Body Melt (MIFF 1994) infecting The Pitt and you might at least have the right genus … but Species is a breed all its own.

films.title: The Last First: Winter K2
films.synopsis: A breathtaking examination of hubris colliding full-force with the limits of human endurance as competing teams of adventurers aim to make history on the world’s most dangerous mountain.
films.description: At the turn of 2021, 13 of the world’s 14 highest mountains had been summitted in winter. The exception? The second-highest mountain on Earth, K2, a notoriously perilous western Himalayan peak towering 8600 metres above sea level. In the northern winter of late 2020, in the depths of the COVID pandemic and with violent snowstorms on the way, Icelandic alpinist John Snorri Sigurjónsson and Pakistani father and son Ali and Sajid Sadpara – all veteran mountaineers – prepare their bid to enter the record books; without warning, they’re joined at base camp by throngs of others vying to be the first, including Nepalese mountaineering rock star Nirmal Purja and commercial climbing tourists of varying experience. As ego, capitalism and the deep scars of colonialism clash with ethics, physics and the indifference of howling winds and infinitesimally icy temperatures, the stage is set for an epic and catastrophic competition. Bringing together stunning big-screen visuals courtesy of influencer GoPros and professional film crews who accompanied the would-be summiteers, director Amir Bar-Lev ( Long Strange Trip , MIFF 2017) masterfully layers a gripping, heartbreaking and nuanced picture of the moral and philosophical conundrums at the heart of extreme achievement.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.57/5；纪录片、冒险 / 自然世界 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实、记忆与历史。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Chili Finger
films.synopsis: A lawyer receives a cash settlement after finding a finger in her fast food – but things start to go sideways when the company investigates how it got there.
films.description: Jess (Judy Greer, Lemon , MIFF 2017), a lawyer in a nondescript Midwestern town, is struggling. With her daughter having left for college, she’s struck by how empty an empty nest feels, particularly when left alone with her nice but not overly exciting husband (Sean Astin) and no money to go anywhere. Her problems seem to be solved when, at local fast food joint Blake Junior’s, she finds a finger in her chilli con carne bowl and a damage-controlling franchise executive offers her a $100,000 settlement on the spot. But when the restaurant’s owner and namesake (John Goodman) hears of the payout, he smells something fishy, and puts his shady yet trusted friend Dave (Bryan Cranston) on the case. Based (very loosely) on a true story that took place in California in 2005, this tar-black crime comedy evokes the early works of the Coen brothers but determinedly follows its own spicy path. Blessed with a cast of incredible character actors all having a ball, Chili Finger is headlined by an electric Greer. So often consigned to supporting parts, she goes to town on her meaty leading role, digging into Jess’s emotional complexity and giving the best performance of her career. It all adds up to a wild, hilarious ride of bad decisions and worse consequences.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.56/5；剧情、喜剧、惊悚 / 北美单元 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、悬疑 / 犯罪张力、音乐与表演。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Gone to Earth
films.synopsis: Hollywood siren Jennifer Jones gives an unforgettable performance as a free spirit caught between the passions of a clergyman and a fox-hunting squire in Powell and Pressburger’s ravishing, rarely screened melodrama.
films.description: In a rural England somewhere between the late 19th century and a Technicolor dream, headstrong nature girl Hazel Woodus – played by a swooning, windswept Jennifer Jones ( The Song of Bernadette ) – lives a carefree existence communing with the animals and fairies of the untamed country. Like the foxes with whom she shares a spiritual kinship, Hazel finds herself hunted by both a dark, dashing squire (David Farrar, Black Narcissus ) and a straitlaced clergyman (Cyril Cusack), two men to whom she refuses to be shackled. Steeped in mysticism and eerie folklore – and serving as vivid inspiration for Kate Bush’s Hounds of Love – this expressionistic romance from the legendary Michael Powell and Emeric Pressburger ( The Red Shoes ; A Matter of Life and Death ) was the British filmmakers’ lone foray into Hollywood, in part due to co-producer (and Jones’s husband) David O. Selznick later bowdlerising the film for its US release as The Wild Heart . Presented here in its original version – and on a vibrant 35mm print, courtesy of Martin Scorsese’s personal collection – the film is a haunting tale of doomed love and the liberating power of nature, led by Jones’s foxy, ferocious performance.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.66/5；剧情、爱情、Period / 锋利现实。推荐理由：亲密关系、政治与社会现实、失去与哀悼、音乐与表演。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: The Day She Returns
films.synopsis: Ever-industrious South Korean auteur Hong Sang-soo meditates on the nature of performance in this tale of a celeb coming in from the cold.
films.description: An actor sits in a restaurant for three back-to-back interviews with journalists about her role in a new independent film, which marks her return to the screen after a 12-year hiatus – a time period that coincided with her (recently ended) marriage. Over coffee and, later, a few crisp lagers, the movie star and her interlocutors chat about everything from dog breeds to divorce, from ageing to authentic self-expression. Despite the press cycle’s familiar beats, epiphanies emerge from both the conversations and the reflective silences in between. The MIFF program would almost feel incomplete without a film from treasured festival fixture Hong Sang-soo, a master of minimalist, conversational works that mine true poetry from everyday exchanges. In his 34th feature – anchored by a beguiling turn from regular collaborator Song Seon-mi ( Walk Up , MIFF 2023) – Hong interrogates the various modes of performance: not only the craft of acting, but also the ways in which we modify our self-presentation for different contexts.

films.title: Intersecting Memory
films.synopsis: Personal memories and collective trauma, home video and news footage intertwine in a powerful portrait of a Palestinian childhood under occupation
films.description: Personal memories and collective trauma, home video and news footage intertwine in a powerful portrait of a Palestinian childhood under occupation. Shayma’ was six when the Second Intifada erupted in 2000. In voiceover addressing her mother, she recalls crisp school uniforms and birthday cakes over archival footage of endless checkpoints, tanks blocking Hebron streets and children moving through carnage and destruction. These harrowing images – reminiscent of those live-streamed today – are captured with disturbing calm, underscoring the humanity we lose when grave violence comes to seem banal.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.74/5；纪录片 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、成长与青春、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: The Education of Jane Cumming
films.synopsis: A student accuses her teachers of a same-sex affair in this luminous drama based on an early-19th-century case that scandalised Scotland’s justice system.
films.description: Edinburgh, 1810: wealthy aristocrat Lady Cumming Gordon arrives at a new girls’ boarding school, wishing to enrol her illegitimate teenage granddaughter Jane. When Jane – born to an Indian mother – is bullied by her peers for her dark skin, progressive teachers Miss Pirie and Miss Woods take her under their wings. But the close bond that develops leads to complicated feelings of jealousy, and a shift in the trio’s dynamic instigates an accusation by Jane that will alter the course of the teachers’ lives. This dramatisation of the true story that inspired Lillian Hellman's famous play The Children's Hour – which in turn was adapted into the 1961 William Wyler film of the same name starring Audrey Hepburn and Shirley MacLaine – deals frankly with the layers of intersectional power that impacted the real-life events behind it. Up-and-comer Mia Tharia (also starring in Taika Waititi’s upcoming adaptation of Kazuo Ishiguro’s Klara and the Sun ) gives a deftly nuanced performance as Jane, while Fiona Shaw is in typically fine form as the acerbic and haughty Lady Cumming Gordon. Aftersun (MIFF 2022) fans will also be delighted to spot Frankie Corio in a supporting role.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.52/5；剧情 / 欧洲与英国 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、成长与青春、政治与社会现实。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: The Sun Never Sets
films.synopsis: A radiant Dakota Fanning anchors US indie cinema luminary Joe Swanberg’s long-awaited return: a canny and heartfelt love-triangle dramedy set in Alaska.
films.description: Wendy (Dakota Fanning, Once Upon a Time in Hollywood , MIFF 2019) is 31 years old and happily in love with Jack (Jake Johnson, Safety Not Guaranteed , MIFF 2012), a divorcee in his 40s. The only snag in their age-gap relationship: she wants to have children, but he doesn’t want more than the two he already has; he’s also leery of getting married again. Hoping to inspire her to outgrow those particular urges, Jack proposes a six-month break in which she might dive into the Anchorage dating pool. What he hasn’t accounted for is the reappearance of Wendy’s handsome ex, a pilot who may prove less flighty than he once was. The Sun Never Sets marks the first full-length feature in nearly a decade by Joe Swanberg ( Drinking Buddies , MIFF 2013), an auteur who helped to forge the mumblecore lexicon – and launch the acting career of Greta Gerwig – with such heavily improvised and incredibly frank flicks as Hannah Takes the Stairs and Nights and Weekends . Though suffused with the same bracing candour and wit as those earlier, lo-fi ventures, Swanberg’s latest is captured on handsome 35mm film.

films.title: Please
films.synopsis: Charming vignettes explore the full awkwardness, absurdity and disappointment of humankind’s yearning to be loved.
films.description: In a series of darkly comedic stop-motion scenes, Winston (Stellan Skarsgård, Sentimental Value ) and other urbanites search for love while the city burns behind them. Even as the characters strive to conceal their needy and pathetic sides, their soft felt bodies are as enchanting as their beautifully detailed clothing and everyday surroundings. Loosely overlapping misadventures gradually form a tender and absurd little portrait of collective longing.

films.title: Past Future Continuous
films.synopsis: This layered, IDFA-awarded portrait of an Iranian family long divided by geography but reunited by technology exudes a keen and haunting resonance.
films.description: Aged 20, Maryam fled Iran in the aftermath of the 1979 revolution, crossing the border into Türkiye camouflaged among a flock of sheep. Now a political exile, she resides in the United States, while her parents still live in the house that she grew up in, which her architect father designed. Longing to somehow bridge the distance, Maryam – herself unseen in the film – has surveillance cameras installed in their home: together with super-8 home videos and archival materials, this dialogue-free footage of her ageing parents, moving around the house or at times performing for the camera, illustrates her reflective voiceover. Morteza Ahmadvand and Firouzeh Khosrovani previously collaborated on Radiograph of a Family (MIFF 2021), in which Khosrovani explored the impacts of the Iranian Revolution through the prism of her own family history. Past Future Continuous , however, gradually reveals itself to be something more complex than straight documentary, provocatively slipping between historical fact and narrative fabrication – in the process, gesturing towards the tragic broader relevance of Maryam’s story of displacement to families in today’s Iran.

films.title: Pressure
films.synopsis: Hours before D-Day, General Eisenhower’s chief meteorologist warns of brutal weather – but doesn’t predict the stormy debate he now faces to save Allied lives.
films.description: In 72 hours, Allied forces will launch a mass invasion of Normandy to recapture Europe from the Nazis. Newly arrived at HQ, Scottish meteorologist Captain James Stagg (Andrew Scott, Blue Moon , MIFF 2025) begs for a delay. His pioneering real-time weather observations are predicting heavy rain and high winds that will swamp boats or drive them calamitously off course. Ebullient US weatherman Irving P. Krick (Chris Messina, Call Jane , MIFF 2022), on the other hand, says Stagg’s just doomcasting, and that historical barometric charts indicate it’ll be positively balmy – which is just what arrogant British commander Bernard “Monty” Montgomery (Damian Lewis, Homeland ) wants to hear. But the fate of the war, and the world, rests on whom General Dwight D. Eisenhower (Brendan Fraser, The Whale ) chooses to trust. Australian director Anthony Maras ( Hotel Mumbai ) dramatises this underappreciated historical turning point with moment-by-moment urgency, co-writing a grippingly cinematic adaptation of David Haig’s 2014 stage play. The always-subtle Scott mesmerises as a decent, plain-speaking expert caught up in the high pressure system of a long-planned military operation. Meanwhile, rain-soaked carnage on beach battlefields and weather-buffeted balloons over Newfoundland reveal the elemental, existential stakes compelling one man to dissuade his complacent colleagues from disaster.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.51/5；剧情、惊悚、Historical / 主打佳片 / 维州首映。推荐理由：政治与社会现实。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Replica
films.synopsis: Like a gender-flipped Her , this empathetic documentary follows three Chinese women whose yearning for intimacy is met by their AI partners.
films.description: Disillusioned with her romantic and professional prospects, Qin embarks on a relationship with Mr Lu, a customisable AI lover who accompanies her everywhere – including (with help from a paid cosplayer) on physical dates. Pragmatic Sonya, meanwhile, is overwhelmed by big-city life and finds solace in Stephen, her virtual husband, who has even met her parents. And Muna, whose marriage has long gone sour, gets the devotion she seeks through a simulated companion on ChatGPT. Are these digital bonds just about escapism and desperation, or are they legitimate surrogates when real life falls short? Informed by her own experience of developing feelings for a chatbot, VCA grad Chouwa Liang’s jointly Australian-produced feature directorial debut expands her short documentary My AI Lover to offer a timely social portrait about technology, fantasy and connection. Focusing on what technology can reveal about “the human desire to be loved”, the film uses the backdrop of modern China – gender inequality, high-pressure work environments and the burgeoning love economy explored in other docs such as Mistress Dispeller (MIFF 2025) – to sensitively contextualise the women’s unorthodox approaches to romance.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.50/5；纪录片、爱情 / 澳洲电影 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、亲密关系、音乐与表演、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Shades of Silk
films.synopsis: Long out of circulation, this evocatively elliptical tale of Sapphic yearning in 1930s Shanghai – newly restored – has been hailed as a major rediscovery.
films.description: Lysanne and Marlène (the latter played by writer/director Mary Stephen herself) were best friends in high school; perhaps, even then, their bond was underpinned by a desire for and fear of greater intimacy. Time has since separated the two women – both originally from Shanghai, Lysanne is now living in Paris and Marlène in Massachusetts, where she’s studying at Wellesley – but they pine for one another still, each in their own way and to tragic ends. Stephen’s darkly dreamy and remarkably assured debut – made when she was just 24 years old – was conceived as a loving counterpoint to Marguerite Duras’s India Song , with her similar cross-cultural narrative instead unfolding from an Asian woman’s perspective. For decades, the Hong Kong–born Stephen has primarily been known for her work as an editor, but the 2025 restoration of this 1978 treasure has stirred up overdue appreciation for her directorial oeuvre, spanning fiction and documentary and characterised by an exploration of colonial identity and cultural memory.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.50/5；剧情 / 经典修复 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：成长与青春、亲密关系、政治与社会现实、记忆与历史、音乐与表演、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: The Only Living Pickpocket in New York
films.synopsis: John Turturro stars as a pavement-pounding old-school thief who needs all his Big Apple savvy to save his wife – and his way of life – from a young crypto gangster.
films.description: Harry is a veteran fingersmith with a heart of pilfered gold, and equally collegial relationships with his long-time fence Ben (Steve Buscemi) and NYPD detective Allan (Giancarlo Esposito). By day, he nimbly relieves unsuspecting New Yorkers of their valuables; by night, he cares for his ailing wife Rosie in their modest Bronx apartment. Fatefully, Harry picks the wrong mark: the random data card he’s swiped is a crypto wallet belonging to petulant crime family scion Dylan Diamond, whose goons threaten Rosie while Harry scours all five boroughs for the lost trinket. Can his bone-deep bond with his city survive the crude transactions of digital crime, or is New York about to have no living pickpockets? Actor-turned-director Noah Segan’s charming old-fashioned crime drama steals its vibe from the pockets of cinema’s 1970s and 1980s New York storytellers: the perfectly cast John Turturro roams the New York of John Cassavetes and Spike Lee, Robert Altman and Elaine May. The subtle, charismatic veteran of the screen owns this elegiac film, which doesn’t resist progress or ridicule digital natives but rather celebrates the evergreen value in human connections and well-honed skills.

films.title: A Shot at Art
films.synopsis: Rogue retired female art festival volunteers take aim at a provocative phallic artwork – and the patriarchy it pretends to critique.
films.description: Volunteers Helen and Peggy are scanning tickets outside an installation inviting visitors to shoot paintball pellets at an exhibited appendage. But a lifetime’s worth of patriarchal bullshit has these two older women on a hair-trigger … and when they go rogue, the art world’s not ready. This wicked satire will make sure you’re extra nice to every lovely MIFF volunteer.

films.title: I Shot Andy Warhol
films.synopsis: In its Australian-premiering 4K restoration, Mary Harron’s fictionalised portrait of radical-feminist avenger Valerie Solanas still aims truly at manufactured cool and exploitative notoriety.
films.description: On 3 June 1968, writer Valerie Solanas (Lili Taylor) walks into Andy Warhol’s Factory, points a handgun at the art superstar and fires three times. Previously, Valerie has been introduced – along with her notorious man-hating polemic, the SCUM Manifesto – to the Factory demimonde. She sees fellow iconoclasts; Warhol sees satirical performance art; Olympia Press publisher Maurice Girodias sees profitable controversy. But Valerie’s abusive past has made her both urgently ambitious and hyper-alert to exploitation, and she suspects that Girodias has been conspiring against her with Warhol – who becomes, in her eyes, a valid target for her rage. Many debates about gender and celebrity have raged since Mary Harron’s astonishing debut feature screened at MIFF 1996, but the complex agency the film grants its protagonist still blows most biopics away. Harron meticulously researched Warhol’s Factory in consultation with its key living figures; but rather than further mythologising its glamour, she emphasises the hollowness of its artifice – a theme she’d revisit in her stylish American Psycho (MIFF 2000). Featuring a score by avant-garde legend John Cale and a stunning cast including Jared Harris, Martha Plimpton and Michael Imperioli – as well as indie band Yo La Tengo standing in for The Velvet Underground – I Shot Andy Warhol returns after a long absence from screens as a vivid, timely provocation.
films.awards: In its Australian-premiering 4K restoration, Mary Harron’s fictionalised portrait of radical-feminist avenger Valerie Solanas still aims truly at manufactured cool and exploitative notoriety.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.48/5；剧情、喜剧、惊悚 / 经典修复 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：政治与社会现实、记忆与历史、音乐与表演、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：首映

films.title: Rose of Nevada
films.synopsis: A Cornish seaside town mysteriously returns to life in analog auteur Mark Jenkin’s lo-fi sci-fi meditation on memory starring George MacKay and Callum Turner.
films.description: Thirty years ago, fishing trawler the Rose of Nevada vanished off the coast of Cornwall, taking its crew with it. Their village has been in decline ever since. When the boat suddenly reappears out of the fog one day, many townsfolk regard it as a good omen. A new crew is hired and heads out to sea. After a few days, they return with a full catch … but their home isn’t quite as they remember it. After the folk-horror stylings of Enys Men (MIFF 2022), Mark Jenkin’s Rose of Nevada returns audiences to the social realism of the Cornish fishing diaspora so evocatively portrayed in his debut Bait (MIFF 2019), albeit with a preternatural twist. Shooting in 16mm on vintage Bolex cameras, Jenkin uses visual and aural textures as storytelling devices, immersing viewers in deliberately uncanny realities and time loops. George MacKay and Callum Turner lend star power, but the film remains distinctly Jenkin’s vision: a cinematic artefact that looks like an oil painting come to life and unfolds like a 1970s ghost story.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.48/5；剧情 / 欧洲与英国 / 维州首映。推荐理由：记忆与历史、恐怖 / 超自然。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Broken English
films.synopsis: From chart-topper to tabloid fodder and back again, the legendary British rock balladeer Marianne Faithfull reflects candidly on her storied past.
films.description: Plucked from obscurity by the manager of The Rolling Stones, Marianne Faithfull had her first hit in 1964 with ‘ As Tears Go By ’. She was 17. Hers would be a monumental career spanning six decades, punctuated by both high-profile love affairs and highly publicised struggles with drug abuse, anorexia and homelessness. But, for all its wealth of archival materials, Broken English is no conventional rock doc. Here, in a playful but sincere twist, the husky-voiced chanteuse is interviewed by a record keeper (George MacKay, Rose of Nevada , MIFF 2026) at the fictional Ministry of Not Forgetting – a bureaucratic institution overseen by none other than Tilda Swinton – while luminaries such as Courtney Love and Beth Orton chime in with their own reflections. Directors Iain Forsyth and Jane Pollard have specialised in immersive, form-defying documentaries built around musicians, from Nick Cave in 20,000 Days on Earth (MIFF 2014) to Emiliana Torrini in The Extraordinary Miss Flower (MIFF 2025). This inventive portrait of a legendary singer-songwriter is suffused with a particular, unanticipated pathos: Faithfull died in January 2025 while the film was still in production, making the rendition we see of her 2018 track ‘ Misunderstanding ’ – accompanied by Cave and Warren Ellis – her final recorded performance.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.46/5；纪录片、音乐 / 音乐电影 / 维州首映。推荐理由：亲密关系、政治与社会现实、记忆与历史、音乐与表演、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Knife: The Attempted Murder of Salman Rushdie
films.synopsis: A religious zealot stabbed him 15 times. But the outspoken novelist survived – and, as this documentary shows, retained his belief that creative freedom is the sharpest weapon.
films.description: In 1989, an Iranian fatwa called for author Salman Rushdie’s death over his theologically provocative novel The Satanic Verses . Booksellers were firebombed and international publishers and translators attacked, even killed. Rushdie went into hiding … but his eventual assailant hadn’t yet been born. In 2022, with more than three decades having passed and the danger seemingly over, the 75-year-old author was speaking on exiled artists’ safety in a relaxed, informal event in upstate New York when a young Shia extremist invaded the stage and stabbed him repeatedly. Despite the horrendous, life-changing injuries he endured, Rushdie refused to become a tragic symbol of ideological violence. Now, doubling down on his commitment to free expression, he documents his own survival. Acclaimed documentarian Alex Gibney ( Zero Days , MIFF 2016) has profiled plenty of creative mavericks, political dissidents and repressive institutions. Here, he allows his subject’s clever, poetic voice to lead an unexpectedly meditative and allusive film that offers deep archival context for the home-video footage of the attack’s aftermath and the arduous recovery that followed, captured with unflinching love by the author’s poet wife Rachel Eliza Griffiths. Physically weakened and spiritually fragile, Rushdie nonetheless takes back his narrative, showing that resistance against authoritarian censorship is sharpened by wit, imagination and a defiantly imperfect humanity.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.44/5；纪录片 / 纪录片单元 / 维州首映。推荐理由：亲密关系、政治与社会现实、失去与哀悼、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Fantasma
films.synopsis: Reuniting the two ‘lonely men’ from Los muertos and La libertad , Lisandro Alonso’s third film wanders lost through the halls of a run-down movie house.
films.description: Argentino Vargas, the star of Los muertos , has arrived in Buenos Aires to attend a retrospective screening of the film at the grand Teatro General San Martín. Out of place and uneasy in the city, he soon gets lost within the cinema — a movie palace that, having fallen into disarray, is full of shadows and secrets. As he drifts through the labyrinthine building, he crosses paths with Misael Saavedra, the central figure of La libertad . Here, the two solitary subjects at the centre of Lisandro Alonso’s first two films are together yet apart, unable to find their place within the cinema. Alonso calls his first three movies his ‘Lonely Men Trilogy’. Inspired by Tsai Ming-liang’s Goodbye, Dragon Inn (MIFF 2004), this unifying conclusion to the triptych is a self-reflexive exploration of the filmmaker’s own work, and particularly on how he took these rural non-actors and turned them into cinematic subjects. With little dialogue or narrative, Fantasma is built from evocative tracking shots and ultra-detailed sound design, full of strange elements such as the unexplained presence of a barking dog. As always, there is mystery in the film’s intention and its title. Is this a portrait of being haunted by the spectre of cinema itself? Or are each of these lonely men lost spirits, fated to forever live on in Alonso’s movies?

films.title: Sentient
films.synopsis: In his directorial debut, veteran Australian journalist Tony Jones explores the moral, emotional and clinical questions – as well as answers – around why we still test on animals, and what it will take us to stop
films.description: In his directorial debut, veteran Australian journalist Tony Jones explores the moral, emotional and clinical questions – as well as answers – around why we still test on animals, and what it will take us to stop. Dr Lisa Jones-Engel is a passionate primatologist who reflects, with a mix of sadness and horror, on her past work at the Laboratory for Experimental Medicine and Surgery in Primates. It was enough to turn her from animal tester to animal welfare advocate, as contemporary and archival footage – both personal and professional – relates. Through the perspectives of other frontline scientists discussing the fraught balance of human needs and animal rights, one compelling fact emerges: animal testing does psychological harm not just to its physical victims, but also to its human practitioners.

films.title: David Bowie Is Dead
films.synopsis: An Ektachrome city symphony that captures a London lost to the quicksand of time
films.description: An Ektachrome city symphony that captures a London lost to the quicksand of time. The day after David Bowie’s death in 2016, Mark Jenkin took to the streets of London to film a city in flux, watching on as decades of so-called progress appeared to collapse in on one another. In the resulting 2018 short, Jenkin narrates the footage with excerpts from his diary, turning a black-and-white dream into an ecstatic Beat poem.

films.title: Think Good
films.synopsis: Grounded by authentic performances, Think Good is a deft psychological thriller about the insidiousness of domestic abuse
films.description: Grounded by authentic performances, Think Good is a deft psychological thriller about the insidiousness of domestic abuse. Gil and Jacques meet young, fall head over heels and then marry quickly thanks to an unexpected pregnancy. But as the dust settles, Gil begins to understand that her relationship is dangerously toxic. Jacques is possessive and controlling: he weaponises their faith against her; isolates her from her friends and family; spies on her; and repeatedly gaslights her. As the threat of physical violence escalates, Gil realises that escape may be the only way to save her life.

films.title: Nadja
films.synopsis: Droll and (un)deadpan, this stylish David Lynch–produced 90s indie curio – newly 4K-restored – follows an elegant vampire stalking Manhattan by night.
films.description: The city that never sleeps suits Nadja, a Romanian migrant nepo vampire who spends her nights drinking coffee, smoking cigarettes and waxing philosophical in East Village dive bars alongside fellow ennui-afflicted New York scenesters. When her dad, Count Dracula, dies from a nasty case of stake-in-the-heart, Nadja finally has a chance to emerge from her father’s long, overbearing shadow. But before she can make a fresh start, a twist of fate romantically entangles Nadja with Lucy – an extended relative of her father’s murderer, Van Helsing. Owing as much to André Breton’s 1928 novel of the same name as it does to 1936 horror classic Dracula’s Daughter, this magnetically cool black comedy by Michael Almereyda ( Marjorie Prime , MIFF 2017) is shot on luminous monochrome 35mm with shimmering vampire POV sequences captured by a toy Pixelvision camcorder. The soundtrack, featuring My Bloody Valentine, Portishead and The Verve, is an education in 90s shoegaze; while the cast includes Hal Hartley regulars Elina Löwensohn and Martin Donovan, Peter Fonda as Van Helsing, and even the late, great David Lynch (one of the film’s key financiers) making a cameo as a morgue attendant, naturally.

films.title: A Dog Called Discord
films.synopsis: Super-8 images of a little white dog on a bleak northern beach are resurrected through the magic of film
films.description: Super-8 images of a little white dog on a bleak northern beach are resurrected through the magic of film. Commissioned by the BFI for the Film on Film Festival in 2023, Mark Jenkin’s playful short began as a latent image in a two-decade-old super-8 cartridge, in which the titular pet scampers along a grim, windswept shoreline. A moving meditation on the power of analog filmmaking, as light, chemicals and sprockets conspire to bring life to a long-gone companion.

films.title: Bell, Book and Candle
films.synopsis: In a stand-out performance, Kim Novak has a bewitching good time toying with Jimmy Stewart’s clueless mortal.
films.description: Among the jazz bars and beatnik scene of 1960s Greenwich Village resides a thriving community of witches and warlocks. Gillian Holroyd (Kim Novak), one of the most powerful among them, lives behind the shop she runs selling African artefacts, while her aimless sorcerer brother (Jack Lemmon, The Apartment ) plays bongos at a local club and her trickster aunt (played by a delightful Elsa Lanchester, Bride of Frankenstein ) keeps tabs on her from the apartment above. When mild-mannered publisher Shep Henderson (James Stewart) moves into the building, she decides on an impulse to playfully seduce him with the help of her familiar, a cat named Pyewacket. What she doesn’t quite bargain for is the very real emotion that ensues. Re-teaming with her Vertigo co-star Stewart, Novak gives one of the most natural and lived-in performances of her career. Her innate charm and vulnerability are a perfect fit for Gillian’s seemingly elusive witch, and she brings genuine emotional gravitas, through which you can really feel her struggle with letting her guard down. Beneath its charm and whimsy, Bell, Book and Candle offers a sly meditation on conformity, desire and the vulnerability required to truly connect with another person.

films.title: Erupcja
films.synopsis: Charli xcx stars as a British tourist crisscrossing Warsaw with an old friend in this effervescent indie that’s equally roiling with emotion.
films.description: Bethany (pop music icon Charli xcx) is a London gal who’s arrived in Warsaw with her boyfriend Rob. He believes he’s in for a romantic getaway culminating in a picturesque marriage proposal, but Bethany has other ideas. She wastes no time peeling off to track down an old friend, Nel, who runs a flower shop and with whom she shares a uniquely electric connection. Through the lens of their mutual infatuation – teetering on romance – the city shimmers with party-girl possibility. Fresh from her star turn in The Moment , a deglammed Charli proves a magnetic screen presence; Erupcja is similarly buoyed by vivid performances from Will Madden, Lena Góra and Jeremy O. Harris ( The Sweet East , MIFF 2023) as Claude, an artist friend of Nel’s who takes on the role of Rob’s bemused but kindly babysitter. In shaping the project together with his cast, US-born Warsaw-based director Pete Ohs has created an intimate, bittersweet paean to his adoptive home – a film that bubbles with understated eruptive potential.

films.title: All The Lovers In The Night
films.synopsis: Based on Mieko Kawakami’s evocative novel, this tale of lost souls finding unexpected connection enchantingly explores loneliness and love.
films.description: Fuyuko is a professional proofreader, a solitary person of quiet routines. She rarely leaves the house, but loves taking walks late at night, when only “half the world remains”. One evening, she meets Mitsutsuka, a soft-spoken physics lecturer who’s just as reticent and fastidious as she is. Soon, they start taking walks together, sitting in cafés and talking the night away – two lonely satellites briefly, tenderly brought into shared orbit. But can their relationship survive the cold light of day? Adapting the book of the same name by Mieko Kawakami (author of the hit novel Breasts and Eggs ), Yukiko Sode takes on a gentle, beautiful story grounded in interior experience, delicately hinting at the difficulty of connecting with others – and the preciousness of the rare moments when it occurs. Starring prolific big- and small-screen Japanese actors Yukino Kishii and Tadanobu Asano (here taking on a relatively subdued role), this lovely two-hander is a film that dwells on conversation and the spaces lurking between the words we say to one another.

films.synopsis: An unexpected rainstorm on a summer day sparks moments of inspiration and connection among neighbours in an apartment building.

films.title: The Enchantment of the Blue Sailors
films.synopsis: With glitter, feathers and gender-bending sailors, Ulrike Ottinger’s rarely screened early experimental film sets the scene for her work to come.
films.description: An exotic sea laps upon the sands of a distant beach, and a woman in an extravagant red gown and matching headdress crosses the dunes, singing. Gay sailors in classic white-and-blue uniforms kiss (years before French artists Pierre et Gilles made such imagery iconic). A woman in a leopard-print party dress fringed with pink, cake-like layers settles in for a solo picnic, while another woman in full-feathered bird regalia prowls nearby, her tinselled wings shimmering in the light. Scantily clad characters cavort and canoodle with bacchanalian pleasure. An assemblage of vivid imagery, The Enchantment of the Blue Sailors was Ulrike Ottinger’s second mid-length experimental film, following Laocoon & Sons: The Story of the Transformation of Esmeralda del Rio (1972/73), and her first in riotous colour. Notably, it is the only work in Ottinger’s filmography that she shares a directorial credit on: joining her at the helm here is her partner and frequent collaborator Tabea Blumenschein, who also oversaw the film’s costumes and make-up. Both women appear onscreen, with Ottinger playing the “Nymph of German Romanticism” alongside influential queer filmmaker Rosa von Praunheim ( It Is Not the Homosexual Who Is Perverse, but the Society in Which He Lives ) as one of the titular sailors.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.40/5；实验 / Ulrike Ottinger: Radical Travels。推荐理由：导演表达与人物处境。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Can She Bake a Cherry Pie?
films.synopsis: Recently restored to 4K, this underseen odd-couple romance is a sweet slice of 1980s New York life featuring cameos from Larry David and Orson Welles.
films.description: After her husband unceremoniously leaves her one morning, Zee heads out to wander the streets of the Upper West Side in a daze, muttering to herself about what she could have done differently. When she stops at Café Central to eat her feelings with a big bowl of chocolate ice cream, Zee’s loud sobs are noticed by Eli, a fellow divorcee, sitting at the next table. Before long, the two strangers develop a distinctly neurotic New York–style case of amour fou , philosophising, bickering and engaging in a courtship characterised by acts of mutual wallowing. This dryly funny romance from iconoclastic American independent filmmaker Henry Jaglom (who died just last September) is an invaluable time capsule of 1980s New York. Filmed on the streets among real pedestrians, its freewheeling style lends the film a refreshing spontaneity, enhanced by the wonderfully quirky central performances by Karen Black ( Five Easy Pieces ) and the director’s brother Michael Emil ( Insignificance , MIFF 1985), as well as memorable cameos from Carol Kane, a young Larry David and Orson Welles – Jaglom’s friend and business partner – as a movie magician.

films.title: Body Blow
films.synopsis: A disgraced, sex-addicted cop is thrust into Sydney’s crime-ridden gay district – and into a raunchy entanglement – in this neon-soaked queer erotic thriller.
films.description: Battling both sex addiction and sexual misconduct allegations, twentysomething undercover cop Aiden (Tim Pocock, Godless: The Eastfield Exorcism , MIFF 2023) is reassigned to an inner-city precinct among Sydney’s nightclubs, bathhouses and drag venues. While investigating, he hooks up with hot bartender and sex worker Cody (Tom Rodgers) – a chance encounter that also gets him embroiled in the machinations of drag mob boss Fat Frankie (Paul Capsis, Head On , MIFF 1998). Caught up in drug deals, shootouts, a deadly heist and more, Aiden must find a way out before he loses himself in this sinister web. Set mostly around Sydney’s Oxford Street, Dean Francis’s ( Drown ) intriguing tale of danger and desire transports the audience to an intricate underworld of crime, conspiracy and duplicitous allegiances. Taking visual cues from Brian De Palma and Gregg Araki, Body Blow is a stunning lovechild of classic film noir and 1990s erotic thrillers, infusing its gripping tale of (anti)heroism and redemption with a playful critique of hyper-masculinity and deprivation. Pocock delivers a formidable lead performance, embodying Aiden’s pent-up lust and dubiously masked fragility, while newcomer Rodgers is beguiling as the film’s seductive spin on the femme fatale.

films.title: Boundaries
films.synopsis: An annoyingly selfish public-transport passenger provokes an imaginative, strikingly tactile reflection on the space we owe one another.
films.description: When a person on a bus reclines their seat too far into a young woman’s lap, she pushes back, provoking a more surreal and expansive meditation on how we intrude upon and defend both physical and territorial boundaries, individually and collectively. This clever blend of hand-drawn linework and stop-motion paper cut-outs uses its tactile textures to express subtly provocative ideas.

films.title: Once in a Body
films.synopsis: Stunning, expressionist oil-painted visuals express how it feels to carry emotions so heavy they take on a life of their own.
films.description: A woman manifests the emotional burden she carries as a strange creature that burrows into her body, distorting her own sense of self. Through remembered adolescent encounters with her sister, she confronts her guilt-gremlin in order to understand and free herself. Boldly experimenting with how to depict interiority, this film’s astonishingly fluid oil-painted brushstrokes swirl and pulse with inner life.

films.title: Eureka
films.synopsis: Alonso’s hugely ambitious triptych explores the collision between Indigenous lives and colonisation.
films.description: Nineteenth-century Old West gunslinger Murphy (Viggo Mortensen), searching for his missing daughter, arrives in a lawless outpost, where the only justice to be found is by way of the gun. In the contemporary United States, Alaina (played by actual police officer Alaina Clifford) is a local cop at snowy South Dakota reservation Pine Ridge, who has her work cut out keeping an eye on colourful locals, crime at a casino and her disaffected teenage niece. Lastly, amid the Amazon gold rush of 1970s Brazil, two young men from a remote tribe fight over a girl – and one of them ends up dead. Stitched together by the migratory flight of a jabiru and the shapeshifting presence of a character called El Coronel (Chiara Mastroianni), Alonso’s ambitious sixth feature is a triptych tripping through eclectic genres and varying aspect ratios. The film’s opening act reunites him with his Jauja stars in a gunslinging riff drawing from old-timey oaters, John Ford’s The Searchers (MIFF 1994) and Jim Jarmusch’s Dead Man ; its second section feels like familiar Alonso minimalism steeped in Twin Peaks –style uncanniness and shot through with pop colour; while its third offers a subversive riff on ethnographic cinema. Throughout, Eureka ’s thesis emerges: a commentary on the intersection of Indigenous cultures and colonial power across history, nations and modes of storytelling.

films.title: Gentle Monster

films.description: Léa Seydoux is riveting as a wife and mother coming to grips with the emotional, moral and practical fallout when her husband is charged with a shocking crime. When avant-garde concert pianist Lucy agrees to move her family from the city to the countryside, the goal is to provide her husband Philip with respite following a burnout-induced panic attack. Instead, the move leaves her isolated and emotionally unprepared when the police arrive to execute a search warrant on Philip’s computers. As she struggles to process her shattered new reality and to protect herself and her young son, Lucy must untangle truth from love.

films.title: Victorian Psycho
films.synopsis: Scream queen Maika Monroe plays an unhinged governess terrorising a posh 19th-century family.
films.description: In 1858, Winifred Notty arrives on the Gothic doorstep of Ensor House, a mansion on the Yorkshire moors. Wealthy John and Emily Pounds hire the well-spoken but definitely odd woman as a governess for their young son and daughter. Courtesy of an inner demon she calls “Fred”, the impulsive carnage that’s always followed in Winifred’s wake doesn’t take long to pop up gruesomely again. Given the Poundses’ own depraved lineage, has Winifred finally found her people? But, then again, she is a very unreliable narrator … Zachary Wigon’s gory Gothic romp – adapted by Virginia Feito from her own novel – is Wuthering Heights slash Jane Eyre slash American Psycho . Demolishing her final-girl persona, Maika Monroe ( It Follows , MIFF 2014; Longlegs ), exploits eat-the-rich sentiments as her gleeful sociopath, aided by Ruth Wilson ( Dark River , MIFF 2018) and Jason Isaacs ( The White Lotus ) as Winifred’s loathsome employers. There are also fun supporting performances from Thomasin McKenzie ( The Justice of Bunny King , MIFF 2021) as the nursemaid who befriends Winifred and Jacobi Jupe ( Hamnet ) and Evie Templeton ( Wednesday ) as the Pounds children. What are they learning from their super-callous, pugilistic, exterminatory governess?

films.title: Madame X: An Absolute Ruler
films.synopsis: Gold. Love. Adventure. Tabea Blumenschein stars as the leather-clad lesbian pirate Madame X, who promises a new way of life to the brave women who dare to join her crew.
films.description: The most successful and cruellest pirate on the Far Eastern Seas, Madame X (Tabea Blumenschein) rules her junk Orlando with a gloved iron fist from which extends a long spike, having lost her real hand to a deadly jellyfish years ago while attempting to save her lover (played by Ulrike Ottinger herself in flashback). Now in desire of “total domination”, she sends out a signal to women across the globe – including an Australian bush pilot with a wildly shorn haircut and Yvonne Rainer in a green visor and rollerskates – inviting them to join her for a life of excitement. Journeying from all corners, they leave their ordinary lives behind in search of something new; but no-one expects the tyranny that awaits. Shot on 16mm, Ottinger’s first scripted feature is a swashbuckling sadomasochistic reverie that caused much controversy for its brightly coloured, often humorous, depictions of queer violence. With its fetishistic costuming designed by Blumenschein (the director’s romantic partner and collaborator at the time) and wild aesthetic, Madame X was a call to arms for a new avant-garde; yet it still pays homage to its queer predecessors, with references to Virginia Woolf and a Sapphic take on the Shangri-Las’ 1964 pop hit ‘Leader of the Pack’ .

films.title: Twelve Tales Told
films.synopsis: Hollywood plays itself in this cheeky deconstruction of iconic studio idents
films.description: Hollywood plays itself in this cheeky deconstruction of iconic studio idents. At once an affectionate ode to Hollywood studios and a playful subversion of global hyper-capitalism, Johann Lurf’s wickedly amusing 2014 short slices and dices pre-feature idents from the familiar likes of Universal, Disney, Paramount and Warner Bros. until they become a glitching symphony of chaos. Seen from the vantage point of the big studio mergers a decade later, Twelve Tales Told becomes a poignant requiem for a once-mighty cinematic model.

Ricardo Rifo has farmed peacefully for decades, unable to speak of his apprenticeship to Íngrid Olderöck, the feared Chilean secret-police enforcer who sadistically tortured the dictatorship’s enemies using trained dogs. Filmed in raw black-and-white, Rifo and his own dogs wander a wildfire-devastated wasteland, whose bold textures speak eloquently of political atrocities, traumatic memories and ecological destruction.

films.title: Aro berria
films.synopsis: With ecstatic sequences reminiscent of Alejandro Jodorowsky’s The Holy Mountain , this complex portrait of new-age commune life is a striking feature debut.
films.description: It’s 1978, and Spanish society is struggling to find a post-dictatorship identity in the wake of Franco’s death. A host of activist workers from a Basque factory, disillusioned by the seeming inevitability of capitalism, attempt a complete rebellion, setting out to join a commune in rural Navarre. Eme (played by a magnetic Maite Mugerza Ronse) wants to believe that Arco Iris offers a new way of living, but she soon learns it’s just as political as a picket line: the commune is riddled with personality clashes and ideological disagreements, and its sexual practices are underpinned by patriarchal power imbalances. Despite what the thousands of pilgrims who flock to its large-scale spiritual rituals might think, this rainbow family isn’t particularly harmonious. Irati Gorostidi Agirretxe has been building to this debut feature through her short films, chronicling life within an intentional community in both documentary (2021’s San Simón 62 ) and fiction (2023’s Contadores ). The connection is personal: Gorostidi Agirretxe’s own parents lived in a new-age commune before she was born. Boasting a wryly comic cameo from the charismatic Oliver Laxe (director of MIFF 2025 stand-out Sirāt ) as a seedy tantric sex guru, her debut feature is a slippery film sitting at the intersection of sincerity and scepticism. Evoking both the daily routines of Direct Action (MIFF 2024) and the ecstatic rituals of The Testament of Ann Lee , Aro berria introduces a vital new voice in Basque cinema.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.07/5；剧情、实验 / 实验影像 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实、失去与哀悼、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Nagi Notes
films.synopsis: This adaptation of classic Japanese play Tokyo Notes is a thoughtful character study set in a sublime modern art museum.
films.description: Yuri, an architect from Tokyo, arrives in regional Nagi, a small town that’s home to both a magnificent art museum and an active military base. She’s there to visit her ex-husband’s sister, Yoriko, a sculptor who wants Yuri to sit for her. Staging sessions in a workshop barn on her dairy farm, Yoriko chips away at a block of camphorwood and at Yuri’s guardedness, their conversation turning personal. Two boys who attend Yoriko’s art classes and workshops, Keita and Haruki, are fascinated by this outsider, unsure of what the relationship between the two women is. As the quartet try to make sense of one another, much is left unsaid. Reminiscent of the films of Éric Rohmer and Ryusuke Hamaguchi ( All of a Sudden , MIFF 2026), and having earnt comparisons to Kelly Reichardt’s Showing Up (MIFF 2023) for its gentle depiction of artistry, this latest work from Koji Fukada ( Harmonium , MIFF 2016) adapts Oriza Hirata’s 1994 theatrical production Tokyo Notes (which itself drew from Yasujirō Ozu’s masterpiece Tokyo Story , MIFF 2016). Nagi Notes is a keenly observed character study with no real explosions – save for the sound of military munitions drills that periodically ring out through the town. Pondering the nature of creativity, queer identity and the challenges of finding belonging amid the social strictures of Japanese society, this is a thoughtful, low-key conversation piece that’s perfect for those who like their dramas intimate and deeply human.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 3.06/5；剧情 / 亚太单元 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、亲密关系、音乐与表演。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: The Legend of Lylah Clare
films.synopsis: Kim Novak once again tackles dual roles in this wildly unhinged satire of the Hollywood system and power in the film industry.
films.description: Lylah Clare is a mythical movie star whose death has long been shrouded in mystery. When unknown ingenue Elsa Brinkman (Kim Novak) is cast in the principal role in a biopic about the doomed thespian, she is almost immediately seduced by the heady Hollywood lifestyle, not to mention by tyrannical director Lewis Zarken (Peter Finch, Network ); she quickly assumes Lylah’s previous status on his arm, in his film and in his bed. Soon, the lines between reality and delusion begin to blur: the spirit of Lylah herself lays claim to Elsa, at times taking hold just long enough to deliver blistering take-downs of her adversaries. With echoes of her iconic role in Vertigo and references to her own transformation at the behest of Columbia Pictures head Harry Cohn, she plays the role with trademark empathy while embracing the inspired chaos of the piece. Robert Aldrich ( Kiss Me Deadly , MIFF 1998) directs with feverish intensity, transforming sound stages, mirrors and camera lenses into sites of psychological entrapment, while Novak delivers a haunting dual performance that blends innocence (quickly lost) and bravado. The Legend of Lylah Clare remains a savage, self-reflexive meditation on Hollywood’s capacity to consume the women it immortalises. Largely dismissed on release, it has since been reclaimed as a camp cult classic.

films.title: The Leader
films.synopsis: A remarkable Tim Blake Nelson and Vera Farmiga are out of this world as Heaven’s Gate co-founders Marshall Applewhite and Bonnie Lu Nettles
films.description: A remarkable Tim Blake Nelson and Vera Farmiga are out of this world as Heaven’s Gate co-founders Marshall Applewhite and Bonnie Lu Nettles. In 1972, Marshall Applewhite overdoses on a bottle of pills and ends up in hospital in the care of nurse Bonnie Lu Nettles, a spiritual seeker feeling empty in her personal life. Dubbing themselves “Do” and “Ti” as their sense of cosmic connection escalates into a Messianic bond, they manifest a doctrine that fuses biblical prophecy with new-age UFO utopianism and strict asexuality, drawing other broken souls into their orbit. Their goal: to leave their weak flesh behind and ascend to the stars. The reality of their Heaven’s Gate cult was grimmer: after Nettles died of cancer in 1985, Applewhite infamously staged a 1997 mass suicide together with 38 of his followers.

films.title: Sparks
films.synopsis: Small-town Nevadan teens attempt to time-travel to 1960s Paris in this charming debut that evokes the hangout spirit of the French New Wave.
films.description: After encountering an old cigarette machine that spits out a dog-eared paperback on the films of Jean-Luc Godard, teenager Cleo is convinced that her new hometown of Sparks, Nevada, could be magical. She quickly falls in with the Crop, a group of local teens with whom she immediately shares her newfound cinephilic obsession. When they tell Cleo about a nearby reservoir that – legend has it – contains a time travel portal, Cleo knows exactly where she wants to go: Paris, 1960, to act in a Godard movie. If only the group could figure out how to make the portal work … Recalling the early films of Richard Linklater, Gregg Araki and Wes Anderson, this coming-of-age fantasy drama blends an offbeat charm with a quietly rebellious energy. Writer/director Fergus Campbell’s debut feature conjures that familiar struggle between yearning to transcend the boundaries of small-town life and holding on to the all-too-fleeting freedom of youth. Compelling performances from Elsie Fisher ( Eighth Grade ; Christmas Eve in Miller’s Point , MIFF 2024) and Charlie B. Foster (son of Jodie) anchor this playful, tender tale that pays tribute to the power of cinema as its own form of time travel.

films.title: Rosebush Pruning

films.description: In a vast mansion in Catalonia, Ed feels stuck – not just between his older and younger brothers Jack and Robert, but also in the company of his sister Anna and their blind father. The family moved from New York at their late matriarch’s behest; now, their days are defined by sordid indulgence and isolation. One day, Jack brings his future bride Martha into the fold, disrupting established dynamics. In tight quarters and with everyone used to getting their way, things are bound to go off the rails. An A-list cast – including Callum Turner, Jamie Bell, Riley Keough, Elle Fanning, Tracy Letts and Pamela Anderson – expertly portray these characters’ escalating exploits in Karim Aïnouz’s ( Motel Destino , MIFF 2024) visually lavish, conceptually audacious domestic satire, a reimagining of Marco Bellocchio’s 1965 classic Fists in the Pocket . Scripted by Dogtooth (MIFF 2009) co-writer Efthimis Filippou and shot by recurring Aïnouz collaborator Hélène Louvart – whose lurid, eye-catching cinematography ramps up the intensity – this devious and provocative film depicts a world where the extremes of wealth and immorality are too close for comfort.

films.title: Forever…Forever
films.synopsis: Experience time on a planetary scale as rhythms of day and night pulsate in harmony with the celestial bodies in a landscape’s seasonal light
films.description: Experience time on a planetary scale as rhythms of day and night pulsate in harmony with the celestial bodies in a landscape’s seasonal light. Over a 22-month time lapse, a glassy Austrian lake reflects chasing clouds. Day and night set sky-rhythms for seasonal light pulses, from bright translucence to milky monochrome. Time accelerates to heartbeats, then eye blinks; sun and stars flash across the screen like exhilarating lightning strikes. In letting us glimpse infinite planetary time, Johann Lurf – the subject of a mini-retrospective within MIFF 2026’s special 3D shorts package Cinema of Spectacle: Experimental Visions – reminds us that we are the quick ones.

films.title: Firestorm
films.synopsis: World-premiering at MIFF, this generative-AI cinematic work explores the future of wildfires and the possibilities of immersive cinema.
films.description: As the world faces a probable super El Niño event in 2026/27, with wildfire conditions that may exceed those of 2019/20, picturing these unprecedented fires defies imagination. Using the latest scientific data, a custom machine-learning model and the Unreal Engine 3D-creation platform, Firestorm allows audiences to visualise historical Australian bushfires and then interactively adjust weather conditions to see how they would dramatically change under escalating global warming scenarios. Presented at the VCA, University of Melbourne, in a world-first conversion of an LED volume into a stereoscopic cinema, Firestorm fully immerses audiences in a panoramic fire landscape, allowing them to viscerally experience the look and feel of these possible futures firsthand.
films.awards: World-premiering at MIFF, this generative-AI cinematic work explores the future of wildfires and the possibilities of immersive cinema.

films.title: Coyotes
films.synopsis: A Palestinian woman confronts an existential threat on a deserted road to Ramallah.
films.description: Driving home from her night shift, Palestinian surgeon Lubna blows a tyre on a desolate stretch of West Bank road. When a man pulls over, her every instinct screams danger – and what she does next will change everything. This terrifying slow-burn thriller hits MIFF after scooping multiple festival awards, including Best Short Film at BFI London and a Special Jury Mention for Bravery in Storytelling at AFI Fest.
films.awards: This terrifying slow-burn thriller hits MIFF after scooping multiple festival awards, including Best Short Film at BFI London and a Special Jury Mention for Bravery in Storytelling at AFI Fest.
films.recommendation: Letterboxd 2.24/5；剧情、惊悚 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：政治与社会现实、悬疑 / 犯罪张力。奖项/展映线索：获奖

films.title: Ben’Imana
films.synopsis: This year’s Cannes Caméra d’Or winner is an unflinching, deeply felt portrait of a community seeking justice, and closure, in the wake of genocide.
films.description: It’s 2012 in Kibeho, a rural village in the mountains of south Rwanda. Though it’s been a generation since the 1994 genocide, people haven’t stopped seeking justice, and community-led Gacaca tribunals – more village assembly than formal trial – are ongoing. Vénéranda, who is deeply committed to the reconciliation process, has been asked to publicly forgive the man who killed most of her family, but her sister Suzanne is outraged at the suggestion. Meanwhile, Vénéranda has just learned with dismay that her daughter Tina is unexpectedly pregnant, news that complicates her commitment to healing and deepens her own reckoning with the past. A decade in the making, Marie-Clémentine Dusabejambo’s debut film is a humanist portrait of the legacy of genocide and the challenges of reconciliation. Working largely with non-professional actors and drawing deeply from interviews with victims and recorded Gacaca testimonies, it deftly evokes the difficulty of moving on as individuals and communities. Blessed with an incandescent turn from Isabelle Kabano as the seething Suzanne, this is a profoundly intimate story of a nation still reckoning with its past
films.awards: This year’s Cannes Caméra d’Or winner is an unflinching, deeply felt portrait of a community seeking justice, and closure, in the wake of genocide.
films.recommendation: 暂无 Letterboxd 评分；剧情、Historical / 非洲与中东 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实、记忆与历史、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：获奖、戛纳

films.title: Rehearsals for a Revolution
films.synopsis: Receiving the highest documentary prize at Cannes, this inspired essay movie channels the tumult of Iranian history into a personal account of loss.
films.description: Living in exile in the UK, actor/director Pegah Ahangarani ( I Am Trying to Remember , MIFF 2022) wants to make a film about Iranian history, as a way to understand how the country ended up where it is today. Fashioning home movies, archival imagery, news footage, animated sequences and sobering statistics into a singular cinematic essay, she chronicles conflict and rebellion across the decades, from 1979’s Islamic Revolution to the 2026 massacre of protesters and war with Israel and the United States. Ahangarani stages this history lesson in five chapters, each dedicated to a friend or family member she lost along the way, poignantly situating her film at the intersection of the personal and the political. Born in 1984 to filmmakers Manijeh Hekmat ( Bandar Band , MIFF 2021) and Jamshid Ahangarani, Pegah Ahangarani grew up intertwined with cinema and the post-revolution history of her homeland. Rehearsals for a Revolution is steeped in both, drawing on clips from her father’s old movies and mobile phone footage captured on the front lines of the 2009 Green Wave protests; but it also dwells in the here and now, intimately capturing scenes from Ahangarani’s current life in London with her newborn daughter. Winning the 2026 L’Œil d’or at Cannes, this documentary vividly interrogates what it means – and costs – to resist.
films.awards: Receiving the highest documentary prize at Cannes, this inspired essay movie channels the tumult of Iranian history into a personal account of loss. | Winning the 2026 L’Œil d’or at Cannes, this documentary vividly interrogates what it means – and costs – to resist.

films.title: The Friend’s House Is Here
films.synopsis: Both a charismatic hangout movie and a courageous act of dissent, this Sundance winner spends quality time with two young women trying to make art in Iran.
films.description: Hanna (Hana Mana) is a contemporary dancer who posts videos of her work online, while playwright Pari (Mahshad Bahram) stages experimental theatre productions. In Iran, both of these acts are illegal. Thick-as-thieves besties and housemates who participate in Tehran’s underground art communities, they live their lives – as artists, feminists and unwed women – in defiance of an authoritarian government that is openly hostile to everything they do. Armed with their friendship, their ideologies and a serious streak of rebellion, Hanna and Pari try to make art and make it through another day. Nearly four decades after Iranian cinema legend Abbas Kiarostami’s breakout 1987 film posed the eternal question Where Is the Friend’s House? , Hossein Keshavarz and Maryam Ataei deliver a playful answer. Inspired by classic Iranian art movies, Jean-Luc Godard’s political films of the 1960s and the US indie mumblecore movement, The Friend’s House Is Here is a movie about how to live your best, most creative life under a repressive regime. Shot in secret and smuggled out of Iran to make its Sundance premiere, the film went on to receive the festival’s U.S. Dramatic Special Jury Award for Ensemble Cast – a just reward for the non-professional actors (many themselves working artists) who appear in this film.
films.awards: Both a charismatic hangout movie and a courageous act of dissent, this Sundance winner spends quality time with two young women trying to make art in Iran. | Shot in secret and smuggled out of Iran to make its Sundance premiere, the film went on to receive the festival’s U.S.

films.title: Tilaï
films.synopsis: An adulterous love affair brings tragedy to a traditional village in Burkina Faso in Idrissa Ouedraogo’s masterful, Cannes-awarded fable, now restored in 4K.
films.description: When Saga returns home after an absence of two years, he’s dismayed to learn that his fiancée, Nogma, has married someone else – his father. Her feelings for Saga haven’t waned, she reassures him, but she had no choice in the matter. Reunited, they find temptation too great to resist: in the straw hut where Saga is staying, just outside the village, he and his would-have-been bride discreetly consummate their courtship. But this transgression is discovered – per tribal law, treated as not just adultery but also incest – fracturing both family bonds and the broader community. Like Idrissa Ouedraogo’s two earlier features, Yam Daabo and Yaaba (MIFF 1990), Tilaï features non-professional actors selected from among his family and friends; the film also offers a potent distillation of complex social dynamics set against a backdrop of expansive plains – the filmmaker’s native lands looming mythically in stark compositions – and ancient moral codes. Having originally screened in 1990 at both MIFF and Cannes, where it shared the Grand Jury Prize, Tilaï returns newly restored, its modernist take on a story steeped in patriarchal tradition as striking today as it was decades ago.

films.title: Yesterday The Eye Didn’t Sleep
films.countries: Lebanon, Saudi Arabia, Belgium, Palestine, Qatar
films.synopsis: The evocative debut of a former student of Abbas Kiarostami and Béla Tarr, this is an atmospheric portrait of the patriarchal weight of Bedouin tradition.
films.description: In Lebanon’s Bekaa Valley, Bedouin tribes still live according to the dictates of ancient customs, even as Israeli missile strikes sound in the distance and civil war in Syria lurks just over the mountains. Reem and Jawaher are two sisters – one quiet, one loud – who are fated to be married off to appease intertribal politics, regardless of their own desires. Gamra, another young woman, has fled her family and this traditional life in a blaze of glory. The connection to family, culture and land is strong, but – despite the open expanses of valley surrounding them – can often feel oppressive for the women of this community. Shot without a script and performed by a non-professional cast, Palestinian filmmaker Rakan Mayasi’s Cannes-premiering first feature follows a run of acclaimed shorts (including the award-winning Trumpets in the Sky , MIFF 2022) and training from global cinema titans Abbas Kiarostami and Béla Tarr. In Yesterday the Eye Didn’t Sleep , Mayasi draws from his own grandmother’s accounts of being forced into marriage at just 14 to document and interrogate tribal custom, ritual and entrenched patriarchy. Showcasing sublime photography and thrumming with political subtext, this evocative work shows a filmmaker engaged with both landscape and people, alive to a sense of place and the interior lives of those who dwell there.

films.title: For the Opponents
films.synopsis: This Palme d’Or–awarded portrait of a Mexican boy boxer quietly referees a contest between dreams and expectations.
films.description: In the tough neighbourhood of Tepito, becoming a boxing champion is the great Mexican dream. But when little Damián is in the ring, cheered on by the family and coaches who’ve poured their ambition into him, his hunger to win battles a yearning for childhood. Modest and humane, Cannes 2026’s Short Film Palme d’Or winner trusts in your empathy to intuit quieter kinds of violence.
films.awards: This Palme d’Or–awarded portrait of a Mexican boy boxer quietly referees a contest between dreams and expectations. | Modest and humane, Cannes 2026’s Short Film Palme d’Or winner trusts in your empathy to intuit quieter kinds of violence.

films.title: The Clouds Are Two Thousand Meters Up
films.synopsis: Venture to the cutting edge of XR in this haunting, reality-bending pilgrimage between digital worlds and a living forest.
films.description: A big screen overlooking a bustling intersection broadcasts the news of a terrible train accident, alerting protagonist Guan to the death of his wife. Discovering in the aftermath of this tragedy that she was working on a novel, Guan is inspired to follow its narrative, charting a path that shifts masterfully between the digital realm of her cloud storage and the fantasy-tinged real-life forest where the book is set, home to an endangered leopard and Taiwan’s Indigenous Rukai people. Singing Chen’s staggering new immersive work – adapted from a short story by acclaimed Taiwanese author Wu Ming-Yi, and the winner of the Venice Immersive Grand Prize – poignantly expands on the theme of memory the artist explored in Afterimage for Tomorrow (MIFF 2019) while taking the medium of extended reality to new technological heights. Rather than adopting a first-person perspective, the user figures as Guan’s shadow: a ghostly presence moving freely through an eight-square-metre space. Co-presented by Now or Never, The Clouds Are Two Thousand Meters Up unfolds a seamless new horizon of storytelling.

films.title: International Shorts 2
films.synopsis: A selection of the world’s brightest and bravest short-form storytelling.
films.description: A Chinese man wanders through a dystopian simulacrum of a German city; a Ukrainian couple’s reality collapses in the wake of the Russian invasion; and a Palestinian woman confronts an existential threat on the road to Ramallah. Plus, catch Cannes’ Palme d’Or–winning short, a moving portrait of a young Mexican boxer.
films.awards: Plus, catch Cannes’ Palme d’Or–winning short, a moving portrait of a young Mexican boxer.
