films.title: Armani Beauty Cinema Club Opening Night: Wishful Thinking
films.synopsis: Armani Beauty brings its signature glow to Australia’s premier cinema event.
films.description: Hosted at The Capitol, the Armani Beauty Cinema Club Opening Night blends the elegance of global cinema with local prestige, offering guests an immersive evening of beauty experiences and a premium screening of MIFF Headliner Wishful Thinking . This SXSW Grand Jury Award–winning high-concept rom-com playfully questions the notion that love is the most powerful force in existence … for better or worse.
films.awards: This SXSW Grand Jury Award–winning high-concept rom-com playfully questions the notion that love is the most powerful force in existence … for better or worse.

films.title: Boss Cat
films.synopsis: Desperate for independence, Sonja discovers the vibrant world of krump dancing, where she embraces expressiveness, challenges social judgement and finds her people.
films.description: When her mother suddenly dies, Sonja (played by impressive newcomer Olivia Hargroder) is thrown into tumult. Dealing with early-twenties existential angst and a sense of responsibility for her teenage younger sister Emma (Julia Savage, Blaze , MIFF 2022), she has a lot on her plate. Suddenly under the care of her controlling grandma and – as a young woman living with Down syndrome – constantly faced with a society that judges what she’s capable of, Sonja is desperate to be herself and take charge of her own life. When a chance encounter with the charismatic Hakim (Elijah Williams, Home and Away ) leads her to a krump class, Sonja discovers a life-changing outlet for expression. Since her Tropfest-winning 2009 short Be My Brother , filmmaker Genevieve Clay-Smith has long been an advocate for stronger onscreen representation of people of diverse backgrounds and identities; her 2018 short Inclusion Makes the World More Vibrant effectively distils her mission statement in its title. Filled with energy, enthusiasm, optimism and a gentle dose of provocation, Clay-Smith’s debut fiction feature is a moving, empowering, feel-good Aussie film.

films.title: Forgotten Island
films.synopsis: H.E.R., Liza Soberano, Manny Jacinto and Lea Salonga lend their voices to this fantastical animated tale of friendship, fearlessness and Filipino culture.
films.description: Best friends Jo and Raissa have just graduated from high school. They vow to remain close forever, even though Raissa’s about to move to the US for university. On their last night together, the girls accidentally open a portal to Nakali, a mysterious island populated by aswangs . The longer you stay in Nakali, the more your memories fade – so they must figure out how to get back home, fast, and also avoid the clutches of the Dreaded Manananggal. From DreamWorks, the studio behind Shrek and How to Train Your Dragon , comes this immersive, exciting adventure in which two teenagers learn about bravery and unbreakable bonds. Steeped in Philippine mythology and rooted in the real-world friendship between directors Joel Crawford and Januel Mercado, the film boasts a voice cast of Filipino A-listers – including Grammy winner H.E.R.; actors Liza Soberano ( Lisa Frankenstein ), Manny Jacinto ( The Good Place ) and Dolly de Leon ( Triangle of Sadness , MIFF 2022); Broadway icon Lea Salonga; and comedian Jo Koy – as well as fellow stars Dave Franco, Jenny Slate and Ronny Chieng. Capped off with resplendent anime-inspired visuals and folk-inspired music, Forgotten Island reminds us that growing up doesn’t have to mean growing apart.

films.title: Maddie’s Secret
films.synopsis: Cult comedian John Early directs and stars as a budding foodie in this big-hearted paean to womanhood and camp TV melodramas.
films.description: Maddie’s (John Early, Stress Positions , MIFF 2024) culinary gifts impress her husband and her best friend. But at the food content-creation company Gourmaybe, where she works, the shy kitchen whiz prefers to stick to washing dishes. When a video of Maddie cooking unexpectedly goes viral, she’s catapulted into the spotlight, but the pressure leads her to revert to a former destructive outlet: bulimia. Can she get away with peddling delectable food while secretly purging it? With visual touches recalling Douglas Sirk and John Waters, the feature directorial debut of actor and comedian Early is a stunning throwback to the golden age of the made-for-TV movie. But it’s also a tragicomic masterpiece all its own: alongside the inevitable laughs, the film is a winning blend of tender character study, ode to feminine resilience and critique of egoistic influencer culture. Supported by a sterling cast that includes Kate Berlant, Claudia O’Doherty, Eric Rahill, Conner O’Malley and Saturday Night Live ’s Vanessa Bayer, Early is formidable as Maddie, sensitively portraying her kindness, candour and weakness for both validation and rejection.
films.awards: With visual touches recalling Douglas Sirk and John Waters, the feature directorial debut of actor and comedian Early is a stunning throwback to the golden age of the made-for-TV movie.

films.title: Rodeo Dreams
films.synopsis: In rural Queensland, four determined young bull riders try to make it in one of the most dangerous sports on Earth.
films.description: The biggest bull-riding event in the Southern Hemisphere, the Mount Isa Mines Rodeo is a place where young cowboys with big dreams go to strut their stuff, hoping to hang on for just eight seconds. Soft-spoken Donovan Rutherford is a true pro, admired by many and feted as a future champion; Indigenous rider Peter Gregory Jr is seeking to match his father’s exploits in the arena; Camicka Anderson is trying to make it as a rare female competitor in a macho sport; and charismatic Darcy Harrison, who’s prone to talking about himself in the third person, is oozing with natural talent and a real contender – or might be, if he bothers to show up. But it’s not just these teens and young adults’ own ambitions that are at stake. When the 60-plus-year event enters involuntary administration, Mount Isa residents are faced with a choice: to put together a last-ditch grassroots bid to save the rodeo, or to let this institution disappear in a cloud of dust. Filmed over three years, Rhian Skirving ( Greenhouse by Joost , MIFF 2022) and W.A.M. Bleakley’s ( A Birthday Party , MIFF 2017) Sydney Film Festival Audience Award–winning documentary is not only a portrait of its young subjects, but also a bigger-picture evocation of life and community in Queensland’s Gulf Country. With original music by Sam Teskey of The Teskey Brothers and backing from the producers behind recent MIFF crowdpleasers Spreadsheet Champions (MIFF 2025) and Ellis Park (MIFF 2024), this beautifully photographed real-life coming-of-age saga is a warm, infectious journey into a part of Australia rarely represented on the big screen.

films.title: Summer 2000: The X-Cetra Story
films.synopsis: The true story of how an album made by four tween girls became an accidental alt-music sensation.
films.description: In the first summer of the new millennium, with girl pop dominating the charts, 11-year-old Ayden moved to Santa Rosa, California, and met her new best friends: Jessica, Janet and Janet’s nine-year-old sister Mary. In between millennial tween pursuits like devising dance routines and holding sleepover seances, the four girls started a band. Calling themselves X-Cetra, they recorded eight songs with the help of Janet and Mary’s musician mum, Robin. But the final album was a disappointment, sounding more like a creepy, haunted version of Pure Moods than the slick pop they’d imagined. When summer ended, so did X-Cetra. Their accidentally avant-garde album made its way onto the internet, however, where it developed a life of its own. Two decades later, having long since settled into the routines and responsibilities of work and family, the now-adult quartet were astonished to find that their childhood experiment had developed a fan base on music forums – and, inspired by the discovery, set to work rekindling their friendship and creating new music together. Winning the Grand Jury Award in SXSW’s Documentary Feature Competition, Ayden Mayeri’s autobiographical documentary doesn’t merely settle for Y2K nostalgia or outsider-music obsessions; rather, it’s an unexpectedly honest, introspective tale about four women reconnecting with fearless creativity and reckoning with their most authentic selves.

films.title: Yesterday Island
films.synopsis: In this award-winning Australian indie comedy, an unsuccessful writer, stuck living the same day on repeat on a subantarctic island, must find another unwitting victim to take his place.
films.description: Amos, a failed novelist with a failed marriage, arrives on a remote southern island in full midlife-crisis mode. But his escape soon becomes a prison: he discovers he’s trapped infinitely reliving the same day, complete with the same dead birds, weird board games, tinned spaghetti and unexplained murders. The only way to get off this hamster wheel is to trick someone else into replacing you, and Amos’s estranged ex-wife, Gemma, seems like the perfect candidate – but her arrival complicates things, and Amos is forced to confront his past. Having landed a veritable alphabet of international film festival prizes – an Audience Award at Austin, Critics’ Prize at Brussels and Best Feature at Cleveland – Yesterday Island is a true crowdpleaser, a small-budget but exceptionally shot achievement in inventive worldbuilding. It also functions as a blackly funny parable about moral responsibility, evoking Greek myth to put its own unique spin on classic time-loop films like Groundhog Day , Palm Springs and One More Shot (MIFF 2025). Boosted by a game cast including acclaimed stand-up Ivan Aristeguieta, up-and-coming comic performer Florence Noble, and veteran character actors David Fane ( Eagle vs Shark , MIFF 2007) and Francis Greenslade, Yesterday Island is a film you’ll want to revisit over and over again.

films.title: Hear My Eyes: Memento
films.synopsis: See Christopher Nolan’s breakthrough film, complete with an all-new live score by Michael “CAVS” Cavanagh (King Gizzard & the Lizard Wizard).
films.description: Before the billion-dollar blockbusters, before Batman, before Oppenheimer , Christopher Nolan released his second feature: Memento . The independent thriller quickly became a cultural phenomenon, earning Oscar nominations for Best Original Screenplay and Best Film Editing, and introduced audiences to the narrative puzzles, fractured timelines and obsessive attention to detail that would come to define the director’s work. For this special presentation, Michael “CAVS” Cavanagh of King Gizzard & the Lizard Wizard reimagines the film with an all-new live score, performing solo across multiple drum kits and a suite of electronic instruments. Technical and bombastic, Cavanagh’s performance will mirror the intensity and precision at the heart of Nolan's most audacious work. Part film screening, part live concert, Hear My Eyes has spent over a decade creating immersive cinema experiences, building a loyal following through collaborations including Terminator 2: Judgment Day with Peter Van Hoesen and Robin Fox, Chopper with Springtime and Mick Harvey, Wake in Fright with Surprise Chef, and Pan’s Labyrinth with Sleep D. For Nolan devotees and anyone who loves cinema that takes risks, this is a chance to revisit a cult classic, live and reimagined.

films.title: My Year Without Sex
films.synopsis: Watt’s warm and keenly funny follow-up to Look Both Ways offers a portal into a suburban Aussie family home rocked by a health crisis.
films.description: Natalie (Sacha Horler, Body Blow , MIFF 2026) and Ross (Matt Day, Underbelly ) haven’t let parenthood unduly interrupt their sex lives … but a brain aneurysm is a different story. When a worse-for-wear Natalie wakes up in the hospital, she discovers that a lot of activities, not just coitus, are going to be off the table for a bit. Chronicling the lives of the members of this loving, casually chaotic household in the Melbourne ’burbs, My Year Without Sex unfolds as a series of 12 endearingly offbeat episodes – one for each month of Natalie’s recovery year. Writer/director Sarah Watt was already an accomplished animator when she made her award-winning live-action debut Look Both Ways in 2005. Her sophomore feature extends that film’s fascination with the tragedy and humour of serious illness, and would have been the second in a proposed trilogy if Watt herself had not tragically passed away from cancer in 2011 without completing another film. Her swan song evinces a distinctive, seasoned talent taken much too soon.
films.awards: Writer/director Sarah Watt was already an accomplished animator when she made her award-winning live-action debut Look Both Ways in 2005.
films.recommendation: 暂无 Letterboxd 评分；剧情、喜剧 / Director in Focus: Sarah Watt。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实、音乐与表演。奖项/展映线索：获奖

films.title: Newport & the Great Folk Dream
films.synopsis: In 1965, Bob Dylan took the stage at the Newport Folk Festival and changed music forever … or so the story goes, as this glorious, immersive documentary explores.
films.description: There are many interpretations of the heckling at the Newport Folk Festival that accompanied Bob Dylan plugging in for the first time, but one thing remains clear: it represented a key turning point in the history of American music. In Newport & the Great Folk Dream, that moment is treated as a starting point for investigating the broader phenomenon of Newport, and how the countercultural event became one of the most iconic of its kind. Mining the archives of previously unseen footage shot over four successive years by Murray Lerner for his 1967 Oscar-nominated documentary Festival, director Robert Gordon (Best of Enemies, MIFF 2015) has assembled an ecstatic account of the Newport Folk Festival in its formative years. Featuring illuminating performances from Johnny Cash, Joan Baez, John Lee Hooker, Muddy Waters, Howlin’ Wolf, Taj Mahal, Buffy Sainte-Marie, The Staple Singers and many others, alongside contemporary interviews with those who were there, it’s a historical document par excellence. Placing this precious footage within its social and political context, Newport & the Great Folk Dream is an extraordinarily raw and energising music documentary.
films.awards: ...f previously unseen footage shot over four successive years by Murray Lerner for his 1967 Oscar-nominated documentary Festival, director Robert Gordon (Best of Enemies, MIFF 2015) has assembled an ecstatic account of the Newp...
films.recommendation: 暂无 Letterboxd 评分；纪录片 / 音乐电影。推荐理由：政治与社会现实、记忆与历史、音乐与表演、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：提名

films.title: A to A
films.synopsis: A road movie that goes absolutely nowhere – to hypnotic, hilarious effect.
films.description: Demonstrating the playful qualities that once saw him dubbed the enfant terrible of experimental film, Johann Lurf filmed this absurdist 2011 short from a sputtering Vespa as it circled – and circled and circled and circled – 100 different roundabouts in his homeland, Austria. Full of mediocre landscapes, shoddy architecture and novelty sculpture, it’s an endearing homage to human achievement at its least wondrous. Winner of a short film award at the 2011 Viennale.
films.awards: Winner of a short film award at the 2011 Viennale.
films.recommendation: 暂无 Letterboxd 评分；实验 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：政治与社会现实。奖项/展映线索：获奖

films.title: As Told by a Corpse
films.synopsis: In this ecstatic vision of the body in flux, a newborn encounters their digital mutation 25 years on
films.description: In this ecstatic vision of the body in flux, a newborn encounters their digital mutation 25 years on. Crackling with sensuality and raucous invention, Yace Sula’s delirious portrayal of queer sexuality – which won an Emerging Experimental Video Artist award at the Ann Arbor Film Festival – uses footage of the director’s own christening as a playground for a series of analog and digital aberrations, a kind of phantom presence haunting the infant from the future.

films.title: Au hasard
films.synopsis: Do we see what other moviegoers see? A fascinating act of cinematic misdirection for our age of misinformation.
films.description: Missed information becomes misinformation in this audacious work of found footage and formal playfulness from Canadian visual artist Darren Dominique Heroux, in which audience members receive different decoder glasses at random – and no two viewing experiences are the same. Winner of the Peter Wilde Award for Most Technically Innovative Film at Ann Arbor.

films.title: Daddy’s Little Meatball
films.synopsis: An angsty teenage girl tags along with her feminine-hygiene-product salesman dad on a business trip to New York, where their relationship gets put to the test.
films.description: Drawing upon her own awkward teenage experiences, Yael Grunseit crafts a tender, funny and endearingly human portrait of a strained father–daughter connection, brought to life by evocative handheld photography and a dynamic pair of lead performances. Winner of Best Local Short Film and Best Cinematography in an Australian Short at Flickerfest.
films.awards: Winner of Best Local Short Film and Best Cinematography in an Australian Short at Flickerfest.

films.title: Daria’s Night Flowers
films.synopsis: Multiple MIFF award winner Maryam Tafakory returns with an epistolary tale of suppressed desire.
films.description: The winner of last year’s MIFF Best Documentary Short Film for Razeh-del , acclaimed Iranian filmmaker Maryam Tafakory constructs another speculative, secret history of her homeland, using her signature reimagining of archival film footage to relay one woman’s letter to another – a dispatch marked by domestic abuse, divorce and a profound lack of agency. This is a vibrant feminist work from one of the world’s foremost experimental filmmakers.

films.title: Lion Rock
films.synopsis: While driving along an eerie ocean road, a teenage boy and his mother hit something with their car. She insists it was nothing, but he begins to doubt her story.
films.description: Inspired by a trip through Auckland’s spooky Waitākere Ranges, the latest work from award-winning filmmakers Prisca Bouchet and Nick Mayow is a unique take on the coming-of-age drama, transforming a routine drive into a mysterious thriller told from the boy’s perspective. Produced in conjunction with Jane Campion’s film school A Wave in the Ocean, this brilliantly executed short film premiered at Venice last year.

films.title: Sound That Summons
films.synopsis: In this dreamy, meditative slice of sensory immersion, a young sound recordist captures the world around him on returning to his hometown.
films.description: Award-winning cinematographer and filmmaker Petra Leslie ( Sunset Country , MIFF 2021) directs this visually striking work shot in Patagonia as part of a Playlab Films workshop run by filmmaker Paolo Sorrentino. With an immersive sound design that places us directly in the world of the protagonist, this luminous, reflective film summons a vivid sense of place.

films.title: Souvenir
films.synopsis: A teen girl struggles with trust due to her girlfriend’s disregard for boundaries.
films.description: During a family holiday, a closeted teenager becomes unnerved by her girlfriend’s possessive impulse to photograph her, both publicly and in their most intimate moments. Accelerator Lab alum Renée Marie Petropoulos ( Tangles and Knots , MIFF 2018) explores queer dimensions of consent, surveillance and self-performance in her evocative SXSW Grand Jury Award–winning short.

films.title: The Airport Chaplain
films.synopsis: Hugo Weaving and Shabana Azeez lead the cast of this compelling new drama series about a chaplain with an unorthodox approach to solving an airport’s daily crises, consistently overstepping boundaries to help passengers and workers alike.
films.description: At a bustling international airport, lost people seek missed connections, while others carry heavy personal baggage. Tobias Wallace (Hugo Weaving, The Rooster , MIFF 2023) shepherds them all. Scruffy and benevolent in his hi-vis “Chaplain”-printed vest and always on call, he’ll bend rules to help passengers and workers alike – that includes Asha, a young woman hiding in the prayer room whose murky history stirs Tobias’s own deeply buried traumas. Not everybody is a fan of his methods, however: ambitious new terminal manager Mira (Shabana Azeez, The Pitt ; Lesbian Space Princess , MIFF 2025) has liability nightmare Tobias marked for redundancy. But as crises appear everywhere – overworked baggage handlers, security breaches, families torn apart and grief-stricken relatives – maybe this infuriating chaplain could be Mira’s salvation. Multi-award-winning screenwriter Elise McCredie ( Stateless ; Ride Like a Girl ) based this much-anticipated new drama series, commissioned by SBS, on co-creator and Wooden Horse producer Jude Troy’s ( The Clearing ) encounter with a Melbourne Airport chaplain who helped her recover a lost handbag – and her identity. In this world premiere of the first three episodes, director Bonnie Moir ( Not Dark Yet , MIFF 2022) warmly illuminates a morally complex protagonist, while his multicultural, multigenerational co-workers are portrayed by a fully booked flight of Aussie talent, from screen legend Claudia Karvan ( Bump ; The Big Steal , MIFF 2017) and Thomas Weatherall ( Heartbreak High ; Narrow Road to the Deep North ) to Kavitha Anandasivam ( The Hunting ) and Erroll Shand ( Wolfram ).

films.title: Too Few to Mention
films.synopsis: Before disappearing from her life, an undocumented father shares a final moment with the daughter for whom he’s sacrificed everything.
films.description: As a young Chinese Australian woman graduates university, her father vanishes, leaving her a pouch of money and a lingering question: Was it all worth it? A work of great emotional precision, this tender short from author-turned-filmmaker Jeff Zechen Hu understands the power of nuance, offering a refreshing perspective on the immigrant experience by focusing on the family that must bear the cost.

films.title: Twosome
films.synopsis: In this comedic exploration of intimacy and consent, a sophisticated polyamorous couple spiral after being snubbed by their third.
films.description: Dining out for their anniversary, Andy and Scarlett get blanked by a woman they had a threesome with months earlier, prompting them to relitigate the encounter’s consent politics in excruciating detail. Wittily scripted and confidently directed by April-Rose Desalegn, co-writer and star of MIFF 2025 Best Australian Short winner The Eviction , Twosome is a fresh, provocative and very recognisable study of relationship dynamics.

films.title: Australian Shorts
films.synopsis: Exciting new work from some of the nation’s most electrifying filmmakers
films.description: Exciting new work from some of the nation’s most electrifying filmmakers. A taste of the finest in short-form Australian filmmaking, this eclectic selection includes a polyamorous screwball comedy from the co-writer and star of MIFF 2025’s Best Australian Short The Eviction , a haunting look at colonial violence, and the story of a man’s obsession with a mind-controlling worm in the family backyard. Plus, the SXSW Grand Jury Award–winning short from MIFF Accelerator Lab alum Renée Marie Petropoulos.

films.title: Documentary Shorts 2
films.synopsis: Films that challenge convention, inspire dialogue and meet the political moment
films.description: Films that challenge convention, inspire dialogue and meet the political moment. How does cinema respond to a world in turmoil? In these bracing, emotionally resonant shorts, we bear witness to a Palestinian childhood under occupation, the confessions of a former dog trainer who worked for the Chilean dictatorship, and the enigmatic babblings between fountains in Bratislava, custodians of a secret history of civic culture.

films.title: Experimental Shorts
films.synopsis: Visionary work from the avant-garde that reimagines the possibilities of cinema
films.description: Visionary work from the avant-garde that reimagines the possibilities of cinema. Expand your cinematic horizons with this formally daring line-up, including a hypnotic homage to three-strip Technicolor, a reimagining of The Decameron that flips Western perspectives and an ecstatic vision of the body as a newborn baby encounters their future digital mutation. Plus, the return of multiple MIFF award winner Maryam Tafakory ( Razeh-del, MIFF 2025) and local fave Allison Chorn ( The Plastic House , MIFF 2020).

films.title: Filipiñana
films.synopsis: In this Sundance-lauded debut, a young Filipina learns that subjugation and silence are par for the course at the golf club where she finds employment.
films.description: Having left rural Ilocos for Manila’s greener pastures, 17-year-old Isabel ends up on a different kind of green: working as a ‘tee girl’ for a country club. She quickly adopts a new routine teeing up balls for wealthy golfers, joining the staff to eat morsels of rice and fish while the clientele feast, and gradually growing infatuated with the wealthy doctor who runs the club. But as she discovers more about her patron and the institution to which he owes his fortune, Isabel finds herself drawn into a web of exploitation, displacement and unseen, sanitised violence. Executive-produced by Jia Zhang-ke and receiving a Special Jury Award at Sundance, Rafael Manuel’s scathing and contemplative feature debut escalates with menace despite its languid, sun-drenched setting, whose sheen conceals the gendered dynamics of power and how the extremely rich prey on the aspirational poor. Supported by Filipino screen mavens Angeli Bayani ( Ilo Ilo , MIFF 2013) and Ruby Ruiz ( First Light , MIFF 2025), Jorrybell Agoto lends an unsettled defiance to her portrayal of Isabel, propelling a sly satire of oppression entrenched through polished appearances.
films.awards: In this Sundance-lauded debut, a young Filipina learns that subjugation and silence are par for the course at the golf club where she finds employment. | Executive-produced by Jia Zhang-ke and receiving a Special Jury Award at Sundance, Rafael Manuel’s scathing and contemplative feature debut escalates with menace despite its languid, sun-drenched set...

films.title: Hollywood Heretic: The Many Faces of Kim Novak
films.synopsis: Take a deeper look at the life of actor Kim Novak with MIFF Senior Programmer Kate Fitzpatrick, Critics Campus mentor and writer Philippa Hawker, and Melbourne Cinémathèque co-curator and lecturer Eloise Ross.
films.description: To celebrate the work of screen icon Kim Novak and the six films screening in the Kim Novak: Hollywood Heretic strand, the panel will share some of their personal favourite performances and iconic moments from her life through clips and commentary. They’ll also look back at her storied career, diving into her early appearance in The Man With the Golden Arm , legendary performances in Vertigo and Bell, Book and Candle opposite James Stewart in the same year, through to lesser-known but no less stunning roles in Middle of the Night , Strangers When We Meet and The Legend of Lylah Clare . They’ll look at the struggles Novak faced coming up in the studio system: fighting to keep her name, protesting at being underpaid, her position among peers and her uneasy relationship with her craft. Come learn about her filmography before meeting the woman herself in the documentary Kim Novak’s Vertigo . Kate Fitzpatrick is a film programmer and general doer of no good. At MIFF, she programs new features, retrospectives and XR, and curates the MIFF Schools section of the program.

films.title: Kings of Venice
films.synopsis: Amid all the colour and chaos of Venice Beach, (ultra-)competitive paddle tennis players are a band apart. The only thing that can unite them? A hatred of pickleball.
films.description: In the Los Angeles neighbourhood of Venice Beach, 11 paddle tennis courts are under threat from an encroaching enemy: pickleball. The hugely popular monolith, like some ruthless developer, is taking up increasing space in its expansion, threatening to wipe out a small community playing an exceedingly similar sport. From Scott, the self-described GOAT of paddle tennis, to hot-tempered Italian model Ernesto, these misfits unite to stage a paddlers vs pickleballers tournament. Winner gets $50,000 – and the right to control these courts. “The Venice Beach paddle tennis world is sort of like a back-alley fistfight,” says one of the oddballs in this quirky, captivating doc. Chronicling a subculture, those who inhabit it and their sporting passions, it’s surely MIFF 2026’s only film to feature a scene where the cops get called on people for yelling at pickleballers. Having enthralled festival-goers at DOC NYC and Slamdance (where it won the Audience Award for Documentary Feature), Kings of Venice is a crowdpleasing delight: a serve of outsized personalities that wouldn’t be out of place in a Christopher Guest comedy.
films.awards: Amid all the colour and chaos of Venice Beach, (ultra-)competitive paddle tennis players are a band apart. | In the Los Angeles neighbourhood of Venice Beach, 11 paddle tennis courts are under threat from an encroaching enemy: pickleball.

films.title: Small Treasures: Animations by Sarah Watt
films.synopsis: A tribute to the early work of the late, great Australian writer/director.
films.description: From the cool splash of a swimming pool on a scorching summer day to a young girl’s spiritual kinship with her feathered friends, these award-winning, rarely seen animated shorts from the beloved Look Both Ways filmmaker reveal her wry, deeply human approach to life, love and our precious connection to the Earth. Films in this package: Deep Dive (1990), Catch of the Day (1990), Shark – Australia (The Web) (1993), Small Treasures (1995), Local Dive (1998), The Way of the Birds (2000) and Living With Happiness (2001).

films.title: Viva Carmen
films.synopsis: This painterly, profound adaptation of Georges Bizet’s iconic opera – told from the perspective of children – wowed Cannes crowds with its incredible use of colour.
films.description: Salva (voiced by Milo Machado-Graner, Goodbye, Cruel World , MIFF 2026), Belén, Piranha and La Bola are street kids scrapping for survival on the streets of 19th-century Seville. Salva works for the blind knife grinder Antonio, who foretells a mystical prophecy of the death of free-spirited young Romani woman Carmen, who will be killed by the soldier who loves her. When Salva leaps into action in the hope of saving Carmen from her fate, his actions instead set the prophesy into motion. From Jacques Feyder’s Carmen (MIFF 1981) and Jean-Luc Godard’s Prénom Carmen (MIFF 1985) through to Mark Dornford-May’s U-Carmen eKhayelitsha (MIFF 2005), Georges Bizet’s iconic opera has proved a fertile text for cinematic adaptations. Animator Sébastien Laudenbach ( The Girl Without Hands , MIFF 2016) offers a unique take on the classic text, turning the narrative away from the star-cross’d lovers at the centre of its tragedy and instead presenting it from the perspective of children watching fate unfold. Filled with expressive lines and evocative colours, this gorgeously hand-painted work is a sheer delight for those who treasure the animated artform.
films.awards: This painterly, profound adaptation of Georges Bizet’s iconic opera – told from the perspective of children – wowed Cannes crowds with its incredible use of colour.
films.recommendation: 暂无 Letterboxd 评分；动画 / 动画单元 / 维州首映。推荐理由：亲密关系、失去与哀悼。奖项/展映线索：戛纳

films.title: John Cameron Mitchell Presents: Hedwig and the Angry Inch 25th Anniversary
films.synopsis: Watch a cult-classic genderqueer musical in the best way – with a witty live commentary by its transgressive and talented director/star.
films.description: Hedwig is an East German punk singer on a quest for her other half. As a teenager named Hansel, she seeks a soulmate in American soldier Luther, becoming his wife after a botched genital surgery leaves her with an “angry inch” of flesh between her legs. After Luther abandons Hedwig in Kansas, she sees herself in shy, repressed Tommy, inviting him to share her rock-star dreams. Tommy spurns Hedwig, but goes on to steal her songs to fuel his own success. As she chases Tommy’s tour on a string of low-rent gigs with her band, the Angry Inch, Hedwig doesn’t see how desperately her husband and bandmate Yitzhak yearns to be her … John Cameron Mitchell’s genderfluid rock musical, based on his off-Broadway stage show, has been reuniting film audiences with their true selves for 25 years. Festivals instantly saw it was special: Hedwig ’s clutch of prizes included the Teddy Award at the 2001 Berlinale as well as Sundance audience and directing awards. But Mitchell’s fluid portrayal of love and identity remains a life-changing cult classic because it defiantly troubles categories of drag and trans, nonbinarity and androgyny.
films.awards: Festivals instantly saw it was special: Hedwig ’s clutch of prizes included the Teddy Award at the 2001 Berlinale as well as Sundance audience and directing awards.

films.title: Daughters of the Late Colonel
films.synopsis: In a merry adaptation of a century-old short story, two sisters seize the courage to live anew after their overbearing father’s death.
films.description: After their dreadful, controlling father finally dies, middle-aged sisters Constantia and Josephine are emboldened to repel the sleazy advances of clergyman Mr. Farolles. In cheeky pen scribbles and watercolour blots that could have been torn from a repressed lady’s journals, Elizabeth Hobbs retells Katherine Mansfield’s celebrated 1920 modernist short story with deadpan verve, granting her heroines an irreverence to fuel their awakening independence. A Cannes Directors’ Fortnight selection.
films.awards: A Cannes Directors’ Fortnight selection.

films.title: Free Eliza (notes on an anatomical imperfection)
films.synopsis: In this deadpan class satire, a luxury hotel worker pushes back against a world that demands women perform happiness at any cost.
films.description: Eliza works at a five-star hotel and she’s great at it, except for one thing – anatomically, she cannot smile. In a droll voiceover, she notes the professional setbacks of being a woman unable to fawn and pander to the idle rich. Coming to MIFF direct from its premiere at Cannes Directors’ Fortnight, this poker-faced satire in the vein of Yorgos Lanthimos will have you doing what Eliza can’t.
films.awards: Coming to MIFF direct from its premiere at Cannes Directors’ Fortnight, this poker-faced satire in the vein of Yorgos Lanthimos will have you doing what Eliza can’t.

films.title: Socks
films.synopsis: A beautifully restrained tale of queer yearning, in which a young Māori boy develops an unlikely bond with a Mormon missionary in rural New Zealand.
films.description: Filmed in timeless black-and-white and anchored by a phenomenal performance by young star Monty Walker, Todd Karehana’s gentle coming-of-ager – executive-produced by Jane Campion, and debuting at the Venice International Film Festival – has all the makings of a crowd-pleasing queer classic. Socks is an evocative look at male friendship that transcends culture, religion and desire.

films.title: The Worm
films.synopsis: A young man’s bizarre obsession spurs a family intervention – but what if there really is a mind-controlling worm in his backyard?
films.description: Convinced a malevolent worm is controlling his thoughts, Kieran (Joe Bird, Leviticus ) hunts it down on his parents’ property, provoking an absurd confrontation. Premiering at Sundance, this raucously silly comedy from Tom Noakes ( Nursery Rhymes , MIFF 2018) could be an allegory for addiction, mental health crisis or conspiracy theory indoctrination – or Kieran could be right …
films.awards: Premiering at Sundance, this raucously silly comedy from Tom Noakes ( Nursery Rhymes , MIFF 2018) could be an allegory for addiction, mental health crisis or conspiracy theory indoctrination – or Kieran could be right …

films.title: ¡PIKA!
films.synopsis: Driven wild by an inexplicable itch, a desperate man seeks relief in a city’s seedy underbelly.
films.description: Lucas wakes up one night with an itch so terrible he’ll brave any grimy underworld den in Lima – and do anything! – to soothe it. Screening at Sitges 2025 and Sundance 2026, Alex Fischman Cárdenas’s magnetic, uncategorisably strange short hurtles its existentially tormented protagonist forward with Safdie-like pace and tension, ratcheting up Lucas’s predicament to an audacious final scene you absolutely won’t see coming.
films.awards: Screening at Sitges 2025 and Sundance 2026, Alex Fischman Cárdenas’s magnetic, uncategorisably strange short hurtles its existentially tormented protagonist forward wi...

films.title: In Conversation: Tamra Davis
films.synopsis: Take a masterclass with visiting filmmaker Tamra Davis exploring her directorial career, from iconic music videos to Crossroads , from Billy Madison to Basquiat , and now to her own 90s past in The Best Summer . Hosted by Kate Jinx.
films.description: Visiting MIFF from Los Angeles with her new documentary The Best Summer – made from archival footage she filmed in Australia while on the 1995–96 Summersault festival tour – Tamra will spend an hour speaking about her incredibly varied career to date. Having made a name for herself directing music videos for a litany of musical luminaries such as Sonic Youth ( ‘Bull in the Heather’ ; ‘Kool Thing’ ), The Smiths ( ‘Shoplifters of the World Unite’ ; ‘Sheila Take a Bow’ ), Young MC ( ‘Bust a Move’ ) and Bette Midler ( ‘From a Distance’ ), Davis has gone on to make nine feature films. Her directorial credits include Guncrazy (1994), starring Drew Barrymore; Adam Sandler breakout Billy Madison (1995); Britney Spears road-trip feature Crossroads (2022); and Netflix’s 13: The Musical (2022), as well as episodes for Grey’s Anatomy and Dead to Me . Previously, her acclaimed documentary Jean-Michel Basquiat: The Radiant Child premiered at the Sundance Film Festival in 2010, where she returned in 2026 to premiere The Best Summer . Kate Jinx is a writer, critic and film curator. She is a Senior Programmer at the Melbourne International Film Festival, film critic for The Monthly , founding program director of Sydney’s Golden Age Cinema and host of the culture podcast See Also .
films.awards: Previously, her acclaimed documentary Jean-Michel Basquiat: The Radiant Child premiered at the Sundance Film Festival in 2010, where she returned in 2026 to premiere The Best Summer .
films.recommendation: 暂无 Letterboxd 评分；对谈活动。推荐理由：家庭关系、成长与青春、记忆与历史、音乐与表演、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：圣丹斯、首映

films.title: Usinimage
films.synopsis: Ulrike Ottinger revisits the locations of her famed ‘Berlin Trilogy’, juxtaposing frank documentation with fantasy.
films.description: In this exploration of both German urban architecture and her own cinematic work, Ottinger returns to selected locations from her ‘Berlin Trilogy’: Ticket of No Return (1979), Freak Orlando (1981) and Dorian Gray in the Mirror of the Yellow Press (1984). Following a joyous title sequence, industrial scenes and cityscapes are intercut with vignettes from their corresponding film scenes – providing less of a ‘then and now’ than a ‘what if’.

films.title: Adam’s Apple
films.synopsis: In this candid and heartwarming portrait of transition – through gender and into adulthood – a filmmaker mother and trans son capture the power of acceptance and authenticity.
films.description: Adam Sieswerda always knew he was a boy, but the aspiring singer-songwriter feared his artistic prospects because he also knew his voice wouldn’t drop. At 15, with support from his parents, he received his first testosterone shot – and so began Adam’s journey to becoming his true self. As he embraces his growing identity as a fledgling young adult male, his mother, filmmaker Amy Jenkins, documents various milestones – psychologist appointments, university applications, a ‘boob funeral’ – with both curiosity and compassion. Screening at SXSW and CPH:DOX, Jenkins’s eight-years-in-the-making film chronicles Adam’s transformative experiences of gender-affirming care while giving glimpses into the logistical, physiological and emotional demands of the process. At times, she even lets Adam take the reins – her handheld footage and archival clips interwoven with his smartphone videos – lending further intimacy to a film already brimming with energetic honesty. Amid the threats to trans rights in their home country and around the world, Adam’s Apple is an urgent and undeniably touching testament to familial solidarity and trans affirmation.
films.awards: Screening at SXSW and CPH:DOX, Jenkins’s eight-years-in-the-making film chronicles Adam’s transformative experiences of gender-affirming care while...

films.title: Aniki-Bóbó
films.synopsis: The feature debut of Portuguese master Manoel de Oliveira returns to the screen, beautifully restored, as a charming, proto-neorealist revelation.
films.description: Young Carlitos’s heart beats a little faster for Terezinha; so does Eduardo’s. Terezinha, for her part, has eyes mainly for a doll in the window display of a shop, the latter of which has the tantalising and lightly ominous name Temptations. Hoping to win his crush’s admiration, Carlitos swipes the doll for her – and, in doing so, introduces a new tension and moral murkiness into the lives of this gang of Porto urchins. Working from a short story by José Joaquim Rodrigues de Freitas, Portuguese director Manoel de Oliveira ( The Strange Case of Angelica , MIFF 2010) anticipated the eruption of neorealism in Italy several years later by choosing to use nonprofessional actors and shoot on location in his home city. But while its greatness is now recognised, Aniki-Bóbó was largely dismissed in its day, and de Oliveira – the only filmmaker whose career stretched from the silent to the digital era, and one who remarkably continued directing beyond his 100th birthday – would not make another feature-length film for 21 years. In its spareness, the auteur’s first fiction film is an edifying counterpoint to his later, lavishly stylised works, and enchanting in its own right.

films.title: Big Girls Don’t Cry
films.synopsis: Set in a coastal New Zealand town over the summer of 2006, this queer coming-of-age story – executive-produced by Jane Campion – is a bittersweet slice of pre-smartphone-era teendom.
films.description: In the hazy days between Christmas and the new school year, 14-year-old Sid Bookman has a chance to reinvent herself for the tourists who flock to her small New Zealand beach town of Ōmaha. She dabbles in all the rebellious teenage rites of passage – drinking, smoking weed, self-piercing and generally trying anything to impress the popular crowd – but also dips her toe into the tantalising and dangerous realms of the internet. As Sid encounters strangers in chat rooms, she flirts with the girl she has a crush on by impersonating her best friend’s brother on an instant-messaging app. Despite her desperate bids for acceptance, she captures the attention of her older sister’s friend Freya, who sees the real Sid underneath all the posturing. The debut feature from writer/director Paloma Schneideman ( Gate Crash , MIFF 2023), a former MIFF Accelerator Lab participant and graduate of Jane Campion’s film school program A Wave in the Ocean, Big Girls Don’t Cry is a sensitive, intimate and unflinchingly honest portrayal of queer adolescence. Ani Palmer’s remarkable debut performance steers the film, conveying Sid’s youthful angst, burgeoning sexual curiosity and uneasiness in her own skin with impressive restraint; while Noah Taylor ( Predestination , MIFF 2014) plays a key supporting role.
films.recommendation: 暂无 Letterboxd 评分；剧情 / 新锐视野 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、成长与青春。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Chimney Town: Frozen in Time
films.synopsis: This thoughtful, visually vivid sequel to anime mega-hit Poupelle of Chimney Town is an epic adventure about the power of belief.
films.description: Though now hailed as the hero of Chimney Town, Lubicchi is struggling to move on after the departure of Poupelle, his magical friend made of garbage. When an accident transports him to a mythical world populated by talking animals and impish spirits, he is told by the realm’s rat queen that he can only return home if he manages to restart the land’s giant clock, which has been frozen at 11.59 for decades. With the help of Fluff, a large white cat with attitude, Lubicchi discovers that a grieving clockmaker and a plant spirit in human form are two figures who hold the key to his quest. The film adaptation of Poupelle of Chimney Town struck a chord in Japan and abroad when it emerged during the pandemic; Akihiro Nishino’s original picture book has since also been adapted into musicals, ballet and kabuki. Working from an original script by Nishino, this sequel is another grand family adventure dealing with big themes like grief and coming of age. Where Poupelle radiated steampunk vibes, however, Frozen in Time takes on a more fantastical, psychedelic style, constructing a visual world reminiscent of Hayao Miyazaki’s all-time classic Spirited Away .

films.title: Closure
films.synopsis: Transcending true-crime cliché, this masterful study of obsession documents a father’s quest for answers in the wake of his teenage son’s disappearance
films.description: Transcending true-crime cliché, this masterful study of obsession documents a father’s quest for answers in the wake of his teenage son’s disappearance. The last-known footage of 16-year-old Krzysztof Dymiński – “Chris” to his friends – was captured in the early hours of the morning in May 2023 on CCTV: he is seen standing on a bridge overlooking Warsaw’s Vistula River; the camera pans away and back, and Chris is gone. One year on, little hope remains that Chris is still alive, but his father Daniel pours his immense grief into the task of at least filling in the narrative blanks. He trawls his son’s social media profiles and the river itself for clues, increasingly consumed by his search.

films.title: Corpus
films.synopsis: Lured to a mansion in the woods of upstate New York, party-loving bros find a threesome of witchy femmes who are down for much darker carnal pursuits.
films.description: In the sultry New York summer of 1998, night-life photographer and small-time drug dealer Sayo (Jeff Wahlberg) reluctantly accepts a party invitation from rising movie star Vince (Brodie Townsend, Heartbreak High ), his long-time friend for whom he has complicated feelings. Their boisterous club buddy Ross is keen to tag along, so the three head upstate to an isolated mansion estate, ready to get rowdy. Instead, they find Billie, Wren and Cata – three mysterious women whose disturbing agenda draws them into a dark web of seduction and terror. Corpus is the feature directorial debut of Corrin Evans, whose interest in exploring eroticism onscreen led her to become an in-demand intimacy coordinator, who has plied her trade on such spicy fare as Ti West’s MaXXXine and Larry Charles’s Dicks: The Musical . She’s joined on this super-horny horror film by co-writer and producer Lily Cowles, who also takes centre stage onscreen as sinister photographer Billie. Steaming into MIFF from its premiere at Montreal’s Fantasia International Film Festival, Corpus announces Evans as an exciting new voice in genre film.
films.awards: Steaming into MIFF from its premiere at Montreal’s Fantasia International Film Festival, Corpus announces Evans as an exciting new voice in genre film.

films.title: Coyote vs. ACME
films.title_cn: 威利狼大戰阿卡米公司
films.synopsis: Sick of being maimed by his shoddy tools, Wile E. Coyote sues the company responsible. Prepare to witness a legal comedy that’s dynamite … in every sense of the word.
films.description: For decades, pure-hearted predator Wile E. Coyote has suffered the slings and anvils of outrageous fortune: every gimmicky mail-order product he bought from the ACME Corporation has failed grievously instead of helping him catch the infuriatingly swift Road Runner. He’s been battered by boulders, dudded by detonators, rattled by rocket-skates, tricked by tunnels and walked into thin air off every cliff in the American Southwest. Now he’s suing the profit-obsessed ‘everything store’. But can his small-time lawyer Kevin Avery (Will Forte, Extra Ordinary , MIFF 2019) and Kevin’s plucky niece Paige (Lana Condor, To All the Boys I’ve Loved Before ) win against Buddy Crane (John Cena), ACME’s slick corporate counsel – and Kevin’s old boss? Blending live action with classic 2D animation in the spirit of Who Framed Roger Rabbit? , this warm-hearted underdog comedy produced by James Gunn and Chris DeFaria echoed its own plot behind the scenes: the completed film was to be shelved in 2023 as a tax write-off, but fan outcry helped it sprint back to the big screen. Don’t miss the Australian premiere of this legendary Looney Tunes reunion, whose cartoon cast includes Bugs Bunny, Daffy Duck, Elmer Fudd, Yosemite Sam, Foghorn Leghorn, Sylvester the Cat and Tweety – it will be dynamite.
films.awards: Don’t miss the Australian premiere of this legendary Looney Tunes reunion, whose cartoon cast includes Bugs Bunny, Daffy Duck, Elmer Fudd, Yosemite Sam, Foghorn Leg...

films.title: Dead Man’s Wire
films.synopsis: A top-notch cast led by Bill Skarsgård and Dacre Montgomery play out a hair-raising real-life 1970s hostage situation in this blackly comedic thriller from Gus Van Sant.
films.description: All Tony Kiritsis (Bill Skarsgård) wants is debt forgiveness, an apology and $5 million. Bullish about his vision for a new shopping mall, the Indianapolis businessman has taken on a hefty loan only to fall behind on the repayments. Convinced that he’s been duped by his mortgage brokers – a father–son duo played by Al Pacino ( Once Upon a Time in Hollywood , MIFF 2019) and Stranger Things ’ Dacre Montgomery ( Went up the Hill , MIFF 2025) – Tony abducts the younger man and rigs a shotgun to his head. His cracked quest for righteous revenge soon becomes a media spectacle, eagerly watched over by national TV crews and narrated by a local soul DJ (Colman Domingo, Sing Sing , MIFF 2024). The first film by stalwart indie auteur Gus Van Sant since Don’t Worry, He Won’t Get Far on Foot (MIFF 2018) finds him in a paranoid 70s groove, harking back to landmark works of that era like Sidney Lumet’s Dog Day Afternoon and Network in his fictionalised retelling of the 1977 kidnapping of Richard Hall. Just like those two “mad as hell” films, Dead Man’s Wire is a riveting tale trip-wired to a piquant social critique.

films.title: Death of a Shaman
films.synopsis: As Ecuador erupts in protest, the custodian of a threatened way of life contemplates the future of his family line and his people.
films.description: In a village deep in the Amazon jungle, Indigenous shaman Rafael Santi practises the sacred customs of his ancestors. As this community elder approaches the end of his life, he hopes to pass his mantle on to his teenage grandson, a devoted student who is nonetheless torn between this heavy responsibility and the opportunities offered by Western education. What’s at stake here is more than just one community’s future, however: this crisis of succession occurs against the backdrop of rising economic pressures and a steady incursion of mining and oil corporations into the Amazon, granted a green light by Ecuador’s pro-market government. As the nation’s Indigenous peoples launch a countrywide protest that takes them to the streets of Quito and into violent conflict with armed forces, Santi uses the traditional medicine of ayahuasca to seek answers from the spirit world. Australian filmmaker Dan Jackson spent over a decade developing connections with the Indigenous communities of Amazanga to tell this intimate story of one man, his family and an age-old struggle – one that echoes around the world, including here in Australia – between postcolonial states and the peoples they displaced but haven’t defeated. Presenting raw recordings of protest alongside stunning photography of the jungle landscape, archival footage of generations past and vivid depictions of hallucinogenic experience, this MIFF Premiere Fund–supported documentary takes us on a journey deep into the fault lines of our modern world and out into the realm beyond.
films.awards: ...footage of generations past and vivid depictions of hallucinogenic experience, this MIFF Premiere Fund–supported documentary takes us on a journey deep into the fault lines of our modern world and out into the realm beyond....

films.title: Digby & Camille
films.synopsis: In this tender, intimate and humorous documentary, experience the adventures and frustrations of a pair of inseparable lovers as they dream of building a life together.
films.description: Archibald Prize–selected painter Digby Webster and his girlfriend, trainee chef Camille Collins, have been mutually smitten since they first met eight years ago in a Sydney pub. Living with Down syndrome and now in their thirties, they imagine a future of wedded bliss, children and a home of their own – but more difficult truths have to be embraced first, as they navigate the demands of jobs and devoted parents who tend to squeeze the brakes on their loftier ambitions. As the couple experience numerous small frustrations and triumphs from moment to moment, however, their passion for one another never wavers. Award-winning filmmaker Trevor Graham ( Chef Antonio’s Recipes for Revolution , MIFF 2021) worked closely with Webster to co-author this unique, heartwarming documentary consisting of fly-on-the-wall footage filmed over several years alongside animated flights of fancy inspired by Webster’s art. Supported by the MIFF Premiere Fund, Digby & Camille ditches the ‘inspirational’ sheen of so many films about disability for a sensitive and honest portrayal of everyday life – one that might just renew your faith in the power of love.
films.awards: Archibald Prize–selected painter Digby Webster and his girlfriend, trainee chef Camille Collins, have been mutually smitten since they first met eight years ago in a Sydney pub.

films.title: Facing the Numbers
films.synopsis: A unique mixture of different forms of Indigenous storytelling, this vibrant documentary finds the humanity behind all-too-familiar statistics
films.description: A unique mixture of different forms of Indigenous storytelling, this vibrant documentary finds the humanity behind all-too-familiar statistics. The numbers don’t lie: First Nations people are disproportionately more likely to be separated from their families, incarcerated as both youths and adults, and become victims of police brutality. But, as Facing the Numbers states on opening: “The statistics tell one story; we tell another.” Sharing the voices of members of the Stolen Generations, those who have lost family members to deaths in custody and survivors of violence in custody, this unique hybrid anthology – a mixture of memoir, music video, poetry, dance, journalism and activism – lends space to the personal stories behind experience of disadvantage and institutionalised violence.

films.title: Family Gala: Forgotten Island
films.synopsis: Tayo na ! Come along to MIFF’s Family Gala – a fun-filled centrepiece event for kids and adults alike – and enjoy the Australian premiere of new DreamWorks adventure Forgotten Island .
films.description: H.E.R., Liza Soberano, Manny Jacinto and Lea Salonga lend their voices to this fantastical animated tale of friendship, fearlessness and Filipino culture. Best friends Jo and Raissa have just graduated from high school. They vow to remain close forever, even though Raissa’s about to move to the US for university. On their last night together, the girls accidentally open a portal to Nakali, a mysterious island populated by aswangs . The longer you stay in Nakali, the more your memories fade – so they must figure out how to get back home, fast, and also avoid the clutches of the Dreaded Manananggal.
films.awards: Come along to MIFF’s Family Gala – a fun-filled centrepiece event for kids and adults alike – and enjoy the Australian premiere of new DreamWorks adventure Forgotten Island .

films.title: Guided by Horses
films.synopsis: Amid the breathtaking vistas of the Kimberley, a groundbreaking equine sanctuary helps First Nations teenagers discover reconnection and healing.
films.description: The natural beauty of Western Australia’s Kimberley region hides a shocking truth: the youth suicide rate in this remote part of the country is among the highest in the world. Pioneering Aboriginal researcher Professor Juli Coffin is seeking to change that with a unique program that pairs young people with horses, offering a culturally safe sanctuary in which they navigate their paths to adulthood, forming life-changing bonds along the way. Director Sean O’Reilly works closely with Coffin – whose own life was once saved by a horse – to document their remarkable journeys. Executive-produced by Hunter Page-Lochard, Huna Amweero, Daniel Gordon, Mark Coles Smith and Stephen Page, O’Reilly’s documentary combines observational footage with first-person testimony, set against the stunningly cinematic backdrop of some of Australia’s most majestic landscapes. Full of charm, joy and gentle humour, Guided by Horses is an engaging reminder of where we can turn to for our inner spirit to be ignited.

films.title: Hard as Puck
films.synopsis: A motley crew of para ice hockey skaters fight to keep their grassroots sporting club on the rink in this stirring, often hilarious documentary.
films.description: The Perth-based Garden Island Pirates are the best para ice hockey team in Western Australia. There’s no competition … literally. A scrappy, mixed-gender group of amateurs, they strap on sledges to play against one another as stoically deadpan founder and would-be figure-skater Dan Perrett calls the shots from the sidelines. Booking the rink is half the trouble, and much of the group’s time away from it is spent negotiating volunteer committee positions and wrangling sponsorships. That all changes, however, when three women on the team earn the opportunity to represent Australia in its first ever appearance at the Women’s World Para Ice Hockey Championships in Slovakia. Scheduled to compete against heavyweights like Norway and the United States in a bruising, physically demanding sport, the newcomers aren’t given a snowflake’s chance in hell – but that doesn’t mean that they’re not about to go down swinging. An affectionate portrait of a niche sporting institution and the weird and wonderful personalities who help it scrape by, Isaac Elliott’s debut documentary feature – supported by the MIFF Premiere Fund – is a laugh-out-loud edge-of-your-sled ride that brings together the underdog drive of Cool Runnings , the behind-the-scenes intrigue of Rats in the Ranks (MIFF 1996) and the irrepressible community spirit of The Golden Spurtle (MIFF 2025).

films.title: Holofiction
films.synopsis: Drawing on thousands of films and TV shows about the Holocaust, this gripping, rigorously crafted supercut queries the ethics of representing atrocity
films.description: Drawing on thousands of films and TV shows about the Holocaust, this gripping, rigorously crafted supercut queries the ethics of representing atrocity. “Fiction is a transgression,” stated Claude Lanzmann, maker of the definitive Holocaust documentary, Shoah (MIFF 1987), in reference to Schindler’s List . “It is my belief that the depiction of certain things is prohibited.” And yet, World War II – and, at its core, the mass murder that was perpetrated by the Nazis – remains the most popular historical subject in cinema. Michal Kosakowski’s found-footage essay film takes Lanzmann’s famous statement as its provocative point of departure, assembling clips culled from a corpus of more than 3000 narrative works – from Rome, Open City to Inglourious Basterds – to reveal both the constants and the contradictions of this genre.
films.recommendation: 暂无 Letterboxd 评分；纪录片、Historical / 电影之梦 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：政治与社会现实、悬疑 / 犯罪张力、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Jebediah: Are We OK?
films.synopsis: Take a 30-year journey with the beloved Australian alternative rock band, from humble beginnings to the harder aspects of growing up.
films.description: Perth alt rockers Jebediah were a breed apart in the halcyon days of the late-90s and early-2000s Australian indie music scene. In stark contrast to the often volatile and soon-to-splinter groups they toured with, the easygoing quartet – lead singer Kevin Mitchell (aka Bob Evans), his drummer brother Brett, bassist Ness Thornton and lead guitarist Chris Daymond – stuck fast to one another. Commanding national attention on Triple J and soon channelling the cred of multiple studio albums into their own record label, Jebediah seemed like quintessential industry survivors. But as the band entered its third decade, the freewheeling lifestyle of their youth had begun to cast a shadow. With recreation shifting to addiction and connections fraying, will these lifelong friends and creative partners find a way to keep the music alive? Built from a wealth of archival and home-movie footage ranging from the band’s humble high school beginnings through to the current day, as well as firsthand perspectives from local music icons such as Tim Rogers, Phil Jamieson and Janet English, Arlo Dean Cook’s MIFF Premiere Fund–supported documentary is a rocking roller-coaster ride and a candid cautionary tale about the price of fame.
films.awards: ...al music icons such as Tim Rogers, Phil Jamieson and Janet English, Arlo Dean Cook’s MIFF Premiere Fund–supported documentary is a rocking roller-coaster ride and a candid cautionary tale about the price of fame....

films.title: Kim Novak’s Vertigo
films.synopsis: In studio-era Hollywood, Kim Novak fought to craft her own identity. In this affectionate documentary portrait, she elegantly redefines her legacy.
films.description: People have rarely given Kim Novak the credit she deserves. She has been dismissed as a generic 1950s studio blonde; her slippery performance in 1958 psychological thriller Vertigo (MIFF 1984) is too often attributed only to director Alfred Hitchcock’s genius; and her long, fiercely private retirement is treated as the wreckage of Hollywood misogyny. All the while, however, Novak has been her own auteur. She fought Columbia Pictures boss Harry Cohn to keep her own surname, and went on strike for better pay. Now 93, she recalls Vertigo as a satisfyingly metatextual collaboration, reframing her life’s dizzying, spiralling narratives as “a big, beautiful circle”. In the prolific Alexandre O. Philippe, Novak finds an avid, erudite listener. His documentaries on cinema craft are frequently valentines to individual films: The Texas Chainsaw Massacre ( Chain Reactions , MIFF 2025), Alien ( Memory – The Origins of Alien , MIFF 2019) and Psycho ( 78/52 , MIFF 2017). Here, as in his William Shatner portrait You Can Call Me Bill (MIFF 2023), Philippe illuminates a complex person rather than worshipping a star. Kim Novak’s Vertigo celebrates an unforgettable movie through its subject’s refreshingly candid musings on the tensions between authenticity and mystique.

films.title: Lomu
films.synopsis: A nuanced portrait of Kiwi Tongan rugby union superstar Jonah Lomu, history’s youngest All Black, whose legend remains a beacon for Pasifika pride.
films.description: In 1994, at the age of 19, Jonah Lomu donned his first All Blacks jersey – the youngest ever to wear it. At the next year’s World Cup, the Tongan New Zealander winger’s jaw-dropping combination of power and agility redefined rugby union. Nearly 2 metres tall and 120 kilograms, Lomu could run 100 metres in 11 seconds and evade every tackle – former Wallaby Peter FitzSimons described him as “a freight train in ballet shoes”. Having grown up bilingual between Tonga and South Auckland, he used his cultural connection and Christian faith to transform the glaring spotlight of sporting glory into a torch to ignite cultural pride across the Pacific. But Lomu wasn’t superhuman. Privately, he battled nephrotic syndrome, a rare kidney disorder that would extinguish his stupendous, record-setting career. A humble, staunch Lomu emerges here in intimate interviews with his closest family, friends and coaches, along with archival footage and previously unseen family videos. Sensitively stewarding Lomu’s world-spanning legacy are Dublin native Gavin Fitzgerald, known for documentary profiles of Liam Gallagher and Conor McGregor, and Kiwi Tongan trailblazer Vea Mafile’o ( Lea Tupu’anga , MIFF 2024; For My Father’s Kingdom , MIFF 2019).
films.recommendation: 暂无 Letterboxd 评分；纪录片 / 纪录片单元 / 维州首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实、记忆与历史、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Mad Rush
films.synopsis: A young woman gets caught in an elaborate phone scam which sends her on a twisted odyssey through the Melbourne CBD.
films.description: Madisha’s morning couldn’t get any worse: a rootless Gen Z already struggling to find her purpose in life, she’s just learned that her housemate is ditching her and is still reeling from having lost her most recent job under cloudy circumstances. So when she gets a call advising her of fraudulent activity on her bank account and urgently requesting that she withdraw all of her money in cash, Madisha is sent on a panicked odyssey through the Melbourne CBD – a pursuit that leads her to ominous alleyways, busy street corners, an alcohol-soaked house party and, perhaps, the fledgling desire to take charge of her own life. Supported by the MIFF Premiere Fund, this visually striking debut feature film by Accelerator Lab alum Maddelin McKenna ( Patterns of the Afternoon , MIFF 2022) is a nuanced, deeply relatable portrait of a spiky but vulnerable young woman caught in a web tightening around her. Anchored around a stunning lead performance from Senuri Chandrani ( Of an Age , MIFF 2022) as the film’s Alice-like protagonist, Mad Rush turns our city into a gritty, disorienting urban Wonderland.
films.awards: Supported by the MIFF Premiere Fund, this visually striking debut feature film by Accelerator Lab alum Maddelin McKenna ( Patterns of the Afternoon , MIFF 2022)...

films.title: Mum, I’m Alien Pregnant
films.synopsis: A twentysomething homebody is stuck carrying more than she bargained for in this gleefully gloopy Kiwi body-horror comedy.
films.description: After an unusual erotic encounter with a neighbour in the laundry room of their apartment building, Mary finds herself unexpectedly knocked up. But worse, Mary’s unborn offspring is a rapidly gestating alien foetus – the baby daddy is part alien (the part that counts). An underachieving millennial who still lives with her oversharing, open-minded-to-the-extreme single mother, Mary must now navigate dismissive doctors, a useless parenting partner and a whole host of discombobulating physical side effects if she is to reclaim autonomy over her body and her life. Expanding on their 2024 short, director duo Sean Wallace and Jordan Mark Windsor – aka THUNDERLIPS – bring an unmistakeably Kiwi self-deprecating humour to this outrageous comedy positively dripping in extraterrestrial bodily fluids. Hannah Lynch ( Petrol , MIFF 2022) shines as the unapologetically pissed-off Mary, and the visceral practical effects and tentacled prosthetics are a sight to behold.

films.title: My Life Without Steve
films.synopsis: Almost as controversial as it was lauded, this gorgeous, rigorously composed reflection on a break-up is a landmark work of Australian feminist cinema.
films.description: “After you left, I moved in here for a year, hoping that the view would pull me out of the misery.” Over a series of domestic tableaux – a copper kettle on the boil; a bulging vase stuffed with white daisies – a woman delivers a discursive monologue: the musings of a wounded heart, rich with intertextual references from Roland Barthes to Laurie Anderson and Bob Dylan. The speaker is Liz, a semi-fictive creation voiced by Jenny Vuletic but seen only fleetingly, as a night-time reflection in the window of her apartment overlooking Sydney’s bustling Blackwattle Bay. Inspired by the works of Chris Marker and Chantal Akerman and made during a notable period for women filmmakers in Australia, Gillian Leahy’s singular, sophisticated essay film – shot on lush 35mm – was honoured at MIFF, the Sydney Film Festival and the AFI Awards on its release. However, My Life Without Steve also ruffled feathers, with Liz’s exploratory and sometimes accusatory monologue critiqued by some as failing certain feminist ideals. Forty years later, in a culture attuned to prickly autofiction, the conversation seems to have finally caught up to Leahy.
films.awards: ...say film – shot on lush 35mm – was honoured at MIFF, the Sydney Film Festival and the AFI Awards on its release....
films.recommendation: 暂无 Letterboxd 评分；纪录片 / 经典修复 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实。奖项/展映线索：获奖

films.title: Mārama
films.synopsis: This distinctly Māori Gothic horror, set in a haunted seaside manor, tells a terrifying tale of dark family secrets and one woman’s wrath in the face of colonial violence.
films.description: In 1859, Māori teacher Mary Stevens receives a mysterious letter summoning her from Aotearoa New Zealand to North Yorkshire, England, with the promise of revelations about her family history. Upon her arrival, lord of the local manor Nathaniel Cole offers Mary board and work as a governess for his niece. Nathaniel proudly shows off his collection of Māori artefacts and fluency in te reo Māori; he claims to have a deep affection for Mary’s culture and people. But the longer Mary stays in the windswept manor, the more she senses someone – or something – in the house is trying to communicate a grave warning. Taratoa Stappard’s striking debut feature is a chilling ghost story reckoning with the violence of British colonialism, taking the tropes and visual language of classic Victorian Gothic horror and redeploying them through a uniquely Māori lens. Ariāna Osborne runs the gamut from grief to righteous fury in her ferocious performance as Mary, while the lavish costume and production design combine with the tale to elevate Mārama to the pantheon of great ghost stories.
films.recommendation: 暂无 Letterboxd 评分；剧情、惊悚、恐怖 / 午夜 / 类型片 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：家庭关系、政治与社会现实、记忆与历史、失去与哀悼、恐怖 / 超自然。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Nostalgia for the Future
films.synopsis: Chris Marker was a filmmaker, artist and writer who was as visionary as he was famously elusive. Now, he forms the mysterious locus of this documentary drawing on his expansive archives.
films.description: Chris Marker liked to describe himself as “the best-known author of unknown films”, but perhaps the opposite is true. Today, there is widespread reverence for works such as La Jetée , his fleet but haunting sci-fi almost entirely comprising still images (and that later formed the basis for Terry Gilliam’s 12 Monkeys ), and Sans Soleil (MIFF 1983), the existential travelogue that redefined the essay film. But Marker – one of numerous pseudonyms he used in his 91 years – very rarely gave interviews; averse to being photographed, he often used a drawing of his cat as an avatar. The director’s death in 2012 left behind 550 boxes of archival material. Together with excerpts from his groundbreaking body of work, these materials form the basis for Brecht Debackere’s probing but necessarily oblique portrait of the artist – as narrated by Charlotte Rampling ( Swimming Pool , MIFF 2003), the magisterial actor here personifying an archivist. In tune with Marker’s preoccupations, Nostalgia for the Future is a meditation on the slippery persistence of memory, personal as well as cultural: a precious souvenir for existing fans, and a beguiling introduction for anyone who wishes to peer behind his Cheshire grin.
films.recommendation: 暂无 Letterboxd 评分；纪录片 / 电影之梦 / 澳洲首映。推荐理由：记忆与历史、失去与哀悼、纪录片视角。奖项/展映线索：暂无明确获奖信息，主要按评分、题材和导演风格推荐

films.title: Of Womb
films.synopsis: Evading the hard truth of their religious matriarch’s death, three sisters gestate dark ecstasies of faith and folk horror. Blessed be Mother.
films.description: Three young women have been raised in innocence on a remote, dilapidated homestead, alone with their bedbound, intensely religious mother. Fuelled by a heady brew of existential terror, dogmatic belief and desire to dominate, The Eldest decides to hide Mother’s death from her siblings, telling them the Devil is lurking behind her closed bedroom door as sound and stench, ready to repay their curiosity with a terrible sickness. Among the paperbark trees, invented sacraments begin playfully but soon plunge the trio into a claustrophobic shared delirium, gasping towards a devastating collapse. Inspired by their own religious upbringing and fascination with European arthouse witch tropes, debutant West Australian director Peter Firebrace shot Of Womb with a tiny, close-knit cast and crew in a heritage-listed South Fremantle house with its own morbid history. Belying its micro-budget, this visually stunning psychological fever dream thrums with the ritual gestures of Dogtooth (MIFF 2009) and The Witch (MIFF 2015) and evokes the landscape-horror tradition of Picnic at Hanging Rock (MIFF 2018). The film’s own gripping simplicity, however, announces an exciting new voice in Australian cinema – one poised to disturb, provoke and captivate.

films.title: Opening Night Gala: Wicker
films.synopsis: MIFF 2026 commences with an event that will get everyone talking: the Australian premiere of Eleanor Wilson and Alex Huston Fischer’s cheeky, star-studded and irresistible fable Wicker .
films.description: Mingle with the brightest stars of the film industry on the red carpet, enjoy a drink on arrival and settle in for one of the year’s most provocative and hilarious films. Audiences can take in the atmosphere of the red carpet and the screening at Hoyts Melbourne Central with a film-only ticket, or book a spot at the legendary afterparty at Melbourne Town Hall with a film-and-party ticket.

films.title: Our Effed Up World
films.synopsis: To save Earth from an insatiable alien, this slacker will need her dad and her effing loyal mates, who make a crap planet worth fighting for.
films.description: Sheri (Sara Thompson, One of Us Is Lying ) hasn’t been okay since her beloved grandma (Kerry Armstrong, Lantana , MIFF 2001) died. She’s over her annoying boyfriend Harry (Jordan Dulieu, The Serpent’s Skin ), and resents her astronomer dad Hank (Scott Major, Neighbours ) for avoidantly watching the skies. When a glowing green meteorite crash-lands near their small town and its passenger starts melding gruesomely with locals, humanity’s last hope is Sheri, her dad and her Scooby Gang of mates: Poppy (Annapurna Sriram, Fucktoys ), Ash (Karis Oka, The Deb ) and Finn (Jess McLeod, One of Us Is Lying ), whose run-down video store has become the alien’s lair. Prolific Aussie queer-horror phenom Alice Maio Mackay ( T Blockers ), directing her seventh feature at the age of 21, merges retro schlock and teen-culture cool with a sincere affection for chosen family. It’s the opposite of effed-up that Major – the 90s Heartbreak High OG himself – stars here, and that she’s casting North Americans as rural Aussie retail shitkickers. Meanwhile, Mackay is building her own enviable friend gang: Our Effed Up World is produced by Jane Schoenbrun ( I Saw the TV Glow , MIFF 2024), and it’s a joy to spot cameos from TV Glow star Jack Haven and Lesbian Space Princess (MIFF 2025) co-writer/director Leela Varghese.

films.title: Phillip Philadelphia
films.synopsis: Crude, lust-riddled and brilliantly cracked, this Gold Coast–set fever dream is the latest twisted caper from maverick auteur Adam C. Briggs.
films.description: Introduced languishing in a faded motel, the eponymous Phillip is a Brit and by trade a screenwriter, though he’s not doing much writing these days. He’s more interested in frittering his time away, pursuing flights of fantasy and intoxicating indulgences even as the line between the two grows ever fuzzier. In this, he’s aided and abetted by an aspiring actor with whom he’s enjoying a dalliance – though Phillip suspects the younger man might be mainly interested in his showbiz connections – and the drug dealer next door. The prolific and indefatigable Briggs has often worked closely with non-professional actors to craft narratives whose blends of fact and fiction are as riotous as they are discomforting; he previously garnered acclaim for his 2024 feature A Grand Mockery (co-directed with and starring Sam Dixon, who also appears in this film in a supporting role), which took out top honours at SXSW Sydney as well as the Best Director prize at Sitges. With Phillip Philadelphia , the Meanjin-born writer/director captures the garish, grubby beauty of the Gold Coast on 16mm, evoking Florida as seen through the lenses of Harmony Korine and Sean Baker yet also extending his own distinctively homegrown, eloquently vulgar style.
films.awards: ...Dixon, who also appears in this film in a supporting role), which took out top honours at SXSW Sydney as well as the Best Director prize at Sitges....

films.title: Premiere With Purpose: Silenced
films.synopsis: Jennifer Robinson, Amber Heard and Brittany Higgins have all experienced the full force of weaponised defamation laws – but these indomitable activists and survivors of gendered violence won’t keep quiet
films.description: Jennifer Robinson, Amber Heard and Brittany Higgins have all experienced the full force of weaponised defamation laws – but these indomitable activists and survivors of gendered violence won’t keep quiet. In 2017, #MeToo replaced the social stigma of discussing gendered violence with public solidarity and support for victim-survivors. The re-silencing was swift, vicious … and legalistic. Alleged perpetrators now used defamation lawsuits as cudgels to punish survivors for speaking out, and to deter journalists from exposing prominent people to public scrutiny. As a barrister who famously stood up for Amber Heard in a long-running, all-too-public defamation case in the UK, Jennifer Robinson has dedicated her career to fighting against these attacks, witnessing firsthand how such actions drag survivors through emotionally, professionally and financially ruinous legal proceedings.
